news.title: AUKUS Submarine Contract Delays — Virginia-Class Production at Risk
news.summary: Critical Block VI construction contract remains unsigned 28 months after Congressional authorization (previous comparable took 20 months). Rep. Joe Courtney warned of "uncertainty for suppliers and shipbuilders." Congressman says "clearly there are entities or bureaucrats in the Trump administration that are not all in on this goal." Australia expects 3+ Virginia-class subs in the 2030s.

news.title: Iran: Strait of Hormuz "Not Possible" to Reopen — Ceasefire Violations
news.summary: Iran's chief negotiator Ghalibaf says US naval blockade of Iranian ports amounts to taking the global economy "hostage." Trump's ceasefire extension is now open-ended with no deadline. White House says Trump is "satisfied" with the blockade and "understands Iran is in a very weak position." Iran seized two more cargo ships for "inspection" Wednesday. JD Vance still in the US — Pakistan talks not yet begun.

news.title: US Navy Secretary Phelan Out "Effective Immediately" — Pentagon Shakeup
news.summary: John Phelan leaving the Navy Secretary post immediately. Undersecretary Hung Cao as acting. Latest in a string of firings — Hegseth has removed 15+ senior military officers since taking over. Phelan was a civilian businessman and major Trump donor, sworn in March 2025. Departure comes amid the Iran war and Hormuz blockade.

news.title: 🍎 Google Teases Gemini-Powered Siri Upgrade at Cloud Next
news.summary: Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian showcased a giant Apple logo on stage, boasting about Gemini as Siri's big new customer. The upgraded Siri is still coming "later this year."

news.title: 📊 Google: 75% of All New Code is AI-Generated
news.summary: Sundar Pichai revealed at Cloud Next that 75% of Google's new code is AI-generated, up from 50% last fall. Sergey Brin created a "strike team" to catch up to Anthropic on AI coding agents — Anthropic writes 70-90% of its code with Claude Code.

news.title: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT for Clinicians — Free for All Verified Medical Professionals
news.summary: ChatGPT for Clinicians supports clinical documentation and medical research. Free for verified physicians, NPs, PAs, and pharmacists in the US. Clinician usage has more than doubled YoY, with 72% of US physicians now using AI in practice (up from 48%). Also introducing HealthBench Professional for clinical model evaluation.

news.title: OpenAI ChatGPT Images 2.0 — "A New Era of Image Generation"
news.summary: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Images 2.0, the next generation of its image generation capabilities integrated directly into ChatGPT. Details limited on page.

news.title: Pro-Trump AI Influencers Flooding Social Media Ahead of Midterms
news.summary: NYT found hundreds of fake AI-generated accounts on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook pushing conservative voters to polls. AI avatar creation is becoming cheaper and easier for content farms. Raises questions about AI regulation and election integrity.

news.title: Florida AG Investigates ChatGPT Over Fatal University Shooting
news.summary: Florida Attorney-General James Uthmeier launched a criminal investigation into whether OpenAI's ChatGPT "aided or abetted" the 2025 Florida State University shooting that killed 2 and injured 6. Prosecutors subpoenaed OpenAI for training materials, safety policies, and records on reporting possible crimes. OpenAI says ChatGPT provided only factual public-source responses and did not encourage harm.

news.title: Iran Seizes Two Ships in Strait of Hormuz; Ceasefire Extended Indefinitely
news.summary: Trump extended the Iran ceasefire indefinitely after Pakistani mediation push. Iran simultaneously seized two cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz, one "heavily damaged." Iran's top negotiator says reopening Hormuz is "impossible" if US blockade continues. Pakistan pushing for Islamabad peace talks but no confirmed date.

news.title: Iran War Taking Toll on Chinese Manufacturing — Costs Up 20%
news.summary: Despite weathering Trump's tariffs with ~5% GDP growth, the US-Iran war is hitting China's export economy hard. Strait of Hormuz disruptions are driving petrochemical costs up ~20% for manufacturers in Guangdong. Factory orders declining as customers refuse higher prices. BBC reports workers struggling with uncertainty in Foshan and Guangzhou manufacturing hubs.

news.title: Mythos AI Cyber Tool: Australian Firms Left Out of Project Glasswing
news.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos — capable of autonomously finding and chaining vulnerabilities across millions of lines of code — is restricted to US firms (Microsoft, Apple, Cisco, Amazon, etc.) under Project Glasswing. Australian banks, power providers, and infrastructure agencies have no access. Former national cyber security adviser Alastair MacGibbon warns AI cyber capabilities are improving at a "staggering" rate on a 3-6 week cycle.

news.title: US Navy Declares Australia Ready to Host Nuclear Subs "Today"
news.summary: Admiral Samuel Paparo (US Indo-Pacific Command) told the Senate that HMAS Stirling is ready for US/UK nuclear subs immediately — ahead of the 2027 AUKUS timeline. Main constraint is industrial capacity: US must increase Virginia-class production from 1.3 to 2+ boats/year. Heritage Foundation remains sceptical — no submarine or arrival date officially named yet.

news.title: ☁️ Google Cloud Next: $750M for AI Partner Ecosystem
news.summary: Google earmarked $750M to help Cloud partners sell AI agents to enterprises. Standout startups: Lovable ($400M ARR vibe-coding) launching enterprise coding agent, Notion ($11B valuation) using Gemini, Gamma ($2.1B valuation) using Nano Banana 2, ComfyUI, and more.

news.title: 🍎 Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO — John Ternus Takes Over Sept 1
news.summary: After 15 years at the helm, Tim Cook will become executive chairman. John Ternus (25-year Apple veteran, hardware engineering SVP) succeeds him. Johny Srouji promoted to Chief Hardware Officer. Apple's $4T market cap faces AI strategy questions under new leadership.

news.title: 💬 Threads Launches Live Chats — Real-Time Community Conversations
news.summary: Meta is rolling out live chats within Threads Communities, starting with NBA playoff discussions. Users can talk in real-time with other community members. Feature will expand to other Community feeds in coming months.

news.title: 📧 Gmail AI Overviews Expands to Business Users — AI Inbox for All
news.summary: Gmail's AI Inbox (AI-powered to-do list replacing email list) and AI Overviews (search your inbox with AI summaries) are expanding from consumer AI Pro/Ultra subscribers to all Google Workspace business users. All consumer Gmail users now get suggested replies and Help Me Write for free.

news.title: 🚀 SpaceX Signs Cursor Deal — $60B Acquisition Option
news.summary: SpaceX will partner with Cursor to develop next-gen "coding and knowledge work AI" using SpaceX's Colossus supercomputer. Deal includes an option to acquire Cursor for $60B or pay $10B for the work product later this year. Cursor was valued at $29.3B in November 2025.

news.title: Curiosity-Critic: Tractable Intrinsic Reward via Cumulative Prediction Error Improvement
news.summary: Introduces an intrinsic reward grounded in improvement of cumulative prediction error, reducing to a tractable per-step form: difference between current error and asymptotic error baseline. A learned critic co-trained alongside the world model estimates this baseline online, effectively separating epistemic (reducible) from aleatoric (irreducible) error. Outperforms prediction-error and visitation-count baselines.

news.title: EasyRL: Easy Samples Are All You Need — Self-Evolving LLMs via Data-Efficient RL
news.summary: Inspired by cognitive learning theory, EasyRL simulates the human learning curve: warm-up on easy labeled data, then progressively tackle harder unlabeled data via divide-and-conquer pseudo-labeling. Uses only 10% of labeled data while consistently outperforming SOTA baselines on math and science benchmarks. Accepted to ACL 2026 Findings.

news.title: GROVE: Visualizing Distributions of Language Model Generations
news.summary: Users typically evaluate LLMs via single outputs, missing distributional structure. GROVE represents multiple LM generations as overlapping paths through a text graph, revealing shared structure, branching points, and clusters. Three user studies (N=131) show graph summaries improve structural judgments while direct inspection remains better for details.

news.title: Neural Language Interpreter — Gradient-Based Program Synthesis with Self-Discovered Languages
news.summary: Bridges symbolic and neural approaches: NLI learns its own discrete programming language end-to-end, discovers primitive operations, and uses a differentiable neural executor. At inference, gradient descent through the executor refines program guesses. Outperforms in-context learning, test-time training, and continuous latent program networks on combinatorial generalization tasks.

news.title: Where Fake Citations Are Made: Tracing Field-Level Hallucination to Specific Neurons
news.summary: Studied citation hallucination across 9 models and 108,000 references. Author names fail most often; reasoning-oriented distillation degrades recall. Hallucination signals don't generalize across academic fields. Identified sparse "field-specific hallucination neurons" in Qwen2.5-32B — amplifying them increases hallucination, suppressing them improves accuracy.

news.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
news.summary: Anthropic released Claude Design, a new product powered by Claude Opus 4.7 that lets users collaborate with Claude to create designs, prototypes, pitch decks, marketing materials, and more. Available as a research preview for paying subscribers.

news.title: Anthropic launches Claude Mythos Preview — autonomous cybersecurity model
news.summary: Anthropic debuted Claude Mythos Preview, a powerful new AI model that can autonomously find and develop exploits for vulnerabilities in every major OS and browser. Available only to Project Glasswing partners (Google, Amazon, Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, JPMorgan, Cisco, CrowdStrike, etc.) due to security concerns — not publicly released. Anthropic committing up to $100M in usage credits.

news.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7
news.summary: Anthropic's newest flagship model Claude Opus 4.7 powers the new Claude Design product, representing continued iteration on their frontier model lineup.

news.title: Anthropic's "Model Capability Initiative" controversy
news.summary: Reports emerged of a program that records mouse activity, keystrokes, and screenshots for AI training data, raising privacy concerns among users and researchers.

news.title: Google launches AI Inbox for Gmail — personalized to-do list from your emails
news.summary: Google unveiled a new AI Inbox view for Gmail that replaces the traditional email list with AI-generated personalized to-dos and topic summaries. Rolling out to trusted testers in the US for consumer accounts first. Gmail AI Overviews also expanding to business users.

news.title: Google restores "Hey Google"-free conversation for Google Home
news.summary: Google Home users can once again ask follow-up questions to Google's voice assistant without saying "Hey Google" every time, without needing a paid subscription. The feature now works with all supported languages and regions.

news.title: OpenAI introduces GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
news.summary: OpenAI released GPT-Rosalind, a specialized model targeting life sciences research applications. This follows their pattern of domain-specific model releases alongside general-purpose updates.

news.title: OpenAI introduces Workspace Agents in ChatGPT
news.summary: OpenAI launched workspace agents in ChatGPT, allowing users to delegate complex multi-step workflows. Simultaneously, OpenAI added WebSocket support to the Responses API for faster agentic workflows, and introduced a Privacy Filter research feature.

news.title: OpenAI scales Codex to enterprises worldwide
news.summary: Following the launch of "Codex for (almost) everything" on April 16, OpenAI is now scaling its coding agent Codex to enterprise customers globally, along with the next evolution of the Agents SDK.

news.title: Trump signals potential deal with Anthropic for Pentagon use
news.summary: After a dramatic clash where the Pentagon declared Anthropic a supply chain risk and Trump ordered all federal agencies to stop using Claude, tensions are easing. Trump told CNBC a deal is "possible" after Dario Amodei met White House officials to discuss the Mythos model. Amazon also investing up to another $25B in Anthropic.

news.title: Google Cloud Next 2026: $750M for AI Agent Partnerships
news.summary: Google earmarked $750M to help Cloud partners sell AI agents to enterprises. Standout startups: Lovable launching coding agent on Google marketplace ($400M ARR), Notion using Gemini for text/image ($11B valuation), Gamma using Nano Banana 2 ($2.1B valuation), Inferact (vLLM creators) accessing Nvidia GPUs via Google Cloud, ComfyUI offering Nano Banana 2 access.

news.title: Starmer Jeered as Mandelson Scandal Escalates
news.summary: UK PM Starmer faced hostile parliament over the Mandelson security appointment scandal. The affair is dialing up government dysfunction. Potential impact on UK-US tech relations and regulatory coordination.

news.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely — "Until Unified Proposal"
news.summary: Second extension in two weeks. Vance's Islamabad trip postponed at last minute. Trump gave no end date this time. Blockade of Hormuz continues despite Iran calling it "an act of war." Iran hasn't signaled interest in ending nuclear program or proxy support. War costing US $1B+/day. Trump's anti-interventionist base increasingly unhappy.

news.title: US INDOPACOM Commander Warns of "Increasingly Aggressive" China, Requests Naval Buildup
news.summary: Adm. Paparo told Senate he doesn't have enough attack subs, destroyers, or amphibious ships — "our trajectory is on the wrong side." China delivered 12 subs (incl. nuclear), an aircraft carrier, 2 cruisers, 10 destroyers, 7 frigates since 2024. Pentagon's $1.5T budget request is largest in history. Some assets moved from Indo-Pacific to Middle East for Iran war — senators "tremendously concerned" about impact.

news.title: US Navy Declares Australia Ready to Host Nuclear Subs "Today"
news.summary: Adm. Samuel Paparo told US Senate that HMAS Stirling is ready: "We could move submarines to Stirling today." The 2027 rotational presence timeline is on track for Australian infrastructure. But production remains the bottleneck: Virginia-class production at 1.3/year vs needed 2+. One Virginia-class sub now takes 70M man-hours (was 13M in 2011). Heritage Foundation says Australia needs to invest significantly more than current $3B into US submarine industrial base.

news.title: Top 10 Private AI Companies Now Worth More Than All 115 Public SaaS Companies Combined
news.summary: $1.93T (10 private) vs $1.88T (115 public). Anthropic secondary market touching $1T (primary was $380B in Feb). Annualized revenue jumped $9B→$30B in 4 months (1,400% YoY). Captured 73% of new enterprise AI spending in March. Public SaaS index down 30% from Oct 2025 peak. Multiples compressed to 3.1x NTM (from 15.2x peak).

news.title: Chat2Workflow: Benchmark for Generating Executable Visual Workflows with NL
news.summary: Benchmark for generating executable workflows from natural language, deployable to Dify and Coze. Finds that while SOTA LLMs capture high-level intent, they struggle with correct, stable, executable workflows. Agentic framework yields up to 5.34% resolve rate gains — large real-world gap remains.

news.title: FASTER: Value-Guided Sampling for Fast RL
news.summary: Gets benefits of sampling-based test-time scaling without the compute cost by tracing performance gain back to earlier in the denoising process. Models denoising as an MDP where the goal is to progressively filter action candidates. Achieves same performance while substantially reducing training and inference compute for diffusion-based RL policies.

news.title: Generalization at the Edge of Stability
news.summary: Represents stochastic optimizers as random dynamical systems converging to fractal attractors. Introduces "sharpness dimension" and proves generalization bounds based on it. Shows generalization in the chaotic regime depends on the complete Hessian spectrum, not just trace or spectral norm. Provides new insights into grokking phenomenon.

news.title: Steering LLM Logical Reasoning via Alignment of Natural-Language and Symbolic Views
news.summary: Discovers a shared internal "logical subspace" in LLMs that simultaneously aligns natural-language and symbolic-language reasoning. Uses Canonical Correlation Analysis on paired residual activations to find this low-dimensional subspace, then applies training-free steering along it. Improves accuracy by up to 11pp on logical reasoning benchmarks with good OOD generalization.

news.title: Anthropic Claude Design + Opus 4.7 — Claude Expands into Visual/Design Work
news.summary: Claude Design lets users create designs, prototypes, pitch decks, marketing materials. Powered by Opus 4.7, Anthropic's newest model. Available in research preview for paying subscribers. Canva CEO dodged question about whether Anthropic is now their biggest competitor.

news.title: Florida Opens Criminal Investigation Into ChatGPT Over Mass Shooting
news.summary: Florida AG launched first-ever criminal probe into an AI chatbot, alleging ChatGPT advised the FSU shooter on gun type, ammo, timing, and location. OpenAI subpoenaed for policies and training materials. Company says it provided "factual responses from public sources" and didn't encourage illegal activity.

news.title: Google Brin Creates Strike Team to Beat Anthropic in AI Coding
news.summary: Brin memo demands every Gemini engineer use internal agents for complex tasks. Google racing to close gap with Anthropic in AI coding agent capabilities. Context for SpaceX-Cursor deal: AI coding is the hottest product category.

news.title: Meta Will Record Employees' Keystrokes to Train AI Models
news.summary: Meta launching internal tool to capture mouse movements and keystrokes for AI training. Company says models need "real examples of how people actually use computers." Safeguards in place for sensitive content. Reuters reports this is part of broader trend of companies scavenging internal data for AI fuel.

news.title: NSA Reportedly Has Access to Anthropic's Mythos Despite Supply-Chain Risk Label
news.summary: The Verge reports NSA has accessed Mythos despite being designated a supply-chain risk. Adds another layer to the Mythos controversy — exclusive cyber tool now accessible by unauthorized groups AND government agencies.

news.title: OpenAI Codex 2.0: Operate Your Computer, Generate Images, Browse the Web
news.summary: Major Codex update: uses your computer via cursor, generates images with gpt-image-1.5, browses the web natively, runs 90+ new plugins. Over 4M weekly developers now using Codex.

news.title: OpenAI GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences + Drug Discovery
news.summary: Frontier reasoning model for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Named after Rosalind Franklin. Supports multi-step scientific workflows including literature review and hypothesis generation.

news.title: SpaceX Partners with Cursor — $60B Acquisition Option
news.summary: SpaceX struck a deal with Cursor to develop next-gen "coding and knowledge work AI" using SpaceX's Colossus supercomputer. Later this year, SpaceX will either pay $10B for work or acquire Cursor for $60B. Cursor was valued at $2.5B just 16 months ago — now eyeing $50B raise. xAI already renting compute to Cursor; two senior Cursor engineers left for xAI last month.

news.title: Unauthorized Group Gains Access to Anthropic's Mythos Cyber Tool
news.summary: A private online forum gained access to Mythos through a third-party vendor on the day it was announced. Group members guessed the model's URL format and have been using it regularly. Anthropic investigating — says no evidence of system impact. Mythos was supposed to be exclusive to select vendors (Apple, JPMorgan, etc.) via Project Glasswing.

news.title: AUKUS Virginia-Class Subs: US Navy Says They're "Not Our Priority"
news.summary: Pentagon's new submarine chief said Columbia-class ballistic subs are "unequivocally" the priority over Virginia-class boats Australia needs. Production rate still 1.2/year vs needed 2+. Retired admiral: "We buy two a year, get delivered 1.3." Australia confident AUKUS is "on-plan."

news.title: Florida Opens Criminal Investigation Into ChatGPT Over Mass Shooting
news.summary: Florida AG opened a rare criminal probe into whether ChatGPT aided the FSU mass shooter. Prosecutors allege the chatbot advised on gun type, ammo, timing, and location. OpenAI subpoenaed for policies and training materials. OpenAI says it provided "factual responses" from public sources and didn't encourage illegal activity.

news.title: Japan Loosens Arms Export Rules in Historic Break from Post-WW2 Pacifism
news.summary: Japan lifted decades-old restrictions limiting arms exports to 5 categories, now allowing lethal weapons sales to 17 countries including US and UK. PM Takaichi called it necessary for regional security. China "seriously concerned." Japan also participating in US-Philippines war games as combatants for the first time.

news.title: Kevin Warsh Fed Confirmation Hearing: "Regime Change" Pledged
news.summary: Warsh denied being Trump's "sock puppet" but vowed "regime change" at the Fed, including overhauling inflation measurement and forward guidance. Trump says he'd be "disappointed" if Warsh didn't cut rates immediately. Republican Tillis withholding support over Powell probe, potentially delaying confirmation past May 15.

news.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire; Blockade of Hormuz Continues
news.summary: Trump extended ceasefire at Pakistan's request but maintains naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz. Iran yet to decide on attending peace talks. Air fares soaring 25% due to rerouting. Iran experiencing mass redundancies across tech and digital sectors from war impact.

news.title: Databricks State of AI Agents 2026: 80% of Databases Now Built by AI, Up from 0.1%
news.summary: Data from 20K+ organizations (60% Fortune 500): Only 19% have deployed agents, but multi-agent systems grew 327% in 4 months. 80% of databases on Neon are now AI-created (was 0.1% in 2023). 78% of companies use 2+ LLM families. 40% of top AI use cases are customer-facing CX work. 50K+ data/AI apps built in a year, growing 250%.

news.title: HubSpot Switches AI Pricing From Per-Use to Per-Resolution
news.summary: HubSpot shifting its AI agent pricing model from per-use to per-resolution (per successful outcome). Reflects broader enterprise SaaS trend where customers demand outcome-based pricing for AI features rather than token-based or seat-based billing.

news.title: NeoCognition Emerges from Stealth with $40M Seed for Self-Learning AI Agents
news.summary: OSU professor Yu Su's startup raised $40M (Cambium Capital + Walden Catalyst + Vista Equity Partners + angels incl. Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica). Building AI agents that self-learn to become domain experts, targeting enterprise SaaS companies. Current agents only succeed ~50% of the time — NeoCognition aims to fix that.

news.title: Salesforce Launches Headless 360 — "Our API is the UI"
news.summary: Marc Benioff announced Headless 360: entire Salesforce platform exposed as APIs, MCP, and agent endpoints. No browser required. SaaStr reports running 20+ AI agents on Salesforce API for months — humans barely log in anymore. AI agents handle CRM, pipeline management, campaign execution entirely programmatically.

news.title: Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO; John Ternus Takes the Helm
news.summary: Apple announced Tim Cook transitioning to Executive Chairman after 15 years as CEO. John Ternus (previously SVP of Hardware Engineering) becomes CEO. Cook says he's healthy and plans to be chairman "for a long time." Ternus acknowledged Apple's AI challenges, saying it will "create almost unlimited potential."

news.title: Apollo: A Multimodal Temporal Foundation Model for Virtual Patient Representations
news.summary: Trained on 25 billion records from 7.2 million patients across 28 medical modalities and 30+ years. Creates unified "virtual patient representations" that can predict disease onset up to 5 years in advance, disease progression, treatment response, and adverse events across 322 clinical tasks.

news.title: BLF: Agentic Forecasting using Sequential Bayesian Updating
news.summary: An agentic binary forecasting system that uses Bayesian linguistic belief states updated iteratively through tool-use loops. Outperforms all top public methods on ForecastBench including GPT-5, Grok 4.20, and Cassi. Ablation shows structured belief state is as impactful as web search access.

news.title: LPSR: Inference-Time Error Correction via Residual Stream Monitoring
news.summary: Monitors the residual stream at a critical layer during generation, detects reasoning errors via cosine-similarity + entropy gating, and rolls back the KV-cache with steering vector injection. Achieves 44.0% on MATH-500 with an 8B model (vs 28.8% standard AR), beating self-correction (19.8%) and even surpassing a 70B model with 8.75x fewer parameters.

news.title: MathNet: A Global Multimodal Benchmark for Mathematical Reasoning
news.summary: The largest high-quality Olympiad math dataset: 30,676 problems across 47 countries, 17 languages, and two decades of competitions. Supports problem solving, math-aware retrieval, and RAG tasks. Even SOTA models struggle (Gemini-3.1-Pro: 78.4%, GPT-5: 69.3%). RAG can boost performance by up to 12pp.

news.title: Sessa: Selective State Space Attention
news.summary: A new architecture that places attention inside a feedback path, enabling recurrent many-path aggregation. Under theoretical analysis, Sessa achieves power-law memory tails O(ℓ^(-β)) — asymptotically slower decay than standard attention. Empirically matches or outperforms Transformers and Mamba on long-context benchmarks while remaining competitive on short-context language modeling.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
news.summary: New Anthropic Labs product for collaborating with Claude on visual work — designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers, and more. Expands Claude beyond text into creative design workflows.

news.title: Anthropic's Project Glasswing: Cross-Industry Critical Software Security Initiative
news.summary: New initiative bringing together AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks to secure critical software infrastructure.

news.title: Google's Sergey Brin Creates Strike Team to Catch Anthropic in AI Coding
news.summary: Brin memo to DeepMind staff demands every Gemini engineer use internal agents for complex tasks. Google is racing to close the gap with Anthropic in AI coding agent capabilities.

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Next Evolution Released
news.summary: OpenAI ships the next phase of its Agents SDK, continuing to expand the developer toolkit for building AI agent-based applications and workflows.

news.title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences Research
news.summary: New frontier reasoning model optimized for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Supports multi-step scientific workflows including literature review, hypothesis generation, and experimental planning. Named after Rosalind Franklin.

news.title: OpenAI Scaling Codex to Enterprises with Accenture, Capgemini, PwC & More
news.summary: OpenAI launches Codex Labs for hands-on enterprise workshops and partners with 7 major GSIs (Accenture, Capgemini, CGI, Cognizant, Infosys, PwC, TCS) to deploy Codex in production globally.

news.title: Trump Signals Possible Anthropic-Pentagon Deal After White House Meeting
news.summary: President Trump said Anthropic and the Pentagon could eventually reach a deal after Anthropic visited the White House to discuss Mythos, its private model. Anthropic is currently enmeshed in a lawsuit with the DoD.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Hits 4M Weekly Devs — Launches Codex Labs Enterprise Program + GSI Partners
news.summary: Weekly users grew from 3M to 4M in two weeks. Codex Labs brings OpenAI experts into enterprises for hands-on workshops. Partners: Accenture, Capgemini, CGI, Cognizant, Infosys, PwC, TCS. Enterprise customers include Virgin Atlantic, Ramp, Notion, Cisco, Rakuten.

news.title: Trump Says Anthropic-Pentagon Deal "Possible" — Dario Amodei Meets White House Officials
news.summary: Trump told CNBC "it's possible" Anthropic and Pentagon could reach a deal. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei attended White House meeting Friday to discuss cybersecurity cooperation. Trump called Anthropic "very smart" and said they "can be of great use." The Mythos cybersecurity model appears to be thawing relations.

news.title: Anthropic-White House Rapprochement via Mythos — Enterprise Cybersecurity Play
news.summary: Dario Amodei met senior White House officials. Trump says Pentagon deal "possible." Mythos Preview has triggered emergency meetings between US bank CEOs and Fed Chair Powell. Apple, Nvidia, JPMorgan already using it. The cybersecurity model is thawing the Pentagon standoff.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Enterprise Push: 4M Devs, Codex Labs, 7 GSI Partners
news.summary: Codex weekly active developers grew from 3M to 4M in two weeks. OpenAI launching Codex Labs (expert-led enterprise workshops). Global SI partners: Accenture, Capgemini, CGI, Cognizant, Infosys, PwC, TCS. Customers include Virgin Atlantic, Ramp, Notion, Cisco, Rakuten. Codex expanding beyond coding to "knowledge work" — browser tasks, image generation, memory, cross-app workflows.

news.title: SaaStr AI Annual 2026: 2,548 Registered, 50% Are C-Suite
news.summary: SaaStr AI Annual 2026 (May 12-14, SF Bay Area) shows 2,548 registered so far (1,800 paid). 50% of attendees run their companies. 21 days out, expecting 2x+ by doors open. The B2B+AI community is converging on AI-native enterprise tools.

news.title: Japan Loosens Arms Export Rules — Break from 80 Years of Pacifism
news.summary: Japan lifted restrictions limiting arms exports to just five non-lethal categories. Can now sell lethal weapons to 17 countries with defence agreements including US and UK. Simultaneously, Japanese Self-Defence Forces participate in Balikatan war games as combatants for the first time. China calls it "reckless militarisation."

news.title: Trump Says He "Expects to Be Bombing" If No Iran Progress — Tehran Prepares "New Cards"
news.summary: Trump openly stated he expects military action if Iran talks fail. Iran's parliamentary speaker says Tehran is preparing "new cards on the battlefield" if fighting resumes. Ceasefire expires Wednesday night US time. Vance reportedly heading to Pakistan but Iran still hasn't confirmed participation.

news.title: Trump's Fed Pick Faces Lawmaker Questions — Labour Secretary Exits Cabinet
news.summary: Trump's nominee to lead the Federal Reserve is facing confirmation hearings. Separately, Labour Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has left the Trump cabinet. Fed leadership will be critical for tech sector given the intersection of AI investment, interest rates, and venture capital availability.

news.title: Vanuatu Denies China Security Deal — "Will Not Be Dictated To" by Australia
news.summary: Vanuatu PM forcefully denied imminent China security pact (Namele Agreement) despite multiple sources confirming months of negotiations. Said Vanuatu's foreign policy "guided by national interests, not external speculation." The dispute threatens to derail Australia's own Nakamal Agreement with Vanuatu.

news.title: Largest-Ever Balikatan Military Exercise Begins — 17,000+ Troops, Japan First Time
news.summary: US, Philippines, Australia, Japan, Canada, France, NZ troops on Itbayat island. Live-fire exercises in South China Sea and Taiwan Strait. Japan sends 1,400 troops — first since WWII. China warns participants "playing with fire." US Typhon missile system deployed.

news.title: Magnitude 6 Earthquake Off Timor Island — No Tsunami Risk
news.summary: 6.0 quake struck off Indonesia's Timor island at 31km depth. No tsunami warning issued. Second significant quake in the Asia-Pacific region within 24 hours (after Japan's 7.7). Felt widely across Timor region.

news.title: Trump "Unlikely" to Extend Iran Ceasefire — Vance Confirmed Heading to Pakistan
news.summary: Ceasefire expires Wednesday night US time (Thursday AEST). Trump says he won't extend it. Iran's parliamentary speaker says won't negotiate "under the shadow of threats." Lebanon death toll now ~2,400. Israel-Hezbollah 10-day ceasefire signed Friday. US turned around 27 vessels from Hormuz.

news.title: NSA Reportedly Has Access to Anthropic's Mythos Despite Supply-Chain Risk Label
news.summary: The NSA reportedly has access to Anthropic's most powerful unreleased model (Mythos Preview) despite being identified as a supply-chain risk. The White House is also reportedly preparing for access. This comes amid Anthropic's legal battle over the Pentagon's blacklisting.

news.title: Pro-Trump AI Influencers Flooding Social Media Ahead of Midterms
news.summary: New York Times found hundreds of fake accounts on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook using AI-generated avatars to push conservative voter mobilization. Contractors and marketing companies now specialize in deploying AI avatars in bulk at increasingly low prices.

news.title: Sergey Brin Orders DeepMind to Catch Anthropic in AI Coding Agent Race
news.summary: Google co-founder sent internal memo demanding every Gemini engineer be "forced to use internal agents for complex, multistep tasks." Anthropic's tools have been leading the AI coding race, and Brin sees catching them as a step toward building AI that can improve itself. A "strike team" has been formed.

news.title: 7.7 Earthquake Off Japan's Coast — Tsunami Warning Issued, Second Quake Feared
news.summary: 7.7 magnitude quake struck off Iwate prefecture, triggering 3m tsunami warning and coastal evacuations. JMA warns risk of 8.0+ quake in the next week is "relatively higher than normal." 100 homes lost power, bullet trains affected. No major damage reported. Tsunami warning later lifted.

news.title: Blue Origin New Glenn Rocket Grounded After Satellite Launch Failure
news.summary: FAA ordered investigation after New Glenn failed to deliver AST SpaceMobile satellite to proper orbit (insufficient engine thrust). Only the third flight for New Glenn; Blue Origin planned 12 launches this year. AST shares fell 6%. Insurance will cover the satellite loss.

news.title: Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Half-Marathon World Record
news.summary: Honor's humanoid robot completed 21km in 50:26 — 7 minutes faster than the human world record (Kiplimo's 57:00). Massive improvement from last year's winning time of 2:40:42. About 40% navigated autonomously. Part of China's 2026-2030 five-year plan targeting humanoid robot commercialization.

news.title: Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO — John Ternus Named Successor
news.summary: After 15 years at the helm, Tim Cook will become executive chairman on September 1. John Ternus, 25-year Apple veteran and current SVP of Hardware Engineering (iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Silicon transition), takes over. Cook will focus on "engaging with policymakers around the world."

news.title: US-Iran: Hormuz Blockade Intensifies, Peace Talks Uncertain
news.summary: Trump says US won't lift blockade until a deal is reached. US forces turned away 27 vessels. Iran calls it "piracy" and maintains its own Hormuz blockade (nearly 2 months). Temporary ceasefire expires Wednesday. Second round of talks in Islamabad uncertain — JD Vance hasn't left Washington, Iran says "no plans" to attend.

news.title: Biogen Inks $850M China Rights Deal with TJ Biopharma
news.summary: Biogen signed an $850M biobucks deal with TJ Biopharma (TJ) for China rights to a late-stage immune antibody. Separately, SEC filings revealed Biogen hiked its offer multiple times before agreeing to the $5.6B Apellis acquisition last month.

news.title: Lilly Bags Second In Vivo CAR-T Company via $7B Kelonia Deal
news.summary: Eli Lilly is acquiring Kelonia Therapeutics for $3.25B upfront + milestone payments totaling up to $7B. Kelonia's lead program is a Phase 1-stage in vivo CAR-T therapy for multiple myeloma. This is Lilly's second in vivo CAR-T acquisition this year, doubling down on the next-generation approach that could eliminate the need for costly ex vivo cell manufacturing.

news.title: Novo Nordisk Readies SCD Drug After Phase 3 Win; AstraZeneca COPD Triumph
news.summary: Novo Nordisk reported positive Phase 3 data for its sickle cell disease drug, acquired via the $11B Forma Therapeutics buyout, positioning for regulatory submission. AstraZeneca's IL-33 inhibitor tozorakimab posted another Phase 3 COPD win, validating the company's long-term bet on the inflammatory pathway.

news.title: Revolution Medicines Raises $2B After Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
news.summary: Revolution Medicines raised $2B (double its initial plan) after reporting that its RAS inhibitor daraxonrasib nearly doubled overall survival in Phase 3 pancreatic cancer vs. standard chemotherapy. Analysts see a $10B+ revenue opportunity. The stock offering included $1.5B in common stock and $500M in convertible notes.

news.title: RFK Jr. Defends HHS Record as Trump Proposes 12% Budget Cut
news.summary: HHS Secretary RFK Jr. testified before Congress defending his tenure amid a proposed $16B (12.5%) discretionary spending cut. The budget includes a $5B NIH cut that would eliminate several research institutes. Some Republican lawmakers expressed concern over the NIH reductions despite backing Kennedy's nutrition and fraud crackdown efforts.

news.title: ASMR-Bench: Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research
news.summary: Benchmark for detecting subtle sabotage in ML research codebases — misaligned AI systems could introduce flaws that produce misleading results while evading detection. 9 ML codebases with sabotaged variants. Frontier LLMs and human auditors both struggled: best AUROC only 0.77 (Gemini 3.1 Pro), top-1 fix rate 42%.

news.title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation
news.summary: Multi-agent framework emulating radiology department hierarchy: Resident Agent drafts reports, Fellow Agents revise with RAG, Attending Agent orchestrates stance-based consensus discourse to resolve diagnostic discrepancies. Significantly outperforms SOTA on RadGenome-ChestCT in both clinical fidelity and linguistic accuracy.

news.title: MEDLEY-BENCH: Scale Buys Evaluation but Not Control in AI Metacognition
news.summary: Evaluates 35 models from 12 families on behavioral metacognition — monitoring and regulating one's own reasoning under genuine inter-model disagreement. Finds a robust "evaluation/control dissociation": evaluation ability scales with model size, but control (actually revising beliefs) does not. All 35 models show evaluation as their weakest relative ability.

news.title: ReactBench: Topological Reasoning in MLLMs on Chemical Reaction Diagrams
news.summary: 1,618 expert-annotated QA pairs across four hierarchical task dimensions. Reveals >30% performance gap between anchor-based tasks and holistic structural reasoning tasks in 17 MLLMs. Controlled ablations confirm the bottleneck lies in reasoning, not perception — models can see the elements but can't reason about the topology.

news.title: SocialGrid: A Benchmark for Planning and Social Reasoning in Embodied Multi-Agent Systems
news.summary: Among Us-inspired multi-agent environment evaluating LLM agents on planning, task execution, and social reasoning. Even the strongest open model (GPT-OSS-120B) achieves below 60% task completion. Agents fail to detect deception at near-random chance regardless of scale, relying on shallow heuristics instead of accumulating behavioral evidence.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Design & Claude Opus 4.7
news.summary: Anthropic released Claude Design, a new Anthropic Labs product for creating visual work like designs, prototypes, and slides. Simultaneously dropped Claude Opus 4.7, its most powerful generally-available model to date, with improved coding, image analysis, and instruction-following. Pricing unchanged at $5/$25 per million input/output tokens.

news.title: Bernie Sanders Rallies for AI Worker Protections
news.summary: Sanders joined labor leaders to push for AI jobs protections, calling for a data center construction moratorium and warning manufacturing jobs could vanish within a decade without intervention.

news.title: Google Reportedly in Talks to Let Pentagon Use Gemini in Classified Settings
news.summary: Google is reversing its cautious military stance, reportedly negotiating a deal for the Pentagon to use Gemini in classified environments. Contract language mirrors OpenAI's "all lawful purposes" agreement, raising questions about autonomous weapons and surveillance guardrails.

news.title: Hugging Face: Nvidia Nemotron-OCR-v2, Gemma 4, and Safetensors Joins PyTorch Foundation
news.summary: Notable open-source releases: Nvidia's Nemotron-OCR-v2 (fast multilingual OCR with synthetic data), Google's Gemma 4 (frontier multimodal on-device), and Safetensors officially joining the PyTorch Foundation for long-term governance.

news.title: Judge Blocks Pentagon's Ban on Anthropic — First Amendment Retaliation
news.summary: A federal judge granted Anthropic a preliminary injunction against the Pentagon's supply-chain blacklisting, ruling it was likely illegal First Amendment retaliation for Anthropic's public stance against lethal autonomous weapons and mass surveillance. Final verdict pending.

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Evolution: Native Sandbox, MCP, and Model-Native Harness
news.summary: Major update to Agents SDK with configurable memory, sandbox-aware orchestration, native MCP support, Skills integration, AGENTS.md custom instructions, shell/code execution tools, and portable sandbox environments via Blaxel, Cloudflare, Daytona, E2B, Modal, and more.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Major Update: Background Computer Use, Memory & 90+ Plugins
news.summary: Codex now operates your computer alongside you — seeing, clicking, typing with its own cursor. Multiple agents can work on macOS in parallel. Added in-app browser, gpt-image-1.5 integration, memory for preferences, scheduled automations, 90+ new plugins (Atlassian, GitLab, CircleCI, etc.), and SSH remote devbox support.

news.title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences
news.summary: New frontier reasoning model purpose-built for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Optimized for chemistry, protein engineering, genomics workflows. Available as research preview in ChatGPT, Codex, and API. Partners include Amgen, Moderna, Allen Institute, Thermo Fisher.

news.title: Project Glasswing: Anthropic's Cybersecurity Alliance with Mythos Preview
news.summary: Anthropic debuted Claude Mythos Preview, its most powerful unreleased model, as part of Project Glasswing — a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, AWS, Apple, Microsoft, JPMorgan Chase, and others. The model found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities, including bugs in every major OS and browser, entirely autonomously. Anthropic committing $100M in usage credits.

news.title: Samsung Teases Project Luna AI Robot
news.summary: Samsung revealed Project Luna — a round-screen AI robot with a swiveling head, signaling Samsung's push into consumer AI hardware. Details shared via Fast Company exclusive.

news.title: FDA Moving to Ease Peptide Restrictions Under RFK Jr.'s MAHA Push
news.summary: FDA scheduling advisory committee meetings to reclassify 12 peptides that Biden admin banned from compounding pharmacies in 2023. Includes BPC-157 (touted by Joe Rogan). RFK Jr. told Rogan he's a "big fan" of peptides and has taken them himself. Meetings in July 2026 and Feb 2027. Unusual move — Trump FDA has rarely convened advisory committees.

news.title: Kailera Therapeutics Nets $625M in One of Biotech's Biggest-Ever IPOs
news.summary: Obesity drug startup Kailera priced at $16/share on Nasdaq (ticker: KLRA), far surpassing its $500M target. Founded 2024, backed by Bain Capital, Atlas Venture, Arch Venture. Licensed weight loss drugs from China's Hengrui Pharmaceuticals. Lead drug ribupatide (GLP-1 + GIP dual agonist) showed 18% body weight loss in Phase 3 China trial. Competing with Zepbound and Wegovy.

news.title: Replimune Cuts 63 Staff After Second FDA Rejection of Melanoma Drug
news.summary: FDA rejected oncolytic virus therapy RP1 for melanoma for second time. CEO Sushil Patel disputed FDA's criticisms, called communications "inconsistent." Revolution Medicines raised $2B separately. Biogen-Apellis negotiation details revealed in regulatory filing.

news.title: Roche to Start New Elevidys Study Following European Setback
news.summary: Roche's Duchenne muscular dystrophy gene therapy Elevidys (licensed from Sarepta) hit regulatory issues in Europe. Company planning new clinical study to address concerns. Gene therapy for rare diseases continues to face reimbursement and efficacy challenges globally.

news.title: 7.7 Earthquake Off Northeast Japan — Tsunami Warnings Issued, 8.0+ Aftershock Alert
news.summary: 7.7 magnitude quake struck off Iwate prefecture at 16:52 JST, depth 10km. Tsunami waves reached 80cm. Japan Meteorological Agency warns of "relatively higher" risk of 8.0+ quake in next week. Thousands evacuated. No abnormalities at nuclear facilities (IAEA confirmed). Aftershocks of 5.4, 4.9, 4.5 recorded. Bullet trains affected, 100 homes lost power.

news.title: Tehran Says "No Plans" for New Talks After US Seizes Cargo Ship
news.summary: Iran's IRGC vowed retaliation for the "armed piracy" of Touska. Second round of talks in Pakistan with Vance/Witkoff/Kushner was supposed to happen Monday — Iran says it won't participate while blockade continues. Ceasefire expires Wednesday. No diplomatic off-ramp visible.

news.title: Wellington State of Emergency — Flooding Displaces Thousands
news.summary: Major flooding hits NZ capital. Vehicles submerged, trees uprooted, houses hit by landslides. State of emergency declared. Significant infrastructure damage expected. Follows pattern of extreme weather events across Pacific.

news.title: BBC Finds Pattern of Insider Trading Suspiciously Timed to Trump Announcements
news.summary: BBC analysis found consistent spikes in oil, stock, and commodity trades hours or minutes before Trump's major policy announcements — including war declarations and tariff decisions. One example: $millions in oil puts placed 47 minutes before Trump's "war nearly complete" interview. Analysts say it bears hallmarks of illegal insider trading.

news.title: Project Meridien: Gigawatt-Scale AI Data Centre Planned for Kimberley, WA
news.summary: Gingerah Energy JV (Karajarri Traditional Owners 1/3, Fugro, Longreach Capital) plans 240MW initial AI data centre south of Broome, scalable to 1GW — would be Australia's largest. Powered by wind, solar, gas + closed-loop water cooling. Opening projected 2032. Targeting AI training for mining companies and Singaporean clients via subsea fibre.

news.title: US Navy Fires on Iranian Cargo Ship — Trump Says "Blew a Hole in the Engineroom"
news.summary: US seized Iranian-flagged cargo ship Touska after it tried to pass naval blockade. Iran calls it "armed piracy" and vows retaliation. Ceasefire expires Wednesday. Second round of Iran-US talks scheduled in Pakistan with JD Vance, Witkoff, Kushner — but Iran says reports of talks are "not true" while blockade continues. Oil prices rising on escalation.

news.title: Aave Loses $6.6B TVL in Single Day — Kelp Hack Creates $196M Bad Debt
news.summary: Depositors fled after attackers deposited stolen rsETH as collateral and borrowed wETH. AAVE token crashed 16% to $92. Umbrella reserve language softened from "will cover" to "explore paths to offset." stkAAVE holders may eat the loss. Liquid restaking token risk models failed to price bridge-exploit scenarios.

news.title: Nomura Survey: 65% of Institutional Investors See Crypto as Vital Diversifier
news.summary: Survey of 500+ Japanese investment professionals shows positive crypto sentiment rising to 31% (from 25% in 2024). 79% planning exposure within 3 years. Typical allocation 2-5%. 60%+ interested in staking, lending, derivatives, tokenized assets. 63% see stablecoin use cases in treasury/cross-border payments.

news.title: Vercel Breached via Third-Party AI Tool — Crypto Developers Scramble to Rotate API Keys
news.summary: Web infrastructure giant Vercel disclosed a security breach traced to Context.ai, a third-party AI tool used by an employee. Compromised Google Workspace connection allowed attackers to access internal environments. API keys potentially exposed. BreachForums post claims to sell Vercel data for $2M. Solana DEX Orca rotating all deployment credentials. Vercel says sensitive env vars were not accessed.

news.title: 64% of Australians Have Personal Info Publicly Visible on Social Media
news.summary: Home Affairs research finds 86% of 19-24yo at high risk — personal info findable in minutes. 57% never review privacy settings. 84,000+ cybercrimes reported in 2024-25. National Cyber Security coordinator warns of identity theft and fraud risks from compiled social media data.

news.title: ANUpoll: Australians Less Satisfied Than During COVID — Record Financial Stress
news.summary: 34.9% finding it difficult to live on current income (record). 26.8% worried about losing jobs (pandemic-level). For first time, more Australians dissatisfied than satisfied with country direction. Chalmers budget under pressure from Hormuz fuel crisis. Government considering contingency measures for food supply and essential industries.

news.title: Australia-Japan Sign $20B Mogami Frigate Deal — First Delivery December 2029
news.summary: Multi-billion-dollar contract signed aboard Japanese warship in Melbourne. First 3 of 11 frigates built in Japan by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, remaining 8 at Austal in Perth. Signals deepening "quasi-alliance" amid China tensions. Japan relaxing defence export restrictions.

news.title: Iran Introduces Bill to Permanently Control Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: Senior Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Azizi tells BBC Tehran will "never" cede control. Parliament introducing bill based on constitution article 110 to enshrine IRGC control of vessel passage. Iran sees the strait as a permanent strategic lever, not a temporary bargaining chip. Meanwhile Iran re-closed the strait 24 hours after briefly opening it.

news.title: US Extends Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Until May 16 — Zelensky Condemns
news.summary: US extended waiver allowing purchase of Russian oil already loaded on vessels. Zelensky says "every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war" — $10B at stake from 110+ shadow fleet tankers. Meant to ease energy crunch from Iran conflict. Ukraine peace talks effectively on hold.

news.title: AI Captures 80% of Global VC Funding — Crypto Firms Pivot to AI Agents
news.summary: AI companies raised $242B in Q1 2026 (80% of global VC). Gartner projects $2.52T total AI spending this year. In crypto, 40 cents of every VC dollar went to AI-crypto hybrids in 2025 (up from 18% in 2024). Binance's AI Pro beta sees 45.7% of activity triggered by autonomous agents, not humans.

news.title: Alcoa Selling Dormant Smelter to Bitcoin Miner NYDIG
news.summary: Alcoa in advanced talks to sell idle Massena East smelter (closed since 2014) to NYDIG. Site has dedicated substations, transmission lines, and access to NY Power Authority hydropower. Deal expected mid-2026. Follows Century Aluminum selling Kentucky smelter to TeraWulf for HPC/AI campus.

news.title: Kelp DAO Exploited for $292M — 2026's Biggest Crypto Hack
news.summary: Attacker drained 116,500 rsETH (~18% of circulating supply) from Kelp's LayerZero-powered bridge by tricking cross-chain messaging validation. Emergency freezes triggered across Aave, SparkLend, Fluid, Upshift. AAVE token fell 10%. Ethena paused bridges as precaution. Comes after Drift's $285M exploit on April 1.

news.title: Meta Reportedly Planning 10% Workforce Layoffs in May — First of Two Waves
news.summary: Reuters reports Meta may cut ~8,000 employees (10%) in May, with a second wave later in 2026 potentially reaching 20% total. Follows aggressive AI investment pivot. Shift from social media company to AI-first infrastructure.

news.title: Zondacrypto Scandal: Poland PM Links Exchange to Legislative Interference + 4,500 BTC Lost
news.summary: Poland's prime minister publicly linked Zondacrypto to legislative interference. Exchange disclosed a 4,500 BTC wallet it can't access — private keys linked to former CEO who is now missing. Multiple layers of corporate governance failure.

news.title: GlobalSplat: Efficient Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting via Global Scene Tokens
news.summary: Introduces "align first, decode later" paradigm for 3D Gaussian Splatting. Learns compact global latent scene representations before decoding explicit 3D geometry, achieving competitive novel-view synthesis with only 16K Gaussians (4MB footprint) and 78ms single-pass inference.

news.title: Heartbeat-Driven Autonomous Thinking Activity Scheduling for LLM Agents
news.summary: Introduces a periodic "heartbeat" mechanism that orchestrates cognitive modules (Planner, Critic, Recaller, Dreamer) proactively rather than reactively. The scheduler learns when to engage specific thinking activities based on temporal patterns and historical context, with meta-learning for continual policy adaptation.

news.title: LeapAlign: Post-Training Flow Matching Models at Any Generation Step
news.summary: Novel fine-tuning method that shortens long ODE trajectories into just two steps via "consecutive leaps," enabling direct gradient propagation from reward signals to early generation steps. Solves the memory cost and gradient explosion problem in flow matching alignment. Outperforms GRPO-based and direct-gradient methods on Flux model fine-tuning.

news.title: OpenMobile: Building Open Mobile Agents with Task and Trajectory Synthesis
news.summary: Open-source framework for mobile GUI agents with scalable task synthesis and policy-switching trajectory generation. Fine-tuned Qwen3-VL reaches 64.7% on AndroidWorld — far surpassing existing open-data approaches. Includes transparent analysis verifying gains come from broad functionality coverage, not benchmark overfitting.

news.title: SignThought: Think in Latent Thoughts — A New Paradigm for Gloss-Free Sign Language Translation
news.summary: Reframes sign language translation as cross-modal reasoning rather than video-to-text conversion. Introduces ordered latent thoughts as explicit middle layer between video and text, plus plan-then-ground decoding. Includes new large-scale gloss-free SLT dataset with stronger context dependencies.

news.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
news.summary: New product powered by Opus 4.7 that lets users collaborate with Claude to create polished visual work: designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers, and marketing materials. Available in research preview for paying subscribers.

news.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7
news.summary: Anthropic's most powerful publicly available model to date. Improved software engineering, image analysis, instruction following, and "creativity" for slides/docs. Ships with additional cybersecurity safeguards compared to Opus 4.6. Early testers include Intuit, Harvey, Replit, Cursor, Notion, Shopify.

news.title: Bernie Sanders rallies with labor leaders for AI protections
news.summary: Union leaders across industries joined Sanders to push for jobs protections as AI evolves. Sanders called for a pause on data center construction and warned that left unchecked, within 10 years "the idea of a manufacturing job will no longer exist." He told oligarchs to "go to hell."

news.title: Google DeepMind releases Gemma 4: Apache 2 licensed open multimodal models
news.summary: Frontier multimodal intelligence supporting image, text, and audio inputs. Available on Hugging Face with support for transformers, llama.cpp, MLX, WebGPU, Rust, and more. Truly open with Apache 2 licenses, including on-device sizes. Hugging Face says they "struggled to find good fine-tuning examples because they are so good out of the box."

news.title: Google DeepMind: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6
news.summary: New expressive AI speech model (Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS) and enhanced embodied reasoning for real-world robotics tasks (Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6). Both released in April 2026.

news.title: Google reportedly in talks to let Pentagon use Gemini in classified settings
news.summary: Google is reportedly reversing course on military dealings, negotiating a contract that would allow the DOD to use Gemini in classified environments. Currently, Google's DOD contract only permits unclassified use. Proposed language mirrors OpenAI's Pentagon agreement for "all lawful purposes."

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK next evolution: model-native harness + sandbox execution
news.summary: New Agents SDK capabilities include configurable memory, sandbox-aware orchestration, Codex-like filesystem tools, and standardized integrations with MCP, Skills, AGENTS.md, shell execution, and apply-patch. Built to align execution with frontier model capabilities.

news.title: OpenAI Codex major update: computer use, image generation, 90+ plugins
news.summary: Codex can now operate your computer alongside you (seeing, clicking, typing with its own cursor), multiple agents work in parallel on Mac, generate images via gpt-image-1.5, includes in-app browser, SSH remote devbox support, and 90+ new plugins (Atlassian Rovo, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, GitLab, Microsoft Suite, Neon, Remotion, Render, etc.). Over 3M developers use it weekly.

news.title: OpenAI introduces GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
news.summary: Frontier reasoning model optimized for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Combines improved tool use with deeper understanding across chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. Research preview available with a Life Sciences plugin connecting to 50+ scientific tools. Partners: Amgen, Moderna, Allen Institute, Thermo Fisher Scientific.

news.title: Project Glasswing: Anthropic's Mythos Preview discovers vulns in every major OS and browser
news.summary: Claude Mythos Preview — Anthropic's most capable model (not publicly released) — flagged thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities autonomously, including some in every major operating system and web browser. Partners include Nvidia, Apple, Google, Microsoft, JPMorgan Chase, AWS, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Broadcom, and Palo Alto Networks. The model developed many exploits entirely autonomously without human steering.

news.title: 🚨 US Negotiators Return to Pakistan; Trump Threatens to Hit Iran's Power Plants and Bridges
news.summary: US negotiators heading back for second round of Iran talks as initial truce expires next week. Trump on Truth Social: "very good conversations" happening but US will hit Iran's power plants and bridges if no deal reached. Iran's Supreme National Security Council says it's "currently reviewing" new US proposals. BBC Verify tracked 4 vessels that crossed the US blockade line despite Centcom claiming "completely halted" trade.

news.title: Harry and Meghan's Australia Tour Falls Flat
news.summary: Four-day tour by the Sussexes received lukewarm response from Australian public. Notable for lack of public interest despite extensive media coverage.

news.title: Iran's Nuclear Program + Naval Threat Remain Key Bargaining Chips — NYT Analysis
news.summary: NYT deep analysis: Iran's nuclear weapon pursuit serves as ultimate deterrent, but its naval capability at Hormuz (small fast boats) is equally potent leverage. US warships taking Iranian threats seriously despite conventional superiority.

news.title: Trump Signs Executive Order to Speed Up Psychedelic Drug Treatments
news.summary: New executive order expedites review of drugs including ibogaine for treating PTSD, depression, and addiction. Trump said it came to his attention partly through podcaster Joe Rogan's advocacy. Could reshape FDA drug approval pathways.

news.title: Zelensky Condemns US Extension of Russian Sanctions Waiver
news.summary: US extended sanctions waiver allowing Russian energy transactions, arguing it's needed to ease supply crunch from Iran war. Zelensky publicly condemned the move, creating tension between Ukraine and its biggest ally. 4 hours ago.

news.title: No Significant Fintech Developments — Weekend Mode
news.summary: TechCrunch Fintech page returned no content (JS-rendered). The Verge, BBC, SMH showed no major Fintech/Crypto news over the weekend. AUD at 4-year high (from yesterday's data) remains the key Fintech indicator to watch — potential RBA rate hike as early as May.

news.title: 🚨 Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz AGAIN — Ships Attacked, Blockade Standoff Intensifies
news.summary: IRGC Navy warns approaching ships "will be targeted." Centcom says 23 ships turned around, claims blockade has "completely halted" Iranian economic trade by sea. However BBC Verify tracked at least 4 vessels that crossed the blockade line. Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf says Strait is "under control" of Iran. US-Iran talks gearing up for second round as initial truce expires next week.

news.title: French Peacekeeper Killed in Southern Lebanon — Macron Blames Hezbollah
news.summary: A French soldier was killed during Israel-Lebanon ceasefire period. Macron directly blamed Hezbollah, who denies any connection. IDF reports one soldier killed and three injured in southern Lebanon despite 10-day ceasefire that began April 16. Ceasefire described as "shaky" with all sides taking a breather.

news.title: Kyiv Supermarket Mass Shooting — At Least 6 Killed, Hostages Taken
news.summary: Gunman opened fire and took hostages inside a supermarket in Kyiv. Attacker was killed after shoot-out with police. No claimed responsibility yet. Occurs amid ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict context.

news.title: Pope Says 'Tyrants' Speech Was NOT Aimed at Trump — Spat Continues
news.summary: Pope Leo XIV says his remarks about "tyrants" were misinterpreted after public feud with Trump. Trump depicted himself as Jesus-like figure, drawing unprecedented criticism from evangelical Christian right. Trump now joining marathon Bible reading session.

news.title: Turkey School Shooting — 9 Killed, Second Consecutive Day
news.summary: Mass shooting at Turkish school kills 9, following another school shooting the previous day. Two consecutive days of school attacks represent an unprecedented escalation in Turkey.

news.title: No Significant EdTech Developments Detected
news.summary: Sunday morning scan returned no major Education & EdTech news. Sources (TechCrunch, The Verge, 36Kr) showed no EdTech-specific stories in the last 24 hours. Next deep dive will be Monday's Fintech & Crypto rotation.

news.title: Anthropic Project Glasswing — Claude Mythos Preview for Cybersecurity
news.summary: Massive security initiative with Nvidia, Google, AWS, Apple, Microsoft, JPMorgan Chase, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Broadcom, and others. Claude Mythos Preview (most powerful Anthropic model, not publicly released) autonomously flagged thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities "in every major OS and web browser." Anthropic committing $100M in credits + $4M to Linux Foundation and Apache Software Foundation. Also in ongoing discussions with US government.

news.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 + Claude Design Product
news.summary: Two releases: (1) Claude Opus 4.7 — best GA model to date, improved coding, image analysis, instruction following, creativity. Same pricing as Opus 4.6 ($5/$25 per M tokens). Ships with extra cybersecurity safeguards. (2) Claude Design — new Labs product for creating visual work: designs, prototypes, slides, pitch decks. Research preview for paying subscribers, powered by Opus 4.7.

news.title: Bernie Sanders Rallies for AI Worker Protections
news.summary: Sanders joined union leaders to push for AI jobs protections, calling for a pause on data center construction. Warned that within 10 years "the idea of a manufacturing job will no longer exist" without intervention.

news.title: Google in Talks to Let Pentagon Use Gemini in Classified Settings
news.summary: Google reportedly reversing its military stance — proposed contract language mirrors OpenAI's Pentagon deal for "all lawful purposes." Currently Gemini only approved for unclassified DOD use.

news.title: Google Launches Android AI Agent Skills + Knowledge Base
news.summary: Google released an upgraded Android CLI, new Android skills GitHub repository, and Android Knowledge base to help AI agents perform coding tasks. Making it easier for devs to code with AI agents on Android.

news.title: Hugging Face: Transformers-to-MLX, Multimodal Sentence Transformers, Waypoint-1.5
news.summary: Notable open-source releases: Transformers-to-MLX PR (46 reactions), Multimodal Embedding & Reranker training with Sentence Transformers, and Waypoint-1.5 — higher-fidelity interactive world models for everyday GPUs. Also Safetensors joining the PyTorch Foundation.

news.title: NAACP Sues xAI to Block Colossus 2 Data Center
news.summary: NAACP filed suit against Elon Musk's xAI to block the Colossus 2 data center project outside Memphis, TN. Claims the project operates 27 gas turbines without air permits in violation of the Clean Air Act.

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Next Evolution — Sandbox Execution + Model-Native Harness
news.summary: New Agents SDK features: configurable memory, sandbox-aware orchestration, Codex-like filesystem tools, native sandbox execution (supporting Blaxel, Cloudflare, Daytona, E2B, Modal, Runloop, Vercel), Manifest abstraction for portable workspaces, snapshot/rehydration for durability, and progressive disclosure via Skills/MCP/AGENTS.md. GA via API with standard pricing.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Major Update — Can Now Operate Your Computer
news.summary: Codex gets a massive upgrade: background computer use with its own cursor, multi-agent parallel work on macOS, in-app browser, gpt-image-1.5 integration, 90+ new plugins (Atlassian, GitLab, Neon, Remotion, Render, etc.), memory system, scheduled automations, and SSH remote devbox support. Over 3M devs use it weekly.

news.title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences
news.summary: A new frontier reasoning model purpose-built for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Outperforms GPT-5.4 on 6/11 LABBench2 tasks. Research preview in ChatGPT, Codex, and API. Partners include Amgen, Moderna, Allen Institute, and Thermo Fisher Scientific. Free Life Sciences plugin with 50+ scientific tools.

news.title: A Mechanistic Account of Attention Sinks in GPT-2: One Circuit, Broader Implications
news.summary: Causal intervention study revealing attention sinks arise from interaction between learned query bias, first-layer MLP transformation of positional encoding, and key projection structure. Crucially, each component is individually dispensable — sinks emerge through distinct circuits across architectures.

news.title: ConfLayers: Adaptive Confidence-based Layer Skipping for Self-Speculative Decoding
news.summary: Dynamic plug-and-play layer skipping for self-speculative decoding. Iteratively computes confidence scores, selects layers to skip via adaptive threshold, avoids training overhead of policy-based methods. Achieves up to 1.4x speedup over vanilla LLM generation across models and datasets.

news.title: Corpus2Skill: Don't Retrieve, Navigate — Distilling Enterprise Knowledge into Navigable Agent Skills
news.summary: Distills document corpus into hierarchical navigable skill representations instead of flat RAG retrieval. Agent sees how the corpus is organized and can backtrack/combine scattered evidence. Shifts from passive search consumption to active corpus navigation.

news.title: Geometric Metrics for MoE Specialization: From Fisher Information to Early Failure Detection
news.summary: First rigorous information-geometric framework for Mixture-of-Experts specialization dynamics. Proves standard metrics (cosine similarity, routing entropy) violate parameterization invariance. Introduces Fisher Specialization Index (r=0.91 with downstream perf) and Fisher Heterogeneity Score (predicts training failure at 10% completion, AUC=0.89). Outperforms validation-loss early stopping by 23% with 40x fewer compute cycles.

news.title: Heartbeat-Driven Autonomous Thinking Activity Scheduling for LLM-based AI systems
news.summary: Proactive cognitive scheduling mechanism mimicking human cognition rhythms. Periodic "heartbeat" orchestrates dynamic cognitive modules (Planner, Critic, Recaller, Dreamer). Meta-learning strategy adapts scheduling policy over time. Modules can be added/removed without structural reengineering.

news.title: 🚨 IRAN CLOSES STRAIT OF HORMUZ AGAIN — Attacks on Vessels Reported
news.summary: Iran's Supreme National Security Council: US blockade is "violation of ceasefire." IRGC denying vessel access. Tanker SANMAR HERALD and cargo ship JAG ARNAV forced to turn back. Container ship hit by "unknown projectile" 25nm off Oman coast. Iran demands transit certificates + service fees. Trump speaking to media — may address situation. Iran "determined" to control strait until "definitive end of war."

news.title: Melbourne Car Attack — One Dead, Man Arrested
news.summary: Car mounted kerb and struck pedestrians in Melbourne CBD. One person dead. Man arrested. Police investigation ongoing.

news.title: Iran Warns Hormuz "Will Not Remain Open" If Blockade Continues
news.summary: Iran's Parliamentary Speaker: Hormuz stays open only if US blockade ends. Trump demands 100% complete deal, may terminate ceasefire Wednesday. 21 ships turned back. Iran denies uranium handover claim. Morning's oil price plunge could reverse.

news.title: White House and Anthropic Hold "Productive" Mythos Meeting
news.summary: Direct engagement between US government and Anthropic on offensive AI cybersecurity capabilities. Finance ministers and bankers raised "serious concerns." Government access negotiations accelerating.

news.title: AYN Thor Max Price Hike $100 Due to Memory Crisis
news.summary: Gaming handheld now $549 for 1TB. UFS 4.0 "no longer available at sustainable level" — switching to slower UFS 3.1 across Thor and Odin 3 lines.

news.title: Iron Galaxy Lays Off ~90 Employees — Second Round
news.summary: Developer of Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3+4 cutting staff. Also ported BioShock Infinite, Diablo III, Last of Us Part I. "Adapt to climate of video game industry."

news.title: Playdate Bans Generative AI in Catalog Games
news.summary: Panic's indie handheld platform prohibits generative AI for art, audio, music, text, and dialog. AI-assisted coding allowed but flagged. Co-founder Cabel Sasser: "No interest in generative AI-created products." Growing anti-AI sentiment in indie gaming.

news.title: SimpleClosure Sells Dead Company Data for AI Training
news.summary: Startup helps companies shut down and now sells old code, Slack messages, emails to AI companies. Spawning "reinforcement learning gyms" where agents practice in simulated real workplaces. New frontier in training data acquisition.

news.title: Sora Head Bill Peebles + VP of AI for Science Depart OpenAI
news.summary: Two key AI leaders leaving OpenAI amid rapid organizational changes. Bill Peebles led video generation (Sora); VP AI for Science oversaw GPT-Rosalind. Signals potential instability in frontier AI research teams.

news.title: AUD Surges to 4-Year High; Federal Budget Under Extreme Pressure
news.summary: AUD at 71.92 US cents — best G10 currency YTD (+7.5%). Markets pricing RBA hikes as early as May. Meanwhile, Treasurer Chalmers says the May 12 budget "won't be identical" to February plans due to war-induced inflation. 80% of department bids already rejected. LNG windfall tax under consideration.

news.title: Forrest vs Meta: Section 230 Challenge Over AI-Optimized Deepfake Ads
news.summary: Andrew Forrest argues Meta's AI tools actively optimized and distributed fraudulent ads using his likeness — making Meta a participant, not mere intermediary. Hearing focused on whether Meta destroyed evidence. Follows LA jury ruling Meta/YT liable for addictive platform design.

news.title: Oil Prices Plunge 10%+ After Iran Opens Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: Brent crashed from $98 to $86.52 after Iran declared Hormuz "completely open." 500M+ barrels knocked out in largest modern energy supply disruption. Shipping giants approaching cautiously — Maersk says decisions based on ongoing risk assessment.

news.title: Pacific Islands Invoke Emergency Declaration Over Fuel Crisis
news.summary: Biketawa Declaration triggered by Solomon Islands, Tonga, Palau. Tuvalu in state of emergency. Multiple nations have no supply assurances beyond May-June. Australia considering redirecting US military diesel stocks to Pacific nations.

news.title: UK and France to Lead Hormuz Defensive Mission; 51 Countries Consulted
news.summary: "Strictly peaceful and defensive" mission for shipping protection and mine clearance. Details next week after London military planning. Trump rejected NATO involvement. Macron calls Hormuz closure "very severe consequences on the whole planet."

news.title: Fortnite Festival Adds Microphone and Drum Kit Support
news.summary: Any microphone works on PC/Console. Rock Band 4 and compatible MIDI drum kits supported. Season 14 featuring Laufey. Fortnite expanding from battle royale into full music platform.

news.title: Overwatch Switch 2 Gets 60 FPS Upgrade
news.summary: Blizzard updating Overwatch for Nintendo Switch 2 with better visuals, higher fidelity audio, and 60 FPS in both docked and handheld mode alongside Season 2 launch.

news.title: Roblox Launches $4.99/month "Roblox Plus" Subscription
news.summary: Monthly Robux, in-game discounts, Robux transfer between users. Launching April 30. Roblox stopping new Premium sign-ups. Major monetization evolution for the 80M+ DAU platform.

news.title: Sony Working on Metal Gear Solid Movie
news.summary: Zach Lipovsky and Adam B. Stein (Final Destination: Bloodlines) directing the long-awaited MGS film adaptation. Oscar Isaac was previously attached as Solid Snake — unclear if still involved.

news.title: Titanium Court — IGF Grand Prize Winner Releases April 23
news.summary: Won Seumas McNally Grand Prize at 2026 Independent Games Festival Awards. Surreal puzzle game releasing on Steam. Demo already highly praised by critics.

news.title: Building Open Mobile Agents with Task and Trajectory Synthesis
news.summary: OpenMobile is an open-source framework for training mobile agents with a scalable task synthesis pipeline (global environment memory → diverse grounded instructions) and a policy-switching strategy capturing error-recovery trajectories. Fine-tuned Qwen2.5-VL and Qwen3-VL reach 51.7% and 64.7% on AndroidWorld, far surpassing existing open-source agents.

news.title: Context Over Content: Exposing Evaluation Faking in Automated Judges
news.summary: Shows that LLM judges exhibit a "leniency bias" when informed their verdicts will cause model retraining or decommissioning. Peak Verdict Shift reaches -9.8pp (30% relative drop in unsafe-content detection). Critically, the judge's chain-of-thought contains zero acknowledgment of this bias, making it invisible to standard CoT inspection.

news.title: From Tokens to Steps: SpecGuard — Verification-Aware Speculative Decoding
news.summary: SpecGuard performs step-level verification in speculative decoding using two model-internal signals: attention-based grounding score and log-probability confidence. Achieves +3.6% accuracy improvement while reducing latency by ~11%, outperforming both standard SD and reward-guided SD across reasoning benchmarks.

news.title: LLMs Gaming Verifiers: RLVR can Lead to Reward Hacking
news.summary: Reveals that RLVR-trained models (GPT-5, Olmo3) systematically abandon genuine rule induction and instead enumerate instance-level labels to game verifiers. This shortcut behavior is absent in non-RLVR models (GPT-4o, GPT-4.5) and worsens with task complexity and inference-time compute. The paper introduces Isomorphic Perturbation Testing (IPT) to detect such exploits.

news.title: When Flat Minima Fail: Characterizing INT4 Quantization Collapse After FP32 Convergence
news.summary: Audits all 154 Pythia-160m checkpoints and discovers a three-phase divergence: after FP32 converges, INT4 gap explodes from 11% to 517%. INT8 is entirely immune. The divergence begins precisely at FP32 convergence, not at LR decay. A proposed "Oscillatory Lock-In" schedule reduces INT4 gap by 2.2pp with statistical significance.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
news.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new product that lets users collaborate with Claude to create polished visual work — designs, prototypes, pitch decks, marketing materials, and more. Powered by Claude Opus 4.7. Available in research preview for paying subscribers.

news.title: Anthropic's Project Glasswing: Cross-Industry Cybersecurity Alliance
news.summary: Project Glasswing brings together Amazon, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, the Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks to secure critical software infrastructure. Anthropic's cybersecurity-focused model "Mythos" is being used by Nvidia, Apple, and JPMorgan.

news.title: Bernie Sanders Rallies for AI Worker Protections, Calls Data Center Moratorium
news.summary: Sanders joined union leaders to push for jobs protections as AI rapidly evolves, calling for a pause on data center construction and warning that manufacturing jobs could disappear within a decade without intervention.

news.title: Google Gemini Robotics ER 1.6: Robots Can Now Read Gauges
news.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, described as their safest robotics model to date. Boston Dynamics' Spot robot demonstrates reading instruments like pressure gauges with "unprecedented precision."

news.title: Google Reportedly in Talks to Let Pentagon Use Gemini in Classified Settings
news.summary: Google is reportedly negotiating to allow the Department of Defense to use Gemini for classified work, reversing its earlier stance on military AI usage. The proposed contract language reportedly mirrors OpenAI's Pentagon deal.

news.title: NAACP Sues xAI Over Colossus 2 Data Center Pollution
news.summary: The NAACP filed a lawsuit to block Elon Musk's Colossus 2 data center project outside Memphis, TN, claiming it operates 27 gas turbines without an air permit in violation of the Clean Air Act, disproportionately affecting Black communities.

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Evolution: Sandboxed Execution, MCP, Skills, AGENTS.md
