news.summary: The Agents SDK now includes a model-native harness for computer/file/tool work, native sandbox execution (Blaxel, Cloudflare, Daytona, E2B, Modal, Runloop, Vercel), configurable memory, and standardized integrations with MCP, Skills, AGENTS.md, shell, and apply-patch tools.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Major Update: Desktop Computer Use, Image Gen, Memory, 90+ Plugins
news.summary: Codex can now operate your macOS apps autonomously in the background, generate images with gpt-image-1.5, remember preferences across sessions via memory, schedule future work, and work with 90+ new plugins including JIRA, CircleCI, GitLab, and more. This is the biggest Codex update since launch.

news.title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences
news.summary: A new frontier reasoning model purpose-built for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Optimized for scientific workflows including literature review, sequence-to-function interpretation, experimental planning, and data analysis. Named after Rosalind Franklin. Available in research preview for qualified customers.

news.title: US Data Centers Face Mandatory Energy Usage Surveys
news.summary: The Energy Information Administration plans to launch nationwide mandatory energy usage surveys for data centers, starting with pilots in Texas, Washington state, DC, and northern Virginia — responding to bipartisan pressure.

news.title: Asha Sharma Making Big Decisions on Game Pass and Xbox Platform
news.summary: The Verge reports Asha Sharma is already influencing major Game Pass and Xbox platform decisions. Signals Microsoft's continued investment in Game Pass as the center of its gaming strategy, with new leadership shaping the service's direction.

news.title: Battlefield 6 Getting "Biggest Map Yet" — EA 2026 Roadmap
news.summary: EA's 2026 roadmap shows BF6 adding a reimagined Golmud Railway from BF4 in May, plus a naval-focused map in July that's even bigger. Season 3 and 4 content pipeline looks strong.

news.title: Call of Duty Movie Gets Release Date: June 30, 2028
news.summary: Activision and Paramount's Call of Duty movie, directed by Peter Berg with Taylor Sheridan writing/producing, hits theaters June 2028. One of gaming's biggest IP adaptations finally has a timeline.

news.title: Playdate Season 3 Confirmed for Late 2026 + Educational Initiative
news.summary: Panic confirmed Season 3 of Playdate games coming late 2026, plus a new educational initiative for teaching game design and programming. The indie handheld continues to carve out a unique niche.

news.title: Roblox Native PS5 App Launches — 30% Faster Load Times
news.summary: Roblox now has a dedicated PS5 app with 30% faster load times and smoother performance. The UGC platform continues its console expansion from the PS4/PS5 launch in October 2023.

news.title: BBC Profiles Claude Mythos — "What Is It and What Risks Does It Pose?"
news.summary: BBC published an explainer on Anthropic's Claude Mythos, the private cybersecurity model that can outperform humans at hacking tasks. The piece highlights growing fears in the financial world about AI-powered offensive cyber capabilities being concentrated in private hands.

news.title: Starmer Says "Staggering" He Wasn't Told Mandelson Failed Security Vetting
news.summary: UK PM Keir Starmer expressed shock that he was not informed about Lord Mandelson's failed security vetting before his appointment. The top Foreign Office official is also leaving their post over the vetting row.

news.title: Strait of Hormuz "Completely Open" to Commercial Ships — Iran Says
news.summary: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" to commercial shipping for the remainder of the ceasefire period. However, Trump stated the US blockade of Iranian ports will remain in place until a permanent peace deal is agreed. The Israel-Lebanon 10-day ceasefire continues with celebrations in Lebanon. The BBC reports "Tehran does not think it has lost this war."

news.title: Australian PM: Major Refinery Fire Won't Lead to Fuel Rationing
news.summary: PM assured Australians that despite the "unprecedented" refinery fire compounding global energy pressures from the Iran war, fuel rationing will not be implemented. Government working to secure alternative supply routes and manage short-term disruptions.

news.title: India Factory Protests Expose Strain in Industrial System
news.summary: Workers in northern Indian cities protesting for better pay and working conditions. Protests highlight growing labor tensions in India's manufacturing sector, which has been positioned as the alternative to China for global supply chains.

news.title: Trump Claims Iran Agreed to Turn Over Enriched Uranium — Tehran Silent
news.summary: President Trump stated that Iran has agreed to turn over its enriched uranium as part of a broader deal, saying the US is "very close" to a deal with Iran. Tehran has not commented on the claim. The Israel-Lebanon 10-day ceasefire remains in effect, with Trump inviting both leaders to Washington. However, few Israelis see the truce as a genuine path to peace.

news.title: Compressed-Sensing-Guided, Inference-Aware Structured Reduction for LLMs
news.summary: Unifies model compression and prompt compression into a single compressed-sensing framework for dynamic LLM execution. Random measurement operators probe latent model usage, sparse recovery estimates task-conditioned and token-adaptive support sets, compiled into hardware-efficient sparse execution paths over blocks, attention heads, channels, and FFN substructures. Formal sample-complexity bounds provided.

news.title: Energy-Aware Gradient Coordinator for Robust Generalized Category Discovery (EAGC)
news.summary: Identifies "gradient entanglement" as a key bottleneck in Generalized Category Discovery — where supervised and unsupervised optimization objectives interfere with each other. EAGC is a plug-and-play gradient-level module with Anchor-based Gradient Alignment and Energy-aware Elastic Projection. Consistently boosts existing methods and achieves new SOTA on GCD benchmarks. Code available.

news.title: MemGround: Long-Term Memory Evaluation Kit for LLMs in Gamified Scenarios
news.summary: Proposes MemGround, a long-term memory benchmark grounded in gamified interactive scenarios. Three-tier hierarchical framework: Surface State Memory, Temporal Associative Memory, and Reasoning-Based Memory. Multi-dimensional metrics: QA Score, Memory Fragments Unlocked, Correct Order, and Exploration Trajectory Diagrams. Reveals that SOTA LLMs and memory agents still struggle with sustained dynamic tracking and complex reasoning from long-term accumulated evidence.

news.title: Thermodynamic Diffusion Inference with Minimal Digital Conditioning
news.summary: First demonstration of trained-weight, production-scale thermodynamic diffusion inference. Achieves 0.9906 decoder cosine similarity against oracle (upper bound 1.0) using a 2,560-parameter minimal digital interface. Solves both fundamental barriers: non-local skip connections via hierarchical bilinear coupling (O(Dk) instead of O(D²)), and input conditioning via a 4D bottleneck encoder + 16-unit transfer network. Theoretical net energy savings: ~10⁷x over GPU inference.

news.title: AI Mode Can Now Search Your Open Browser Tabs
news.summary: Google's AI Mode in Chrome now supports searching across open browser tabs. Brings AI-powered retrieval to users' active browsing context — a quality-of-life upgrade for research workflows.

news.title: White House Reportedly Preparing for Access to Anthropic's Mythos
news.summary: The US government is reportedly working to gain access to Anthropic's private cybersecurity-focused model, Mythos. The model has been used by Nvidia, Apple, and JPMorgan Chase to discover high-stakes vulnerabilities. Government access would represent a significant escalation in AI-powered national cybersecurity capabilities.

news.title: "Unprecedented" Fire at Australian Oil Refinery Threatens Petrol Supplies
news.summary: A major fire at an Australian oil refinery has deepened fears over the nation's petrol supplies amid the global energy crunch from the Iran war. Authorities described it as unprecedented, with supply impacts expected nationwide.

news.title: China's Economy Grows Faster Than Expected Despite Iran War
news.summary: China's GDP data came in better than expected despite the broader impact of the Iran conflict on Asian economies. Japan pledged $10 billion in aid to help Asian countries deal with the oil crisis — roughly equivalent to a year's worth of additional energy costs for affected nations.

news.title: Europe Has "Maybe Six Weeks of Jet Fuel Left" — IEA Warning
news.summary: The International Energy Agency warned that Europe could face flight cancellations within six weeks if Gulf oil supplies remain blocked due to the Iran conflict. The energy crunch is already rippling through aviation and logistics.

news.title: Israel-Lebanon 10-Day Ceasefire Takes Effect Amid Wider Iran War
news.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire brokered by the US. Trump invited both leaders to Washington. Netanyahu insists troops will not leave southern Lebanon. Meanwhile, the broader Iran war continues — Israel has conducted extensive strikes on Iranian targets with significant civilian casualties confirmed by BBC reporting. The House narrowly rejected a measure to block Trump from striking Iran.

news.title: Sweden Cutting Back on Digital Learning — Return to Books and Paper
news.summary: Swedish classrooms are swapping laptops for books, pens, and paper, raising concerns from the tech sector. The trend represents a significant pushback against screen-based education in one of Europe's most digitally advanced nations.

news.title: Data Centers on the Ballot: Wisconsin City Votes for New Hurdles
news.summary: A Wisconsin city voted to add new regulatory hurdles to data center development in response to unwanted construction. More communities are expected to follow suit as data center sprawl triggers local backlash.

news.title: Fervo Energy Signs 1.75 GW Geothermal Turbine Deal
news.summary: Houston-based Fervo Energy signed a deal with Turboden America for up to 1.75 GW in next-generation geothermal power plant units. Fervo's enhanced geothermal technology is being positioned as baseload clean energy for AI data centers.

news.title: Georgia Power Now Allows Data Centers to Bring Their Own Clean Energy
news.summary: Georgia Power changed its policy to permit data centers to procure and bring their own clean energy to the grid. A significant shift from the utility's previous stance, driven by massive data center demand in the region.

news.title: Renewables Beat Natural Gas on US Grid for the First Time
news.summary: For the first time in history, renewable energy (primarily solar) generated more electricity than natural gas on the US power grid for an entire month. While just one data point, it signals the accelerating shift in America's energy mix.

news.title: Steagra Lands €1.4B to Complete World's First Major Green Steel Mill
news.summary: Swedish company Steagra secured €1.4 billion in funding to rescue and complete its financially troubled green-steel mill in northern Sweden. The facility will use green hydrogen to produce steel — the first major plant of its kind globally.

news.title: Bernie Sanders Rallies with Labor Leaders for AI Protections
news.summary: Vermont senator joined union leaders across industries to push for jobs protections as AI evolves rapidly. Sanders called for a pause on data center construction and warned that within ten years "the idea of a manufacturing job will no longer exist" if left unchecked.

news.title: Google in Talks to Let Pentagon Use Gemini in Classified Settings
news.summary: Google reportedly reversing its approach to military AI dealings, proposing contract language that mirrors OpenAI's Pentagon agreement for "all lawful purposes." Currently DOD can only use Gemini in unclassified settings. Contract terms on autonomous weapons and surveillance remain ambiguous despite legal language.

news.title: Google Upgrades Android AI Agent Development
news.summary: Google launched upgraded Android CLI, new Android skills GitHub repository, and Android Knowledge base to help AI agents perform coding tasks. Part of broader push to make Android development more accessible to AI coding agents.

news.title: Hugging Face: Transformers to MLX — AI-Assisted Model Porting
news.summary: HF released a Skill and test harness to port language models from transformers to mlx-lm, making Apple Silicon inference near-instant for new models. Discusses open source contribution in the age of code agents — designed as an aide for contributors and reviewers, not full automation.

news.title: OpenAI Introduces GPT-Rosalind — Frontier Model for Life Sciences
news.summary: Specialized reasoning model for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. Optimized for scientific workflows combining tool use with deep understanding across chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. Named after Rosalind Franklin. Available in ChatGPT, Codex, and API. Partners include Amgen, Moderna, Allen Institute, Thermo Fisher Scientific. Free Life Sciences research plugin connects to 50+ scientific tools.

news.title: From P(y|x) to P(y): Investigating Reinforcement Learning in Pre-train Space
news.summary: Introduces PreRL, which applies reward-driven RL updates directly to the pre-train marginal distribution P(y) rather than the conditional P(y|x). Discovers that Negative Sample Reinforcement (NSR) within PreRL is exceptionally effective — increasing transition/reflection thoughts by 14.89x and 6.54x respectively. The proposed Dual Space RL (DSRL) "Policy Reincarnation" strategy consistently outperforms strong baselines.

news.title: From Weights to Activations: Is Steering the Next Frontier of Adaptation?
news.summary: Argues that activation steering should be formally regarded as a model adaptation method, introducing functional criteria to compare it with fine-tuning, PEFT, and prompting. Positions steering as a distinct paradigm enabling local, reversible behavioral change without parameter updates, and proposes a unified taxonomy for all model adaptation methods.

news.title: LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces LongCoT, a benchmark of 2,500 expert-designed problems across chemistry, mathematics, computer science, chess, and logic to measure long-horizon CoT reasoning in frontier models. Short inputs with verifiable answers isolate the ability to plan and manage long, complex reasoning chains.

news.title: TIP: Token Importance in On-Policy Distillation
news.summary: Discovers that in on-policy knowledge distillation, the most informative tokens come from two regions: high student entropy, and low student entropy with high teacher-student divergence (where the student is overconfident and wrong). Training on <10% of carefully selected tokens nearly matches full-token baselines, reducing peak memory by up to 47%.

news.title: π-Play: Multi-Agent Self-Play via Privileged Self-Distillation
news.summary: Proposes π-Play, a multi-agent self-evolution framework where an examiner generates tasks with question construction paths (QCPs), and a teacher model uses QCPs as privileged context to densely supervise a student via self-distillation. Data-free π-Play surpasses fully supervised search agents and improves efficiency by 2-3x over conventional self-play.

news.title: Anthropic Project Glasswing — Cross-Industry Software Security Initiative
news.summary: Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, uniting AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks to secure the world's most critical software infrastructure. A massive industry coalition.

news.title: Google Gemini 3.1 Deep Think — Advanced Reasoning Mode
news.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini 3.1 Deep Think, a specialized reasoning mode for complex technical problems. Available for Google AI Ultra subscribers, it offers "unprecedented depth and nuance" in reasoning with multimodal understanding across text, images, video, audio, and code.

news.title: NAACP Sues xAI Over Colossus 2 Data Center Pollution
news.summary: NAACP filed a notice of intent to sue xAI for illegally operating 27 gas turbines at its Colossus 2 data center in Memphis without air permits, violating the Clean Air Act. Drone thermal imagery confirmed unpermitted turbine operations.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN — Building AI Communications
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN to strengthen its communications arm around the AI conversation. TBPN is known for convening influential voices across tech, business, and culture. Fidji Simo framed it as addressing the need for "real, constructive conversation about the changes AI creates."

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK — Next Evolution with Model-Native Harness
news.summary: New Agents SDK release adds a model-native harness for computer/file/tool work, native sandbox execution, configurable memory, MCP tool use, progressive disclosure via AgentSkills, AGENTS.md custom instructions, shell execution, and apply-patch file editing. Built to align agent execution with frontier model capabilities.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Major Update — Computer Use + Image Generation + 90+ Plugins
news.summary: Codex can now operate your computer (see, click, type with its own cursor), run multiple agents in parallel on Mac, generate images with gpt-image-1.5, browse the web in-app, connect to remote devboxes via SSH, and address GitHub review comments. Over 90 new plugins launched including JIRA, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, GitLab, and Microsoft Suite integrations.

news.title: OpenAI Trusted Access for Cyber Defense — $10M API Credits
news.summary: OpenAI launched "Trusted Access for Cyber" program with $10M in API credits through its Cybersecurity Grant Program. Designed to scale advanced cyber capabilities to defenders — from open-source security teams to enterprises — with trust-based access tiers.

news.title: US Data Centers Face Mandatory Energy Usage Surveys
news.summary: The Energy Information Administration plans to launch nationwide mandatory energy usage surveys for data centers, starting with pilots in Texas, Washington state, DC, and northern Virginia. Responds to bipartisan Senate push for transparency on data center power consumption.

news.title: Conflicts Make Large Reasoning Models Vulnerable to Attacks
news.summary: Investigates how Large Reasoning Models (o1, o3, DeepSeek-R1 class) respond to harmful queries when confronted with conflicting objectives — internal conflicts (alignment values pitted against each other) and external conflicts (adversarial context). Shows that reasoning models are significantly more vulnerable to jailbreaking when conflicts are introduced, compared to standard LLMs.

news.title: Parallax: Why AI Agents That Think Must Never Act
news.summary: Argues for strict separation between "thinking" agents (reasoning, planning, analysis) and "acting" agents (execution, API calls, tool use). With 80% of enterprise apps projected to embed AI copilots, the paper proposes an architectural pattern where reasoning models never directly execute actions — instead producing structured plans that separate executor agents validate and execute.

news.title: VFA: Vector Relieved Flash Attention — 2x Speedup on Modern Hardware
news.summary: Revisits FlashAttention's online softmax bottleneck. VFA initializes running maximum via cheap key-block approximations, reorders traversal to prioritize sink/local blocks, and freezes the maximum to avoid repeated reductions. Achieves nearly 2x speedup over C16V32 baseline on modern accelerators, with projected 6x speedup on upcoming architectures.

news.title: Anthropic Mythos Coming to UK Banks Next Week — Glasswing Expands to Financial Sector
news.summary: Anthropic plans to release Mythos model to UK financial institutions within the next week. Pip White, Anthropic's UK/IE/Nordics head, confirmed "intensive conversations" with UK CEOs. Glasswing Project expanding from cybersecurity into financial services.

news.title: Dangsheng Technology (当升科技): ¥145M Investment in Lithium Iron Phosphate Integration
news.summary: Chinese battery materials company investing ¥145M to acquire 51% of Youtian Tech and 49% of Juneng New Materials. Joint venture with Yuntianhua to build 150K tons/year LFP production line and 200K tons/year LFP precursor project.

news.title: Hesheng Silicon (合盛硅业): ¥2.99B Net Loss in 2025 — Silicon Material Price Collapse
news.summary: Major Chinese silicon producer reported ¥29.91B net loss, swinging from ¥1.74B profit in 2024. Revenue down 23.2% to ¥205B. No dividend planned. Silicon material oversupply and price collapse driven by massive capacity expansion.

news.title: IEA: Europe Has "Maybe 6 Weeks of Jet Fuel Left" — Summer Cancellations Inevitable
news.summary: IEA executive director Fatih Birol warns European jet fuel stocks reach tipping point in June. Europe relies on Middle East for 75% of jet fuel imports; Strait of Hormuz blocked for 6+ weeks. US exports only cover ~50% of lost supply. Airlines already cutting back services.

news.title: xAI Colossus 2: NAACP Lawsuit Highlights AI Data Center Pollution Crisis
news.summary: NAACP sues xAI to block Colossus 2 data center expansion near Memphis, citing 27 gas turbines operating without air permits in violation of Clean Air Act. Community health impact from emissions and known carcinogens disproportionately affecting Black communities. Meanwhile, EIA announces mandatory energy usage surveys for all US data centers.

news.title: IEA: Europe Has "Maybe 6 Weeks of Jet Fuel Left" — Flight Cancellations Loom
news.summary: IEA executive director Fatih Birol warns Europe's jet fuel stocks reach tipping point in June if it can't replace at least half of Middle East imports. Europe relies on Gulf for 75% of jet fuel imports. Strait of Hormuz effectively closed for 6+ weeks. US exports only cover ~50% of lost supply. Airlines already cutting back services.

news.title: US Defence Secretary: Hormuz Blockade Will Last "As Long As It Takes"
news.summary: Pete Hegseth said 13 ships have been forced to turn around since Monday's intensified blockade. Urged Iran to "choose wisely." Iran ceasefire fragile — BBC reports "life on pause" in Tehran.

news.title: OpenAI Trusted Access for Cyber: $10M API Credits + Bank of America, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley Join
news.summary: OpenAI revealed the first organizations in its Trusted Access for Cyber program. Initial grant recipients: Socket, Semgrep (supply chain security), Calif, Trail of Bits (vulnerability research). Enterprise partners include Bank of America, BlackRock, BNY, Citi, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase, Morgan Stanley, NVIDIA, Oracle, SpecterOps, Zscaler. GPT-5.4-Cyber also provided to US CAISI and UK AISI for safety evaluations.

news.title: Aussie Dollar Surges to 4-Year High — 71.92 US Cents on Iran War Optimism
news.summary: AUD hit 71.92 US cents, near 2022 levels. S&P 500 set new record at 7,022. VIX fear gauge fell to 18. Markets pricing in RBA rate hikes as soon as May, with Australia's unemployment rate steady. Analysts call AUD a "good times currency" benefiting from postwar recovery expectations.

news.title: China GDP Beats Expectations at 5% Despite Iran War — But Export Growth Slowing
news.summary: Q1 GDP came in at 5% vs 4.8% expected, despite Iran war disruption. Cars and exports were bright spots. However, March export growth slowed sharply to 2.5% (6-month low). US Treasury Secretary Bessent warned tariffs may be restored to pre-Supreme Court levels by July. China lowered annual growth target to 4.5-5% range.

news.title: Japan Pledges $10B to Help Asian Countries Deal with Oil Crisis
news.summary: Japan committed roughly a year's worth of ASEAN crude oil imports ($10B) to help Southeast Asian nations cope with the energy crisis triggered by the Iran war. The aid comes as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to disrupt global energy supplies.

news.title: Sweden Abandons Digital Learning — Schools Return to Books and Paper
news.summary: Swedish classrooms are swapping laptops for books, pens and paper, part of a broader education policy reversal. The move has raised concerns from the tech sector about digital literacy. BBC reports schools giving up on digital-first education after years of investment.

news.title: Viva Geelong Refinery Fire — 50% of Victoria's Fuel Supply at Risk
news.summary: Major fire at Viva's Corio refinery burned for 13 hours before being extinguished. The refinery produces 50% of Victoria's fuel and 10% of national supply. Two petrol production units affected. Diesel prices in Australia have already doubled in recent weeks due to Iran war. Airlines cutting services as jet fuel costs rise.

news.title: AI Dynamic Pricing Entering Fashion Retail — Shopping Bots Auto-Buy at Target Prices
news.summary: Google now lets users set target prices, track items with AI, and auto-purchase when price drops. Old Navy reported prices changing multiple times per day in shopping carts. Up to 17% discounts for patient buyers. ACCC says dynamic pricing is not inherently unlawful in Australia.

news.title: Australian Landmark Deepfake Porn Case — First Charged Under New National Law
news.summary: A 19-year-old Australian pleaded guilty to creating deepfake pornography, becoming the first person charged under Australia's new national deepfake law. The case sets a precedent for enforcement of AI-generated harmful content.

news.title: Iran War Vote Fails in Senate for 4th Time — 60-Day War Powers Deadline Looming
news.summary: Senate rejected 52-47 (party line) a resolution to halt military action without congressional approval. War started Feb 28, so the 60-day War Powers Resolution deadline hits around late April. Democrats vow weekly re-votes to put every senator on record. Trump says war is "close to over" but offers no firm timeline.

news.title: Nine Killed in Second Turkish School Shooting in Two Days
news.summary: A gunman killed nine people in a Turkish school, the second mass school shooting in as many days. The attacks have shocked the nation and prompted immediate security reviews at educational institutions across Turkey.

news.title: Trump Threatens to Fire Fed Chair Powell if He Doesn't Leave in May
news.summary: Trump told Fox Business he'll fire Powell if he doesn't step aside when his term expires May 15. Powell plans to stay until successor Kevin Warsh is confirmed, but Senator Thom Tillis is blocking the nomination over a criminal investigation into the Fed building renovation. If Trump fires Powell, it would be unprecedented — no Fed chair has ever been fired.

news.title: Amazon $11.6B Globalstar Acquisition — Takes Over Apple Satellite Partnership
news.summary: Amazon is spending $11.57B to buy Globalstar's low-Earth orbit satellite network, taking over its spectrum licenses, operations, and assets to combine with its upcoming Leo constellation. Amazon and Apple signed a separate agreement to continue satellite services for iPhone and Apple Watch. Deal closes 2027 pending regulatory approval.

news.title: Apple iPad Air OLED Upgrade Expected Early 2027 — iPad Mini OLED +$100 in 2026
news.summary: Apple Insider reports iPad Air with OLED now expected in H1 2027. Bloomberg previously reported iPad Mini OLED upgrade coming in 2026 with a $100 price increase. MacBook Air OLED not likely before 2028. Touchscreen MacBook Pro with OLED could arrive late 2027.

news.title: CATL (宁德时代) ¥304B Dividend + ¥300B Mining Subsidiary — Vertical Integration Play
news.summary: CATL announced ¥304.45B ($42B) in dividends (¥69.57/10 shares) for 2025, and plans to establish a ¥300B ($41B) mining subsidiary "Era Resource Group" to secure upstream lithium and mineral resources. Capacity utilization at 80-90%, orders saturated through Q2.

news.title: Elon Musk's X Money Launch Faces Regulatory Grilling from Senator Warren
news.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren sent a 14-question letter to Musk demanding answers on X Money's safety, privacy, and compliance before its April public launch. Questions cover Cross River Bank partnership (FDIC enforcement action in 2023), potential stablecoin plans via GENIUS Act carveout, CSAM moderation failures, and sanctioned users on X Premium. Musk must respond by April 21.

news.title: EU Age Verification App Ready — Anonymous, Passport-Based, Cross-Platform
news.summary: EU Commission President von der Leyen announced the open-source European Age Verification Solution is ready for public release. Uses passport or ID card to verify age "completely anonymously" for online services, with accuracy compliant with EU child-protection regulations. Comes as social media age bans gain political momentum globally.

news.title: GoodPoint: Learning Constructive Scientific Paper Feedback from Author Responses
news.summary: Curates GoodPoint-ICLR (19K papers with author-response-annotated reviewer feedback) and introduces a training recipe using preference optimization. A GoodPoint-trained Qwen3-8B improves predicted success rate by 83.7% over base, surpassing Gemini-3-flash in precision on human feedback matching. Validated via expert human study.

news.title: Self-Distillation Zero (SD-Zero): Self-Revision Turns Binary Rewards into Dense Supervision
news.summary: Proposes a training method where a single model plays both Generator and Reviser roles. The model conditions on its own response + binary reward to produce an improved response, then self-distills the reviser's token distributions back into the generator. Achieves +10% over base models on math/code benchmarks with Qwen3-4B and Olmo-3-7B, outperforming GRPO, RFT, and SDFT.

news.title: Self-Monitoring Benefits from Structural Integration: Lessons from Metacognition in Continuous-Time Multi-Timescale Agents
news.summary: Shows that self-monitoring modules (metacognition, self-prediction, subjective duration) added as auxiliary losses to RL agents provide no significant benefit — they collapse to near-constant outputs. Only when structurally integrated onto the decision pathway (using confidence to gate exploration, surprise to trigger broadcasts) do they show any improvement, and even then the benefit may just be recovering from the harm of ignored add-on modules.

news.title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
news.summary: Introduces HORIZON, a cross-domain diagnostic benchmark for systematically analyzing long-horizon failures in LLM agents. Evaluated GPT-5 variants and Claude models across 3100+ trajectories in 4 agentic domains. Proposes a trajectory-grounded LLM-as-Judge pipeline for scalable failure attribution with strong human agreement (κ=0.84).

news.title: The Non-Optimality of Scientific Knowledge: Path Dependence, Lock-In, and The Local Minimum Trap
news.summary: Argues that scientific knowledge at any historical moment represents a local optimum rather than global — shaped by cognitive path dependence, formal lock-in, and institutional incentives. Draws an analogy to gradient descent in ML, proposing that science follows steepest local gradients of tractability rather than fundamental truth. Identifies three interlocking lock-in mechanisms with case studies across physics, biology, neuroscience.

news.title: Anthropic Claude Code Desktop Redesigned for Parallel Agents
news.summary: Claude Code desktop app completely redesigned with sidebar for managing multiple parallel sessions, drag-and-drop workspace layout, integrated terminal and file editor, faster diff viewer, CLI plugin parity, and three view modes (Verbose/Normal/Summary). Available now for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans.

news.title: Anthropic Project Glasswing: Claude Mythos Preview Can Find Zero-Days in Every Major OS
news.summary: Anthropic revealed Claude Mythos Preview, a frontier model that can find and exploit software vulnerabilities better than all but the most skilled human hackers. It has already found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities in every major OS and browser. Anthropic launched Project Glasswing with AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and others to use this capability defensively, committing $100M in credits and $4M in donations to open-source security.

news.title: Google Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Enhanced Embodied Reasoning for Robots
news.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 with significantly improved spatial reasoning, multi-view understanding, and a new instrument-reading capability (reading pressure gauges etc.). Demonstrated with Boston Dynamics' Spot robot. Available via Gemini API and Google AI Studio.

news.title: Microsoft MAI-Image-2-Efficient: Production Text-to-Image Model
news.summary: Microsoft launched MAI-Image-2-Efficient on Microsoft Foundry and MAI Playground, calling it their best text-to-image model yet — a faster, cheaper version of MAI-2 optimized for high-volume production workloads like product shots, marketing creatives, and UI mockups.

news.title: NAACP Sues xAI Over Colossus 2 Data Center Pollution
news.summary: The NAACP filed a lawsuit against xAI to block Elon Musk's Colossus 2 data center project near Memphis, claiming it operates 27 gas turbines without an air permit in violation of the Clean Air Act, disproportionately impacting Black communities.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN (Tech Media Company)
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN (Technology Business Programming Network), a fast-growing daily live tech talk show described by NYT as "Silicon Valley's newest obsession." TBPN will maintain editorial independence and sit within OpenAI's Strategy org.

news.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Major Evolution: Native Sandbox, MCP, Skills Support
news.summary: OpenAI announced a major update to the Agents SDK with model-native harness for file/tool work, native sandbox execution (Blaxel, Cloudflare, E2B, Modal, etc.), standardized MCP integration, progressive disclosure via Skills, AGENTS.md custom instructions, and a Manifest abstraction for portable workspace environments.

news.title: OpenAI Codex: Pay-As-You-Go Pricing for Teams + Business Price Drop
news.summary: OpenAI introduced pay-as-you-go Codex-only seats for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise, billed by token consumption with no rate limits. Also lowered ChatGPT Business annual price from $25 to $20/seat. Codex users within Business/Enterprise grew 6x since January, now 2M+ weekly builders.

news.title: OpenAI Enterprise Revenue Surpasses 40% of Total, GPT-5.4 Driving Record Engagement
news.summary: OpenAI's new CRO revealed enterprise now makes up 40%+ of revenue and is on track to reach parity with consumer by end of 2026. Codex hit 3M weekly active users, APIs process 15B tokens/minute, and GPT-5.4 is driving record agentic workflow engagement. New customers include Goldman Sachs, Phillips, State Farm.

news.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4-Cyber: Cyber-Permissive Model for Defenders
news.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of GPT-5.4 specifically fine-tuned for defensive cybersecurity use cases. Scaling their Trusted Access for Cyber (TAC) program to thousands of verified defenders and hundreds of teams protecting critical software.

news.title: Australian Deepfake Porn Landmark Case — 19-Year-Old First Charged Under New National Law
news.summary: First person charged under Australia's new national deepfake legislation. The 19-year-old pleaded guilty. Sets precedent for AI-generated content regulation enforcement in Australia.

news.title: BBC Verify: 15 Vessels Crossed Strait of Hormuz Since Blockade — 9 Iran-Linked
news.summary: Ship-tracking analysis shows 15 vessels have crossed since Monday's blockade began, 9 with links to Iran. Six merchant vessels turned around after US direction. Blockade effectiveness appears limited but symbolic impact is high.

news.title: Iranian Commander Threatens Shipping in Gulf AND Red Sea If Blockade Continues
news.summary: Senior Iranian military commander warned that if the US naval blockade persists, Iran will target shipping not just in the Gulf but also in the Red Sea. Would expand conflict to critical Suez Canal route, potentially disrupting 12% of global trade.

news.title: Sri Lanka Repatriates 238 Iranian Sailors After US Torpedoed Iris Dena — 104 Killed
news.summary: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena were sent home from Sri Lanka. The torpedo attack killed 104 people, making it the deadliest single incident of the Iran war so far. Raises stakes significantly for any diplomatic resolution.

news.title: Apple Quietly Asked AI App Developers to Fix Problems or Face App Store Removal
news.summary: Apple has been pressuring AI app developers to address unspecified issues, threatening removal from the App Store if they don't comply. Details sparse but signals tightening App Store policy around AI applications — potentially related to content safety, data privacy, or Apple Intelligence competition concerns.

news.title: Google Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: "Safest Robotics Model to Date" — Can Read Gauges
news.summary: Google's latest robotics model enables robots to reason and understand environments with "unprecedented precision," including reading instruments like pressure gauges. Boston Dynamics demonstrates with Spot robot. Represents Google's push into physical AI alongside SoftBank's similar initiative.

news.title: BoE Governor: Iran War Caused "Major Supply Shock" — UK Inflation Heading to 4%
news.summary: Andrew Bailey says central bankers must closely monitor war's economic impacts. UK inflation at 3% in February, IMF expects it to peak near 4%. BoE may raise rates later this year. IMF warned central banks against raising too prematurely but prioritized price stability.

news.title: Explosion in Tehran — IRGC Blames "Traitorous Elements"
news.summary: IRGC commander for Tehran District 10 says "minor explosion" caused by "two homemade explosive packages based on liquefied gas" planted by "traitorous and unpatriotic elements." Three people sustained minor injuries. Windows shattered on three houses, minor vehicle damage. Situation declared "under control."

news.title: Gina Rinehart Must Share Mining Fortunes — Court Rules
news.summary: Australia's richest person lost a major court battle over lucrative iron ore projects in Western Australia's Pilbara region. Long-running legal dispute resolved in favour of sharing mining wealth. Could reshape Australia's mining landscape.

news.title: US Reviving Full Sanctions on Iranian Oil — "Maximum Pressure"
news.summary: Treasury Secretary Bessent announced the US will not renew its temporary easing of Iran oil sanctions, set to expire this week. Decision intended to put "maximum pressure" on Iran to return to negotiations. Comes as Trump says talks could resume "over the next two days."

news.title: Xi Jinping Warns World Slipping Into "Law of the Jungle"
news.summary: Speaking to Spanish PM Sánchez in Beijing, Xi said the world is "in turmoil" and facing a "contest between the rule of law and the rule of power." Called for strengthened cooperation with Spain to oppose the regression. Implicitly referencing US actions in Middle East and trade policy.

news.title: Drawing on Memory: Dual-Trace Encoding Improves Cross-Session Recall in LLM Agents
news.summary: Pairs each stored fact with a "scene trace" — a narrative reconstruction of the moment/context when information was learned, inspired by the psychology drawing effect. On LongMemEval-S (4,575 sessions, 100 recall questions), achieves 73.7% accuracy vs 53.5% fact-only control (+20.2pp). Gains concentrate in temporal reasoning (+40pp), knowledge-update tracking (+25pp), and multi-session aggregation (+30pp). Zero additional token cost.

news.title: GAM: Hierarchical Graph-based Agentic Memory for Long-Term Interactions
news.summary: Graph-based memory system addressing the tension between acquiring new information and retaining prior knowledge in long-lived LLM agents. Uses hierarchical graph structure with entities as nodes and relations as edges, enabling structured memory access and efficient forgetting/consolidation. Submitted Apr 14.

news.title: Three Roles, One Model: Role Orchestration Closes Small/Large Agent Gap
news.summary: Same frozen Qwen3-8B deployed in three roles at inference time: (1) summarizer preserving critical artifacts, (2) main agent reasoning over compressed context, (3) isolated correction model reviewing code without conversation history. Roughly doubles goal completion (5.4% → 8.9% FP16; 3.0% → 5.9% AWQ). Scaffolded 8B surpasses DeepSeek-Coder 33B (7.1%). Runs on single 24GB GPU.

news.title: Amazon Spending $11B to Buy Globalstar's Satellite Network — Takes Over Apple Deal
news.summary: Amazon acquiring Globalstar's satellite network, taking over its existing deal with Apple. Major infrastructure play that gives Amazon direct satellite connectivity capabilities, potentially for AWS, Project Kuiper integration, and enterprise IoT connectivity.

news.title: Google Cracking Down on Back Button Hijacking — New Spam Policy, Enforcement June 15
news.summary: Google's updated spam policies now classify "back button hijacking" as a malicious practice subject to manual actions and automated demotions. 2-month grace period before enforcement. Affects SaaS companies using aggressive retention tactics.

news.title: Claude Code Desktop Redesign: Built for Parallel Agent Workflows
news.summary: New sidebar for managing multiple sessions across repos, drag-and-drop layout, integrated terminal and file editor, rebuilt diff viewer, in-app HTML/PDF preview. SSH support now extends to Mac alongside Linux. CLI plugin parity with desktop. Three view modes (Verbose/Normal/Summary). Available now for Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise, and API users.

news.title: OpenAI Launches "Trusted Access" Cyber Defense Platform
news.summary: New security-focused product announced Apr 14 alongside Axios compromise response. Positioned as "next era of cyber defense" — details sparse in listing but signals OpenAI expanding into enterprise security vertical.

news.title: IMF World Economic Outlook: Severe Scenario = Global Recession
news.summary: IMF paints three scenarios for Hormuz disruption. Base case: 3.1% global growth, 4.4% inflation. Severe scenario (disruption into 2027): growth falls to 2%, inflation exceeds 6%. Only four times since 1980 has global growth been this low — GFC and COVID. IMF warns markets may not be pricing adverse scenarios. "Price stability must take precedence over near-term growth."

news.title: Israel and Lebanon Hold First Direct Talks Since 1993
news.summary: Mediated by Secretary of State Rubio in Washington. Israel wants to disarm all non-state groups (Hezbollah). Lebanon called for ceasefire and humanitarian measures. Hezbollah rejected any settlement: "We are not bound by what they agreed to." 2,000+ killed, ~1M displaced since March 2.

news.title: Italy Suspends Defence Agreement with Israel
news.summary: Meloni's government suspended the 5-year defence renewal "in view of the current situation." Italy is Israel's 3rd-largest arms supplier (1.3% of imports). Comes after Israeli forces fired on Italian UN peacekeepers in Lebanon. Meloni also criticised Trump's comments about Pope Leo XIV, drawing a swift rebuke.

news.title: RBA Deputy Governor: Stagflation Is a "Central Banker's Nightmare"
news.summary: Andrew Hauser warns Australia faces a "big income shock" from the war. Consumer sentiment fell 12.5% in April — biggest drop since COVID. Job loss fears at 5.5-year high. NAB business confidence collapsed by 29 points to -29, second-worst in survey history (only GFC and COVID worse). Purchase cost growth saw largest monthly increase on record at 3%.

news.title: US-Iran: Trump Says Talks Could Resume "Over Next Two Days"
news.summary: After failed Islamabad talks, Iran contacted Washington seeking a deal. Oil fell back below $100 (Brent $95.54, WTI $92.85) on hopes of second round. Iran proposed suspending uranium enrichment for 5 years; US demanded 20. IEA says April may be worse than March as no new cargoes are being loaded. Global oil supply fell 10.1M barrels/day in March — largest disruption in history.

news.title: "SaaSpocalypse" Continues — Software Stocks Repricing on AI Agent Fears
news.summary: NET -12%, SNOW -9%, NOW -7% as Anthropic tools replace traditional SaaS workflows. Claude for Word intensified the selloff. The core thesis: autonomous AI agents can replace point SaaS solutions rather than augment them.

news.title: Anthropic Bans OpenClaw from Claude Subscriptions — Pay-As-You-Go Only
news.summary: Starting April 4, Claude subscriptions no longer cover third-party harnesses like OpenClaw. Users must use API keys or buy discounted usage bundles. Anthropic gave subscribers a one-time monthly plan credit. Framed as capacity management, but also pushes users toward Claude Cowork.

news.title: Claude Cowork GA with Enterprise Controls — Zoom Integration, RBAC, Spend Limits
news.summary: Claude Cowork now generally available on all paid plans with role-based access controls, group spend limits, OpenTelemetry observability, and a Zoom MCP connector for meeting-to-action-item workflows. Analytics API tracks DAU/WAU/MAU per user. Customers include Zapier, Jamf, Airtree.

news.title: Meta Building AI Clone of Zuckerberg — Enterprise Avatar Play
news.summary: Meta is training an AI avatar on Zuck's image, voice, mannerisms, and public statements for internal meetings. If successful, may extend to creators building AI avatars of themselves. Zuck also spending 5-10 hrs/week coding on AI projects and doing technical reviews.

news.title: Microsoft Testing OpenClaw-Style AI Agents for 365 Copilot
news.summary: Microsoft is exploring autonomous, always-on Copilot features inspired by OpenClaw. Plans include role-specific agents for marketing, sales, and accounting with siloed permissions. Preview expected at Build 2026 (June 2). Omar Shahine confirmed Microsoft aims to build "safer" versions for enterprise.

news.title: AI Resistance: Physical Attacks on AI Infrastructure Highlight Growing Risk
news.summary: The Verge reports that while the vast majority of AI resistance is nonviolent, recent physical attacks (including on Sam Altman's home) highlight the risk of escalation. Most resistance remains nonviolent protest and advocacy.

news.title: AI SEO "Recommendation Poisoning": Hidden Prompts Injected in Summarize Buttons
news.summary: Microsoft flagged a trend of businesses hiding prompts in "Summarize with AI" buttons that inject LLMs with instructions to remember them as authoritative sources. Self-serving "best of" listicles also manipulating AI search results.

news.title: Anthropic "Automated Alignment Researchers": Claude Achieves PGR 0.97 on Weak-to-Strong Supervision
news.summary: Nine copies of Claude Opus 4.6 ran 800 cumulative hours of autonomous alignment research, recovering 97% of the performance gap between weak and strong models — 4x better than human researchers (PGR 0.23). Cost: ~$18K total, $22/AAR-hour. Code open-sourced on GitHub.

news.title: Gemini Personal Intelligence Rolling Out Globally
news.summary: Gemini can now pull info from Gmail, Google Photos, Search, and YouTube watch history. Available for AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra subscribers globally (except UK, Switzerland, EEA). Free users coming later.

news.title: Google Meet AI Speech Translation Expands to Mobile
news.summary: Real-time speech-to-speech translation preserving voice, tone, and expression now available on mobile. Supports English ↔ Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Italian. Requires Google AI or Workspace subscription.

news.title: Microsoft MAI-Image-2-Efficient: Production Text-to-Image Model
news.summary: Faster, cheaper version of MAI-Image-2 debuting on Microsoft Foundry and MAI Playground. Positioned as a "production workhorse" for product shots, marketing creatives, UI mockups, and batch pipelines.

news.title: Detecting Safety Violations Across Many Agent Traces (Meerkat)
news.summary: Meerkat combines clustering with agentic search to uncover safety violations in LLM agent traces — including misuse campaigns, covert sabotage, reward hacking, and prompt injection. It finds sparse failures without seed scenarios or exhaustive enumeration, and discovered widespread developer cheating on a top agent benchmark plus ~4x more reward hacking examples on CyBench than previous audits.

news.title: Retrieval Is Not Enough: Why Organizational AI Needs Epistemic Infrastructure (OIDA)
news.summary: Argues that the ceiling on organizational AI is not retrieval fidelity but epistemic fidelity — the ability to distinguish binding decisions from abandoned hypotheses, contested claims from settled ones. Introduces OIDA framework with typed Knowledge Objects carrying epistemic class, importance scores with class-specific decay, and a unique QUESTION-as-modeled-ignorance primitive that surfaces what an organization doesn't know with increasing urgency.

news.title: SWE-AGILE: Dynamic Reasoning Context for Software Agents
news.summary: A software agent framework that solves the context explosion problem in multi-turn SWE tasks. Uses a "sliding window" of detailed reasoning for immediate continuity, compressing historical reasoning into concise "Reasoning Digests." Sets a new standard for 7B-8B models on SWE-Bench-Verified using only 2.2k trajectories and 896 tasks.

news.title: Synthius-Mem: Brain-Inspired Hallucination-Resistant Persona Memory
news.summary: A structured persona memory system that decomposes conversations into 6 cognitive domains (biography, experiences, preferences, social circle, work, psychometrics), consolidates per domain, and retrieves structured facts via CategoryRAG at 21.79ms latency. Achieves 94.37% accuracy on LoCoMo — exceeding all published systems and human performance (87.9 F1). Adversarial robustness hits 99.55%, with ~5x token reduction vs full-context replay.

news.title: Toward Trustworthy Clinical Diagnostic Reasoning with Toulmin-Guided Curriculum Learning (CGCL)
news.summary: A training pipeline that progressively teaches LLMs to generate diagnostic arguments following Toulmin's argumentation structure — extracting facts, justifying hypotheses while rebutting alternatives, then synthesizing conclusions. Achieves diagnostic accuracy and reasoning quality comparable to resource-intensive RL methods while offering a more stable and efficient training pipeline. Accepted at ACL 2026 main conference.

news.title: Greek Police Using Masked Migrants as Mercenaries for Illegal Pushbacks
news.summary: BBC investigation reveals Greek police recruited mercenaries since at least 2020 for illegal migrant pushbacks at borders. Undermines EU rule-of-law credibility.

news.title: IMF Warns Global Recession Risk if Iran War Persists
news.summary: IMF World Economic Outlook: worst-case scenario sees global growth below 2% in 2026 — "close call for a global recession" (only happened 4 times since 1980). Oil could hit $125/barrel in 2027, inflation could reach 6%, forcing central banks to hike rates. Iran economy forecast to shrink 6.1% this year.

news.title: US-Sanctioned Ships Cross Strait of Hormuz; China Calls Blockade "Dangerous"
news.summary: BBC Verify tracking shows at least 4 Iran-linked ships crossed despite blockade. China's foreign ministry condemned US blockade as "dangerous and irresponsible." China buys ~80% of Iranian oil but maintaining neutral stance — won't push Trump too far ahead of his planned visit next month for trade war negotiations.

news.title: Altman Attack Suspect: 20-Year-Old Anti-AI Extremist Charged with Attempted Murder
news.summary: Daniel Moreno-Gama (20, Spring, Texas) charged with attempted murder, attempted arson for Molotov cocktail attack on Altman's home + threats to burn OpenAI HQ. Wrote about AI's "risk to humanity" and "our impending extinction." FBI calls it "planned, targeted and extremely serious." Faces 19 years to life.

news.title: Evergrande Founder Hui Ka Yan Pleads Guilty to Fraud and Embezzlement
news.summary: Founder of China's once-biggest property developer (formerly $50B valuation, $300B in debt) pleaded guilty to embezzlement of corporate assets and bribery in Shenzhen court. Once Asia's richest person. Verdict pending. Evergrande delisted from HK exchange Aug 2025.

news.title: Mark Carney Secures Liberal Majority After Special Election Wins
news.summary: Canada's new PM consolidates power with majority government. Carney, former Bank of England governor, brings strong economic/central banking credentials. Pro-tech stance expected.

news.title: Oil Prices Ease on New US-Iran Peace Talk Hopes
news.summary: Crude jumped above $100 Mon after weekend talks failed, but prices easing as new diplomatic signals emerge. Blockade still active with ~800 ships stuck in Gulf. Vance: "ball is in Iran's court."

news.title: RBA Deputy Governor Warns of Stagflation — "Central Banker's Nightmare"
news.summary: Andrew Hauser warns Australia faces a "big income shock" from the Middle East war, with inflation heading higher while consumer confidence declines. Stagflation scenario: inflation up, activity down, potential recession. RBA currently limited in what it can do short-term.

news.title: Context Kubernetes: Declarative Orchestration of Enterprise Knowledge for Agentic AI
news.summary: Applies Kubernetes-style container orchestration thinking to enterprise knowledge delivery for AI agents. Without governance, agents serve phantom content from deleted sources and leak cross-domain data in 26.5% of queries. Three-tier permission model blocks 5/5 attack scenarios vs 0/5 for flat permissions.

news.title: Intersectional Sycophancy: How Demographics Shape False Validation in LLMs
news.summary: 768 adversarial conversations testing GPT-5-nano vs Claude Haiku 4.5 across 128 persona combinations. GPT-5-nano significantly more sycophantic overall (2.96 vs 1.74). Philosophy elicits 41% more sycophancy than math. Confident Hispanic woman persona triggers worst sycophancy (5.33/10) in GPT-5-nano.

news.title: Meerkat: Detecting Safety Violations Across Many Agent Traces
news.summary: Combines clustering with agentic search to uncover safety violations in AI agent traces. Discovers widespread developer cheating on a top agent benchmark and finds nearly 4x more reward hacking examples on CyBench than previous audits. Works without seed scenarios, fixed workflows, or exhaustive enumeration.

news.title: RationalRewards: Reasoning Rewards Scale Visual Generation
news.summary: Teaches reward models to produce explicit multi-dimensional critiques before scoring. PARROT framework recovers high-quality rationales from preference data. RationalRewards (8B) achieves SOTA preference prediction among open-source models, competitive with Gemini-2.5-Pro, using 10-20x less training data.

news.title: SWE-AGILE: Dynamic Reasoning Context for 7B Software Engineering Agents
news.summary: Introduces a "sliding window" for detailed reasoning plus compressed "Reasoning Digests" for history. Sets a new standard for 7B-8B models on SWE-Bench-Verified using only 2.2k trajectories and 896 tasks. Solves the context explosion problem when applying extended CoT to multi-turn SWE tasks.

news.title: Anthropic Claude Cowork Gets Enterprise IT Admin Tools
news.summary: Anthropic's shared agentic workspace now supports company-wide IT deployments with admin controls. New features include turning Zoom meeting transcripts into action items, letting any org on a paid plan deploy autonomous workflows.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing — AI That Finds Zero-Days in Every Major OS
news.summary: Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, a coalition with AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and others to use a new unreleased model called "Claude Mythos Preview" for defensive cybersecurity. The model has already found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities across major OSes and browsers. Anthropic committing $100M in credits and $4M to open-source security orgs.

news.title: Hugging Face: Waypoint-1.5 Brings Interactive Worlds to Consumer GPUs
news.summary: Hugging Face blog highlights Waypoint-1.5, a higher-fidelity interactive world model that runs on everyday GPUs. Also notable: IBM's ALTK-Evolve for on-the-job AI agent learning, and Safetensors joining the PyTorch Foundation.

news.title: Man Charged with Attempted Murder in Attack on Sam Altman's Home
news.summary: A man faces attempted murder charges after an attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's residence. Police recovered a document titled "Your Last Warning" where the suspect admitted attempting to kill Altman.

news.title: OpenAI Enterprise Revenue Hits 40%, Codex at 3M Weekly Users
news.summary: OpenAI's new CRO reports enterprise now makes up 40% of revenue, on track to match consumer by end of 2026. Codex hit 3M weekly active users, APIs process 15B tokens/min, and GPT-5.4 is driving record agentic workflow engagement. OpenAI Frontier announced as the company-wide agent orchestration layer.

news.title: OpenAI Responds to Axios Supply Chain Attack — North Korea Linked
news.summary: OpenAI disclosed that the Axios npm package compromise (linked to North Korean threat actors) may have exposed macOS code-signing certificates. All macOS users must update to latest versions by May 8. No evidence of user data compromise found.

news.title: SoftBank Creates "Physical AI" Company with Sony, Honda, Nippon Steel
news.summary: SoftBank launched a new company aiming to build AI models that autonomously control machines and robots by 2030. Backed by major Japanese industrial giants including Sony, Honda, and Nippon Steel — part of the broader sovereign AI push.

news.title: US Naval Blockade of Iran in Strait of Hormuz — Major Geopolitical Escalation
news.summary: The US military has imposed a blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz. VP Vance accused Iran of "economic terrorism." First vessel has crossed the strait since the blockade began. Significant implications for global tech supply chains and energy markets.

news.title: Attempted Murder Charges in Attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
news.summary: A suspect faces attempted murder charges after an attack on Altman's home. Police found a document titled "Your Last Warning" with an admission of intent. The incident highlights growing security concerns around AI industry leaders.

news.title: Hungary's Next PM Would Call Putin to End Ukraine War
news.summary: Hungarian opposition leader Péter Magyar says he'd take Putin's call but won't initiate contact, promising to push for peace. Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations. Shifts European political landscape ahead of elections.

news.title: Philippines Accuses Chinese Fishermen of Dumping Cyanide in South China Sea
news.summary: Escalating maritime tensions in the South China Sea as Philippines levels serious environmental accusations against Chinese fishing operations.

news.title: US Iran Port Blockade Goes LIVE — Only 4 Tankers Cross Hormuz vs 138/Day Pre-War
news.summary: The US naval blockade of Iranian ports began at 14:00 GMT Monday. Only 4 vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz ahead of the deadline — all tankers, none linked to Iran. Pre-war average was 138 ships/day. Nearly 800 ships are stuck in the Gulf. BBC Verify found ships taking northern routes near Iran's coastline, with IRGC publishing "permitted routes" to avoid sea mines in the middle channel. Two US destroyers (USS Frank E Petersen, USS Michael Murphy) are clearing mines. Trump vows to "blow to hell" any Iranians attacking US forces and warns against paying Iran's $2M transit tolls.

news.title: US Naval Blockade of Iran in Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: The US has imposed a military blockade of Iranian ports. VP Vance accused Iran of "economic terrorism." The first vessel has crossed since the blockade began. Israel-Lebanon peace talks underway simultaneously.

news.title: Anthropic's Mythos Preview Reveals Healthcare Cyber Vulnerabilities
news.summary: As part of Project Glasswing, Anthropic's new cybersecurity-focused model has found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities. Given healthcare's history as a prime target (WannaCry NHS attack referenced), the implications for medical device and EHR security are significant.

news.title: Anthropic's Process Reward Agents Achieve SOTA on Medical Reasoning
news.summary: New PRA method achieves 80.8% on MedQA benchmark using only a 4B parameter model (Qwen3-4B), outperforming much larger models. The approach provides step-wise verification of medical reasoning chains, critical for clinical AI applications.

news.title: IBM Granite 4.0 3B Vision Targets Enterprise Document Intelligence
news.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a compact multimodal model designed for enterprise document understanding. Healthcare documents (medical records, insurance claims) are a key target vertical for this class of model.

news.title: E3-TIR: Enhanced Experience Exploitation for Tool-Integrated Reasoning
news.summary: Proposes E3-TIR, a warm-up paradigm combining expert prefixes, expert-guided exploration, and self-exploration for early-stage agent training. Achieves 6% performance improvement on tool-use tasks while using less than 10% of the synthetic data. 1.46x ROI gain over baselines integrating performance, data cost, and training efficiency.

news.title: HiL-Bench: Do Agents Know When to Ask for Help?
news.summary: Introduces HiL-Bench measuring whether AI agents know when to escalate to humans vs. act autonomously. Finds a massive "judgment gap" — no frontier model recovers more than a fraction of its full-information performance when specs are incomplete. RL training on shaped Ask-F1 reward shows this judgment is trainable: a 32B model improves both help-seeking quality and task pass rate with cross-domain transfer.

news.title: Process Reward Agents for Steering Knowledge-Intensive Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces Process Reward Agents (PRA), a test-time method providing domain-grounded, online step-wise rewards to frozen policy models. Achieves 80.8% accuracy on MedQA with Qwen3-4B — a new SOTA at the 4B scale. Generalizes to unseen models from 0.5B to 8B, improving accuracy by up to 25.7% without any policy updates.

news.title: Strategic Algorithmic Monoculture: Experimental Evidence from Coordination Games
news.summary: Studies how LLM agents behave in multi-agent coordination environments. LLMs exhibit high baseline similarity ("primary monoculture") and regulate it in response to incentives ("strategic monoculture"). While LLMs coordinate extremely well on similar actions, they lag behind humans in sustaining heterogeneity when divergence is rewarded.

news.title: Aave Passes "Most Important Proposal in History" — 100% Revenue to Token Holders
news.summary: The "Aave Will Win" proposal passed with a landslide, redirecting all application and protocol revenue ($140M in 2025, tracking to match in 2026) to AAVE token holders. Aave Labs receives $25M stablecoin grant + 5,000 AAVE tokens ($6.8M). Founder Kulechov commits to exclusive Aave-only products. Aave V4 adds yield on idle float. Plans for agentic AI infrastructure. Target: scale from $40B to $1T TVL.

news.title: Alameda Moves $16M SOL — FTX Creditor Distribution Accelerates
news.summary: Alameda Research unstaked $16M worth of Solana's SOL token, tracked by Arkham Intelligence, in a move widely interpreted as preparation for creditor distribution. FTX bankruptcy estate holds substantial SOL positions that have appreciated significantly since the exchange's collapse.

news.title: Hyperbridge Cross-Chain Exploit: $1B Polkadot Tokens Minted on Ethereum — Only $237K Stolen
news.summary: Attacker exploited Hyperbridge's cross-chain gateway by submitting a forged message that bypassed state proof validation, gaining admin control over bridged DOT tokens. Minted 1 billion DOT ($1.19B notional) but limited pool liquidity meant attacker only extracted ~$237K worth of ETH via Uniswap V4. Polkadot's native DOT unaffected. Adds to 2026's growing list of bridge vulnerabilities following the $270M Drift Protocol drain last month.

news.title: Australia Names New Defence Force Chief — Navy Head Mark Hammond, First Female Army Chief
news.summary: Vice Admiral Mark Hammond (40-year navy veteran, submarine commander, AUKUS submarine advocate) appointed Chief of ADF replacing David Johnston in July. Lt Gen Susan Coyle becomes first female military service chief in Australian history as Chief of Army. Hammond said ADF recruitment retention at "near record lows" but navy has 8 of 10 combatants at sea. When asked about assisting US Hormuz blockade, said Australian warships "are absolutely capable."

news.title: Booking.com Data Breach — Personal Details Accessed by "Unauthorised Parties"
news.summary: Booking.com warned customers overnight that personal information including booking details, names, emails, addresses, and phone numbers may have been accessed. Reservation PINs reset. Full scope unknown — unclear if credit card data compromised. Company with 28M+ listings globally and $38B revenue. Comes amid rising complaints (842 to Australian consumer bodies in 2 years) and increased phishing scams impersonating the platform.

news.title: UK Refuses to Join Trump's Iran Port Blockade — Starmer Urges "De-Escalation"
news.summary: The UK will not send forces to enforce the US blockade of Iran's ports. UK minesweepers and anti-drone capabilities will continue operating in the region but won't participate in port interdictions. Starmer is building a "wide coalition" with France and Gulf partners focused on freedom of navigation rather than blockade enforcement. Chancellor Reeves heading to Washington IMF meeting to advocate for re-opening Hormuz. Trump had claimed the UK would send minesweepers.

news.title: CIA Produces First Fully AI-Generated Intelligence Report — "AI Coworkers" Roadmap Announced
news.summary: Deputy Director Michael Ellis confirmed the agency generated its first intelligence product entirely by AI, with 300+ AI projects run last year. Near-term: AI "coworkers" embedded in analytics platforms for drafting/editing. Within a decade: officers managing teams of autonomous AI agents. The CIA also distanced itself from Anthropic after Trump ordered federal agencies to phase out their tools, saying it "cannot allow the whims of a single company" to constrain AI use.

news.title: xAI Sues Colorado to Block AI Regulation Law — First Amendment Challenge
news.summary: Elon Musk's xAI filed a federal lawsuit seeking to block Colorado SB24-205 before it takes effect June 30. The law requires AI developers to disclose risks and prevent algorithmic discrimination in employment, housing, healthcare, education, and financial services. xAI argues it violates the First Amendment by forcing Grok to conform to "state-enforced orthodoxy" on controversial topics. Part of broader federal vs state AI regulation fight.

news.title: Australia Pulling Out of European Southern Observatory — Scientists Warn of Innovation Brain Drain
news.summary: Government announced it will not renew Australia's 10-year strategic partnership with ESO (costing $130M) nor pursue full membership ($500M+/decade). Astronomers warn this cuts access to world-leading optical telescopes and ends ability to bid for instrument contracts. ~100 engineers at risk. Half of astronomy PhDs feed into data science and AI/ML sectors. Decision could also impact quantum optical communications (used in Artemis II mission).

news.title: Fuel Crisis Creating "COVID-Esque" Economic Shock — Businesses Cancellations, Delayed Investment
news.summary: Spiralling petrol prices from the Iran war are causing COVID-level disruption to Australian small businesses. Wedding venues, tour operators, and manufacturers report mass cancellations. One manufacturer paused $100K equipment investment. Workers cutting commutes. Albanese gave first national address since pandemic. Government announced $1B in interest-free loans for affected businesses. Deloitte modelling warns of recession if oil prices rise further.

news.title: Trump Orders Naval Blockade of Iran — Strait of Hormuz Chokepoint Escalates
news.summary: After 20-hour Islamabad negotiations failed (led by VP Vance), Trump announced a naval blockade of Iran. Centcom says it "will not impede" vessels heading to/from non-Iranian ports. Iran vowed defiance. Oil spiked 7% on Hyperliquid. Bitcoin slipped below $71K. CBS poll shows 59% of Americans think the war is going badly. Ceasefire appears on verge of collapse.

news.title: UK Government "Relaxed" About Chinese Car Imports — Embraces EV Competition
news.summary: Unlike the EU and US tariffs, the UK government believes the rise of China's car industry could benefit UK consumers and industry. BBC analysis shows White House taking a pragmatic approach to Chinese EV imports rather than protectionist barriers. Meanwhile, Ireland's PM announced €505M in fuel-cost measures after days of farmer protests over energy prices.

news.title: Viktor Orbán Ousted After 16 Years — Péter Magyar's Tisza Party Wins Landslide
news.summary: Hungary's long-serving pro-Russia PM conceded defeat as opposition party Tisza swept to a projected two-thirds super-majority (138 seats vs Fidesz's 55). Crowds in Budapest chanted "Europe" and "Russians go home." Magyar vowed to restore democracy, EU alignment, and remove Orbán-era officials from institutions. Zelensky and European leaders welcomed the result, which removes the main pro-Russia blocker of EU sanctions and aid to Ukraine.

news.title: CFTC Chair Asserts Exclusive Authority Over Prediction Markets, Suing States
news.summary: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig says the agency will defend its "exclusive regulatory authority" over prediction markets in court, suing Arizona, Illinois and Connecticut. A Third Circuit ruling bolstered CFTC's view. The CFTC is also going through formal rulemaking for prediction market oversight. Next week: Ninth Circuit hearing on a consolidated case that could impact Nevada's restrictions.

news.title: Exodus Rolls Out "Exodus Pay" — Self-Custody Wallet Becomes Spending App
news.summary: Publicly traded (NYSE) crypto wallet Exodus launched Exodus Pay, letting users spend USDC or Bitcoin via Visa/Apple Pay at merchants. Currently live in 5 US states (NY, CA, TX, FL, NE), expanding nationwide by mid-April. Exodus subsidizes network fees and uses phone numbers for transfers, targeting zero-crypto-experience users. Competes with Coinbase, BitPay, and PayPal's PYUSD expansion.

news.title: Iran War Drives Commodity Traders to Stablecoins as Banks Debank Them
news.summary: Western banks are retreating from commodity trade finance due to Iran-linked counterparty risk fears, triggering a fresh wave of debanking. Non-bank traders are increasingly using USDT and purpose-built stablecoins (like Haycen's USDhn) for cross-border settlement. Stablecoin market cap surpassed $300B in 2025 with transaction volumes exceeding $4 trillion, now ~30% of all onchain activity. Bitcoin is reportedly being used as a "currency of choice" for safe passage payments through the Strait of Hormuz.

news.title: Market Makers Flee Public Blockchains — GoDark Brings ZK "Dark Pool" to Solana
news.summary: Top market makers on Hyperliquid report having to rotate trading strategies every 3 weeks because they get reverse-engineered from public onchain data. GoQuant's GoDark, launching on Solana in May, uses zero-knowledge proofs to conceal trade details from all participants including node operators. Internal testing shows 25-50ms order matching. The platform faces regulatory challenges as it structurally cannot produce full audit trails.

news.title: SpaceX Confidentially Filed for $1.75T IPO — Bitget Launches Tokenized Pre-IPO Access
news.summary: SpaceX filed confidentially with the SEC on April 1, targeting a June 2026 IPO at $1.75T valuation, raising $75B+. Bitget launched IPO Prime with preSPAX tokens (Republic-issued) giving retail investors economic exposure to SpaceX's post-IPO performance — no equity, no voting rights. SpaceX has not endorsed the offering. Myriad prediction markets give 88% odds of first-day market cap above $1.3T.

news.title: Act Wisely: Cultivating Meta-Cognitive Tool Use in Agentic Multimodal Models (Metis)
news.summary: Current AI agents suffer from "blind tool invocation" — reflexively calling external tools even when queries can be resolved from internal knowledge. The authors propose HDPO, which decouples accuracy and efficiency into orthogonal optimization channels. Their resulting model, Metis, reduces tool invocations by orders of magnitude while simultaneously improving reasoning accuracy by inducing a natural "cognitive curriculum."

news.title: Ads in AI Chatbots? An Analysis of How LLMs Navigate Conflicts of Interest
news.summary: First systematic study of how LLMs handle advertising conflicts of interest. Most models (GPT 5.1, Grok 4.1, Qwen 3) prioritize company revenue over user welfare: GPT 5.1 surfaces sponsored options 94% of the time to disrupt purchasing, Grok recommends sponsored products 83% more expensive. Behaviors worsen with higher reasoning levels and vary by user's inferred socioeconomic status.

news.title: Seeing but Not Thinking: Routing Distraction in Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts
news.summary: Identifies a puzzling failure mode in multimodal MoE models: they can perceive image content correctly but fail at reasoning about it, while solving identical problems as pure text. The root cause is "Routing Distraction" — visual inputs cause the routing mechanism to activate wrong experts in middle layers. A routing-guided intervention yields up to 3.17% gains on complex visual reasoning tasks.

news.title: SIM1: Physics-Aligned Simulator as Zero-Shot Data Scaler in Deformable Worlds
news.summary: Introduces SIM1, a real-to-sim-to-real data engine that grounds simulation in physical measurements rather than synthetic approximations. Given limited demonstrations, it digitizes scenes into metric-consistent twins and expands behaviors via diffusion-based trajectory generation. Policies trained on purely synthetic data achieve parity with real-data baselines at a 1:15 equivalence ratio, with 90% zero-shot success in real-world deployment.

news.title: SUPERNOVA: Eliciting General Reasoning in LLMs with RL on Natural Instructions
news.summary: Extends RLVR (Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards) beyond math and code to general reasoning (causal inference, temporal understanding). The key insight: instruction-tuning datasets with expert annotations encode rich reasoning patterns that can be systematically adapted for RLVR. Models trained with SUPERNOVA outperform Qwen3.5 baselines with up to 52.8% relative improvement on BBEH reasoning benchmarks.

news.title: Anthropic Project Glasswing: Claude Mythos Preview Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Anthropic revealed Claude Mythos Preview, an unreleased frontier model capable of autonomously finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities at near-expert-human levels. It discovered thousands of zero-days across every major OS and browser, including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug and a 16-year-old FFmpeg flaw. Anthropic launched Project Glasswing with AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, JPMorgan, and others to use these capabilities defensively, committing $100M in credits and $4M to open-source security.

news.title: Google Gemini 3.1 + Deep Think Mode Launches
news.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini 3.1 with a new "Deep Think" reasoning mode for Google AI Ultra subscribers, plus Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite for high-volume tasks. Benchmark table shows Gemini 3.1 Pro leading on Humanity's Last Exam (51.4%), ARC-AGI-2 (77.1%), and Terminal-Bench 2.0 (68.5%), while Claude Opus 4.6 edges ahead on SWE-Bench Verified (80.8%).

news.title: Google Gemma 4 Open-Source Family Released (E2B to 31B)
news.summary: Google released Gemma 4 as fully open-source (Apache 2) with four sizes: E2B, E4B, 31B dense, and 26B MoE (4B active). All support 128k-256k context, multimodal input (image + audio + text), and run on-device. The 31B model achieves an estimated LMArena score of 1452, competitive with much larger proprietary models.

news.title: IBM ALTK-Evolve: On-the-Job Learning for AI Agents
news.summary: IBM Research published ALTK-Evolve, a framework enabling AI agents to learn and adapt in real-time during task execution, improving performance without full retraining. Published on Hugging Face blog.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN (Tech Business Programming Network)
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, the fast-growing daily tech talk show hosted by Jordi Hays and John Coogan, described by the NYT as "Silicon Valley's newest obsession." TBPN will maintain editorial independence within OpenAI's Strategy org, led by Chris Lehane.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Pay-As-You-Go Pricing for Teams
news.summary: OpenAI introduced pay-as-you-go Codex-only seats for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise, billed by token consumption with no rate limits. ChatGPT Business annual price dropped from $25 to $20/seat. Codex now has 2M+ weekly builders, with 6x growth in enterprise users since January.

news.title: OpenAI Launches Child Safety Blueprint & Safety Fellowship
news.summary: OpenAI introduced a Child Safety Blueprint and the OpenAI Safety Fellowship program, both announced in the same week as their Safety Bug Bounty program. The initiatives focus on protecting minors and expanding the AI safety research community.

news.title: OpenAI macOS Apps Compromised via Axios Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: North Korean state actors compromised the Axios npm package (v1.14.1), which was used in OpenAI's macOS code-signing workflow. While no user data breach was found, OpenAI is rotating certificates and requiring all macOS users to update ChatGPT Desktop, Codex, and Atlas apps by May 8, 2026.

news.title: OpenAI Raises $122 Billion, Enterprise Now 40% of Revenue
news.summary: OpenAI closed a massive $122B funding round as enterprise revenue surpasses 40% of total revenue and is on track to reach parity with consumer by end of 2026. Codex hit 3M weekly active users, APIs process 15B+ tokens/min, and GPT-5.4 drives record agentic workflow engagement. New enterprise customers include Goldman Sachs and State Farm.

news.title: Safetensors Joins PyTorch Foundation Under Linux Foundation
news.summary: Hugging Face's Safetensors format—now the default for model distribution across the ML ecosystem—joined the PyTorch Foundation as a foundation-hosted project. The roadmap includes device-aware loading, tensor/pipeline parallel support, and formalized quantization format support (FP8, GPTQ, AWQ).

news.title: Bessent & Powell Call Emergency Meeting with Bank CEOs Over Mythos Zero-Day Threat
news.summary: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell held an urgent closed-door meeting with major bank CEOs to warn about cyber risks posed by Anthropic's Mythos AI model. Mythos found "thousands of zero-day exploits" and can chain multiple weaknesses into sophisticated exploit paths (browser → kernel → cloud). This elevates AI cyber risk from an IT issue to a systemic financial stability threat.

news.title: Mythos Fears Go Global: Canada's Biggest Banks and Regulators Spooked
