news.summary: The impact of Anthropic's Mythos model has reached Canada, with executives of Canada's biggest banks and regulators expressing alarm. The Cloud Security Alliance is holding emergency "CISO Huddles" on the "AI vulnerability cataclysm." Security experts warn that Mythos's capabilities will be reverse-engineered into Chinese and Russian open-source models by late 2026.

news.title: Security Analysis: "Why Anthropic's Mythos Is a Systemic Shift for Global Cybersecurity"
news.summary: Dan Lohrmann (former Michigan CISO) calls Mythos a "ChatGPT moment" for cybersecurity. Key implications: vulnerability scanners can't keep up, patch cycles will collapse, enterprise security teams overwhelmed. Anthropic providing $100M in credits to "blue teams" but the capability gap is temporary — adversarial models converging on same capabilities within months. 512,000 lines of Claude code already leaked to Chinese developer forums.

news.title: SoftBank and Major Japanese Firms Set Up New Company for AI Development
news.summary: SoftBank and other major Japanese companies have established a new joint venture for AI development. This follows SoftBank's stated goal of deploying 1 billion AI agents by 2026. Details are still emerging.

news.title: "SaaSpocalypse" Continues: NET -12%, SNOW -9%, NOW -7% as Anthropic Rewrites the Rules
news.summary: Software stocks continue to face heavy selling pressure as Anthropic's AI tools demonstrate the ability to replace traditional SaaS workflows. The market is repricing the entire software sector on AI agent fears. Anthropic triggered the initial trillion-dollar selloff in February; the latest Claude for Word release has intensified concerns. 24/7 Wall St asks: "Are software stocks uninvestable?"

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude for Word — Direct Assault on Microsoft's Core Productivity Suite
news.summary: Anthropic released a Claude add-in for Microsoft Word, enabling AI-powered document editing, drafting, and review directly inside Word. The move expands Claude's presence across the full Microsoft Office Suite. Microsoft stock has slid 22% as Anthropic increasingly threatens its software empire. Claude for Word targets legal review, financial editing, and general document workflows.

news.title: Anthropic Withholds Mythos Model Citing Security Risks
news.summary: Anthropic has decided to withhold the release of its Claude Mythos cybersecurity model despite initial plans. The model, which demonstrated the ability to find zero-days in every major OS and browser, was deemed too dangerous for broad release. Vance and Bessent had questioned tech giants on AI security before the planned Mythos release. The decision reflects Anthropic's cautious approach to powerful dual-use capabilities.

news.title: Intuit and Gusto Both Tout Claude Integrations
news.summary: Both Intuit and Gusto announced Claude AI integrations for their respective platforms, extending Anthropic's reach into financial and HR software. The integrations demonstrate that enterprise adoption of Claude is accelerating beyond tech companies into traditional vertical SaaS.

news.title: Markets Brace for "No Deal" Aftermath — Gold at Record $4,751
news.summary: Gold steadied at $4,751 per ounce as peace talks in Islamabad concluded without agreement. Domestic gold rates in India gained to ₹1.52 lakh/10g. Markets are recalibrating expectations — the lack of a comprehensive deal means the Iran conflict could resume at any time, maintaining the risk premium across energy, shipping, and financial markets.

news.title: Oil Tankers Begin Exiting Strait of Hormuz Amid Fragile Ceasefire
news.summary: Al Jazeera reports that oil tankers have started exiting the Strait of Hormuz, the first sign of traffic resuming since the blockade began. However, traffic remains limited and Trump has criticized Iran for doing a "poor job" of allowing oil through. The physical oil market remains tight despite some resumption. Gold steadies at $4,751/oz amid the uncertain peace process.

news.title: US-Iran Peace Talks END Without Agreement — Vance Leaves Pakistan
news.summary: The historic direct US-Iran peace negotiations in Islamabad have concluded without a deal. JD Vance departed Pakistan, calling it Tehran's "final and best offer." The main sticking point remains the Strait of Hormuz — Iran and the US have "serious disagreement" over control and access. Pakistan says it is "imperative to uphold the ceasefire" despite the lack of agreement. Talks paused for now with disagreements unresolved, though both sides left the door open for future negotiations.

news.title: AI Deep Research Comparison: Claude vs ChatGPT vs Grok
news.summary: New benchmarking from AIMultiple compares deep research capabilities across Claude, ChatGPT, and Grok. Each model shows distinct strengths: Claude excels at long-context analysis and multi-step reasoning, ChatGPT leads in breadth and tool integration, and Grok differentiates with real-time information access. No single model dominates across all research tasks.

news.title: Anthropic IPO: $60B Raise at $380B Valuation — But Internal Drama Clouds Outlook
news.summary: Anthropic is reportedly planning a $60B IPO at a $380B valuation, making it one of the largest tech IPOs in history. However, Fortune reports internal drama at OpenAI may affect broader AI company IPO sentiment. The Anthropic IPO timeline and mechanics are being closely watched as a bellwether for the AI infrastructure sector.

news.title: Anthropic Revenue Surges to $30B+ Run Rate — Explores Custom AI Chips
news.summary: Anthropic's run-rate revenue now exceeds $30B, up from ~$9B at end of 2025 (3.3x in ~3 months). The company is exploring designing its own AI chips, following OpenAI's hardware push. Currently uses Google TPUs and Amazon chips. Designing an advanced AI chip costs ~$500M. Anthropic also signed a long-term deal with Google and Broadcom, building on its $50B commitment to US computing infrastructure.

news.title: CoreWeave Stock Jumps 13% as Anthropic Signs Major Cloud Deal
news.summary: CoreWeave stock surged 13% after signing a significant deal with Anthropic for cloud computing infrastructure. This validates the AI infrastructure build-out thesis and signals Anthropic is diversifying beyond Google Cloud and AWS for compute. CoreWeave's GPU-focused cloud is increasingly becoming the "third leg" of AI compute alongside hyperscalers.

news.title: EU AI Act: Agentic AI Governance Challenges Emerge
news.summary: AI News reports that agentic AI systems are creating new governance challenges under the EU AI Act. The autonomous decision-making capabilities of AI agents fall into regulatory gray zones not anticipated by the original legislation. EU delays in implementation have allowed high-risk systems to dodge oversight. Meanwhile, China's AI regulation debate has entered the "agent era."

news.title: Hackers Actively Targeting Claude Code Users with AMOS Stealer via ClickFix Campaign
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers detected 91,000+ attack sessions targeting AI deployments. A specific campaign uses "EvilTokens" and "ClickFix" techniques to deliver AMOS stealer malware to Claude Code users. Malicious Chrome AI extensions are also attacking 260,000+ users via injected iframes. The rapid weaponization of AI coding tools represents a new threat vector.

news.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark — Proprietary AI Model, First Since Superintelligence Labs Formation
news.summary: Meta unveiled Muse Spark, its first proprietary AI model since reorganizing into Superintelligence Labs. Notably, Meta did NOT open-source this model — a significant strategic shift from the company's Llama-first open approach. The model features a "Contemplating" mode for deeper reasoning. This signals Zuckerberg's pivot toward proprietary models for competitive differentiation while potentially keeping Llama for developer ecosystem lock-in.

news.title: OpenAI Supply Chain Attack via Axios HTTP Library Compromise
news.summary: OpenAI disclosed that the Axios npm package (v1.14.1) was compromised on March 31, affecting macOS code-signing certificates for ChatGPT Desktop, Codex, Codex-cli, and Atlas. A GitHub Actions workflow downloaded and executed the malicious version during app signing. OpenAI has issued new certificates and an update. Axios has ~50M weekly npm downloads — this is a generational supply chain security incident.

news.title: Oil Markets in Whiplash: IEA Warns Crisis Worse Than 1973, 1979, and 2022 Combined
news.summary: The IEA has declared the oil and gas crisis from the Iran war worse than the three previous shocks combined. Physical prices hit $150/barrel before the ceasefire. While prices dropped on ceasefire news, they've since rebounded as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked. The EIA warns fuel prices could keep rising for months even if Hormuz reopens. Goldman Sachs lowered Q2 forecasts on the ceasefire but markets remain volatile. J.P. Morgan analysts warned US gas could breach $5/gallon.

news.title: OpenAI Supply Chain Attack via Axios Compromise
news.summary: OpenAI disclosed that a supply chain attack on the Axios HTTP library (v1.14.1, compromised March 31) potentially compromised macOS code-signing certificates used for ChatGPT Desktop, Codex, Codex-cli, and Atlas. OpenAI has issued new certificates and an update. The attack vector: a GitHub Actions workflow downloaded and executed the malicious Axios version during the macOS app-signing process.

news.title: RBA May Rate Hike Bets Rise to 4.35% as Inflation Stays Sticky
news.summary: Economists are increasingly calling for a May rate hike to 4.35% after the RBA already raised twice in 2026 (to 3.85% in Feb, 4.10% in Mar). Westpac now expects two extra hikes with a 4.85% peak. Treasury tips inflation to hit the "high 4s" by June, driven by the oil shock from the Iran conflict. Australian households face a double whammy of rate hikes and higher petrol prices, with mortgage stress risk rising 1pp in February alone.

news.title: Trump "Quietly Scraps His Own Playbook on China" — WSJ
news.summary: The Wall Street Journal reports the Trump administration is quietly shifting its China strategy, moving away from aggressive tariff escalation. Supreme Court rulings have blunted Trump's tariff weapon, and Beijing has leveraged the opening. Trump-Xi summit plans remain in flux amid the Iran war, with Treasury Secretary Bessent suggesting it could be delayed. Meanwhile, US-China trade talks were held in Paris to clear the path for a potential summit. China's trade surplus hit a record $1.2 trillion in 2025 despite tariffs.

news.title: US-Iran Direct Peace Talks Stretch Past Midnight in Islamabad
news.summary: The first-ever direct US-Iran peace negotiations are underway in Islamabad, brokered by Pakistan. JD Vance is leading the US delegation, meeting face-to-face with Iranian officials to turn the fragile ceasefire (now in effect for 43 days of conflict) into a lasting agreement. Talks have stretched past midnight, with Iran demanding a Lebanon ceasefire as a precondition. Meanwhile, two US warships transited the Strait of Hormuz as Trump claims the waterway will reopen "soon."

news.title: Nectir Raises $12.5M Series A for AI Campus Communication & Student Support
news.summary: Nectir, an AI-powered campus communication platform, closed a $12.5M Series A. The platform provides real-time AI-assisted communication between students, faculty, and administrators, aiming to reduce dropout rates and improve student outcomes by proactively identifying at-risk students and facilitating timely interventions.

news.title: OpenAI Partners with Six Indian Institutions to Embed AI in Higher Education
news.summary: OpenAI is partnering with six Indian higher-education institutions — including IIT Delhi, IIM Ahmedabad, and AIIMS New Delhi — to integrate ChatGPT Edu into core academic workflows. The initiative targets 100,000+ students, faculty, and staff in its first year. IIM Ahmedabad and Manipal Academy will also introduce OpenAI-backed certifications. This comes alongside partnerships with Indian edtech platforms Physics Wallah, upGrad, and HCL GUVI for structured AI training courses.

news.title: Preply Raises $150M as Investors Back Human-Led AI Learning at Scale
news.summary: Language learning platform Preply raised $150M, with plans to expand aggressively in Latin America. The company positions itself as "human-led AI learning" — using AI to match tutors, personalize curricula, and scale operations while keeping human tutors at the core of the experience.

news.title: Subject Raises $28M Venture Round for AI-Powered Education Platform
news.summary: Subject, an AI-native education platform, raised a $28M venture round. The company is building tools that leverage generative AI to transform how educational content is created, delivered, and personalized — positioning itself as a next-generation infrastructure layer for schools and institutions.

news.title: Benchmarking AI Models on Real-World Prediction Markets
news.summary: Prediction Arena is the first benchmark that pits frontier AI models against real prediction markets (Kalshi, Polymarket) with actual capital. Six frontier models traded autonomously for 57 days starting with $10K each. The results are humbling: most models lost money on Kalshi (-16% to -30.8%), but there's a striking cross-platform contrast — the same models averaged only -1.1% on Polymarket, with grok-4 achieving a 71.4% settlement win rate and Gemini 3.1 Pro netting +6.02% in just 3 days.

news.title: CLEAR: Context Augmentation from Contrastive Learning of Experience via Agentic Reflection
news.summary: Instead of retrieving past experience via RAG, CLEAR trains a dedicated Context Augmentation Model (CAM) that generates task-specific knowledge through contrastive reflection on past trajectories, then optimizes it with RL. On AppWorld, it boosts task completion from 72.6% to 81.2%; on WebShop, average reward from 0.68 to 0.74.

news.title: Conservation Law Breaking at the Edge of Stability: A Spectral Theory of Non-Convex Neural Network Optimization
news.summary: Provides a rigorous theoretical framework explaining why gradient descent works despite non-convex loss landscapes. Shows that gradient flow on ReLU networks preserves L-1 conservation laws (||W_{l+1}||² - ||W_l||² = const), and derives a closed-form spectral crossover formula for how these laws break under discrete gradient descent. Validated across 23 experiments.

news.title: Munkres' General Topology Autoformalized in Isabelle/HOL
news.summary: Using ChatGPT 5.2 and Claude Opus 4.6 as coding agents, researchers autoformalized all 39 sections of Munkres' General Topology textbook into 85,000+ lines of Isabelle/HOL code in just 24 active days. All 806 theorems are fully proved with zero "sorry" placeholders — including heavy hitters like the Tychonoff theorem and Stone-Čech compactification.

news.title: Rhizome OS-1: Semi-Autonomous Operating System for Small Molecule Drug Discovery
news.summary: A multi-agent drug discovery system where AI agents act as computational chemists, medicinal chemists, and patent agents. Paired with a 246M-parameter GNN (trained on 800M molecules), it generated 2,355–2,876 novel molecules per oncology target (BCL6, EZH2), with 91.9% of scaffolds being structurally novel. Binding predictions achieved ROC AUC of 0.88–0.93.

news.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Officially Begin in Islamabad — Indirect Negotiations
news.summary: Highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979 Islamic Revolution. Talks being carried indirectly through Pakistani mediators. JD Vance in Islamabad; Iran's Parliamentary Speaker Ghalibaf representing Tehran. Zero chance of Trump's "peace deal within 2 weeks" prediction. Israel refuses to discuss Hezbollah ceasefire at parallel Washington talks.

news.title: Artemis II Crew Returns Safely After Historic Lunar Flyby
news.summary: Four astronauts completed "textbook" splashdown in the Pacific after travelling further from Earth than any humans since Apollo 13. NASA hails 10-day mission as triumph. Validates Orion spacecraft for future lunar landing missions.

news.title: UK Shelves Chagos Islands Deal After Trump Opposition
news.summary: UK government paused the handover of Chagos Islands to Mauritius after Trump administration opposed the deal. Officials say not entirely abandoned but "run out of time." Deal would have affected a key US-UK military base on Diego Garcia.

news.title: Australian Economy Faces Inflation Surge; Albanese Secures Singapore Fuel Deals
news.summary: Economists project Australian consumer prices galloping at ~10% annualised pace in June quarter. RBA expected to keep hiking. PM Albanese visited Singapore for fuel supply agreements. Construction sector warns of mass layoffs amid cost pressures.

news.title: India Proposes Sweeping Social Media Regulation for News/Political Content
news.summary: India's IT ministry proposes extending content regulation to influencers, podcasters, and ordinary users sharing news. Platforms must comply with government orders to keep safe harbour protection. X blocked a dozen satirical accounts last month under existing rules. Public feedback deadline: April 14.

news.title: Taiwan Opposition Leader Meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
news.summary: KMT's Cheng Li-wun is the first sitting opposition leader to visit China in a decade. Meeting comes amid elevated cross-strait tensions and US focus on Iran. Signals complex Taiwan-China dynamics beyond the DPP-CCP axis.

news.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Begin in Pakistan; Strait of Hormuz Still Closed
news.summary: Iran delegation arrived in Pakistan for US-mediated talks. EU airports warn of systemic jet fuel shortages within 3 weeks if Hormuz stays closed — 50% of Europe's aviation fuel comes from the Gulf. Jet fuel prices hit all-time high of $1,838/tonne (up from $831 pre-war).

news.title: Borderslands Mobile Launches on iOS — Zynga/Take-Two Push
news.summary: Borderlands Mobile, developed by Take-Two-owned Zynga, just launched on iOS. An FPS with cel-shaded graphics and Claptrap quips. Arrives as Borderlands 4 for Switch 2 is "on pause."

news.title: Professor Layton Coming to PC and PlayStation for the First Time
news.summary: Professor Layton and the New World of Steam will launch on PS5 and Steam alongside Switch/Switch 2 later this year. First time the beloved puzzle franchise leaves Nintendo platforms.

news.title: Roblox Launches "Roblox Plus" Subscription at $4.99/mo, Retiring Premium
news.summary: Roblox Plus launches April 30th at $4.99/month, offering monthly Robux, in-game discounts, and Robux transfers between users. The existing Roblox Premium subscription will stop accepting new sign-ups. A significant pricing strategy shift for the platform's 80M+ daily active users.

news.title: Unity + Meta Extend Multi-Year VR Partnership
news.summary: Unity and Meta extended their partnership. Unity already powers the "majority" of top-selling VR games on Meta's platform. Ensures continued VR ecosystem stability after Unity's runtime fee controversy.

news.title: Anthropic Revenue Surpasses $30B Run-Rate; Multi-GW Google/Broadcom TPU Deal
news.summary: Run-rate from ~$9B (Dec 2025) to $30B+ in 5 months. 1,000+ customers spending $1M+ annually, doubling from 500 in under 2 months. Multi-gigawatt next-gen TPU capacity starting 2027.

news.title: Claude Cowork Now GA with Enterprise Admin Controls
news.summary: Anthropic's agentic workspace gets role-based access controls, group spend limits, usage analytics, and Zoom MCP connector. Auto-converts meeting transcripts into action items. Vast majority of usage from non-engineering teams.

news.title: Gemma 4: Frontier Multimodal On-Device (Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Google DeepMind open models in 4 sizes (2.3B to 31B dense + 26B MoE). Image/text/audio input, 128-256K context. 31B reaches LMArena ~1452 text score. Runs on transformers, llama.cpp, MLX, WebGPU.

news.title: Google Meet AI Speech Translation Now on Mobile
news.summary: Real-time speech translation (English ↔ Spanish/French/German/Portuguese/Italian) comes to mobile. Previously web-only. Available to Google AI and Workspace subscribers.

news.title: Holo3: New SOTA on OSWorld Computer Use Benchmark (78.85%)
news.summary: H Company's model achieves 78.85% on OSWorld-Verified with only 10B active parameters (122B total). Trained via agentic flywheel on synthetic enterprise environments. Apache 2.0 on Hugging Face.

news.title: IBM ALTK-Evolve: On-the-Job Learning for AI Agents
news.summary: New framework enabling AI agents to learn and adapt during task execution rather than only during training. Addresses the gap between static fine-tuning and dynamic real-world environments.

news.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
news.summary: New multimodal embedding and reranking models integrated into Sentence Transformers library. Enables cross-modal retrieval (text-image, text-audio) with production-ready APIs.

news.title: OpenAI Enterprise 40%+ Revenue; GPT-5.4 Drives Record Agentic Adoption
news.summary: Enterprise now 40%+ of revenue, on track for consumer parity by end 2026. Codex 3M weekly active users, APIs 15B tokens/min. New customers: Goldman Sachs, Phillips, State Farm. Strategy: frontier intelligence layer + unified AI superapp.

news.title: OpenAI Releases Child Safety Blueprint for AI-Enabled Exploitation
news.summary: Co-developed with NCMEC and Attorney General Alliance. Three priorities: modernize CSAM laws for AI, improve provider reporting, build safety-by-design into AI systems.

news.title: Project Glasswing: Claude Mythos Finds Zero-Days in Every Major OS/Browser
news.summary: Anthropic reveals Claude Mythos Preview — a frontier model surpassing all but top human researchers at vulnerability discovery. Thousands of high-severity vulns already found. 12 tech giants + JPMorgan unite for defensive use. $100M in credits + $4M to open-source security.

news.title: Safetensors Joins PyTorch Foundation Under Linux Foundation
news.summary: Hugging Face's model serialization format becomes a vendor-neutral foundation project. Zero-copy loading, no code execution. Roadmap: device-aware loading, tensor/pipeline parallel support, FP8 + sub-byte quantization.

news.title: Waypoint-1.5: Real-Time Video World Model on Consumer GPUs
news.summary: Overworld's next world model runs at 720p/60FPS on RTX 3090-5090. 360p tier for gaming laptops. Trained on 100x more data than v1. Interactive generative worlds without datacenter compute.

news.title: CLEAR: Context Augmentation from Contrastive Learning of Experience via Agentic Reflection
news.summary: Instead of retrieving past experience for agents, CLEAR learns to generate task-specific context via a contrastive learning approach. A reflection agent analyzes execution trajectories, trains a context augmentation model with SFT + RL, and produces context tailored to each new task.

news.title: MOSAIC: Scaling-Aware Data Selection for End-to-End Autonomous Driving (CVPR 2026)
news.summary: A general data selection framework that fits neural scaling laws per data domain, then optimizes data mixtures by iteratively adding data from domains that maximize metric improvements. Applied to E2E autonomous driving, MOSAIC outperforms baselines using up to 80% less training data.

news.title: Munkres' General Topology Autoformalized in Isabelle/HOL
news.summary: LLM coding agents (ChatGPT 5.2, Claude Opus 4.6) produced 85,000+ lines of Isabelle/HOL formalizing all 39 sections of Munkres' Topology in just 24 active days. All 806 theorems fully proved with zero gaps — including Tychonoff, Baire Category, and Stone-Čech compactification.

news.title: SOLAR: Subspace-Oriented Latent Adapter Reparameterization
news.summary: A post-training compression framework for PEFT adapters (LoRA, AdaLoRA, etc.) that exploits subspace similarity between foundation models and fine-tuned updates. Expresses each adapter as a linear combination of the model's singular vectors, drastically reducing parameter count while preserving task performance.

news.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 Adds Voice and Head Movement Controls
news.summary: The latest Call of Duty title now supports playing with voice commands or by moving your head, expanding accessibility and immersion options. The feature follows broader industry trends toward multimodal input methods in AAA gaming.

news.title: Framework Announces "Next Gen" Event with Penguin-Themed Teaser and Linux References
news.summary: Framework's playful penguin-themed video announcing a "Next Gen" event is sprinkled with Linux references, strongly hinting at a next-generation Framework laptop with native Linux support. The company has been expanding beyond its modular laptop focus into displays and other hardware categories.

news.title: Sony Metal Gear Solid Movie in Production — Final Destination Directors Attached
news.summary: Sony is developing a Metal Gear Solid film directed by Zach Lipovsky and Adam B. Stein (Final Destination: Bloodlines). Oscar Isaac was previously attached to play Solid Snake but his involvement now appears unlikely. The project signals continued Hollywood investment in gaming IP adaptation.

news.title: Valve May Be Developing "SteamGPT" for Internal Support Operations
news.summary: Steam code screenshots reveal references to "SteamGPT" and "SteamGPTSummary" in Steam's codebase. The tools appear designed for support-related tasks like task management and pulling account details including player trust scores and account age. Currently internal-only, no public release planned.

news.title: India Proposes New Rules Regulating News and Political Posts on Social Media
news.summary: India's government proposed regulations giving it more power over news-related content shared by podcasters and social media influencers. The rules would extend the country's already stringent digital content framework, potentially affecting how AI-generated content and platforms operate in the world's largest democracy.

news.title: Israel-Hezbollah Overnight Strikes; Trump Warns Iran Over Hormuz "Fees"
news.summary: Israel struck Hezbollah rocket launchers in Lebanon overnight while Hezbollah fired rockets at Israeli targets. Netanyahu reiterated "no ceasefire in Lebanon." Separately, Trump accused Iran of doing a "very poor job" managing the Strait of Hormuz and warned against charging fees to passing tankers. Peace talks scheduled in Pakistan this weekend are under tension.

news.title: Russia-Ukraine Agree to Orthodox Easter Truce — Saturday to Sunday
news.summary: Putin announced a truce lasting from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday (April 11-12). The brief pause follows months of heavy fighting. Both sides' ability to maintain even a short ceasefire remains uncertain given recent violations of similar agreements.

news.title: Taiwan KMT Leader Meets Xi Jinping — First Sitting Leader Visit in a Decade
news.summary: Kuomintang leader Cheng Li-wun met Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing, the first sitting KMT leader to visit in 10 years. The meeting comes as Beijing courts Taiwan's opposition ahead of a potential Trump visit, signaling continued cross-strait pressure despite broader geopolitical distractions.

news.title: China Helped Broker Iran Ceasefire via "26 Phone Calls"; Courts Taiwan Opposition
news.summary: China played key diplomatic role in bringing Iran to negotiating table through extensive back-channel communications. Meanwhile, Beijing is courting Taiwan's KMT opposition leader ahead of potential Trump visit, signaling continued pressure on cross-strait dynamics.

news.title: Fragile US-Iran Ceasefire Holds, But Israel Bombs Lebanon — 303+ Killed in Beirut
news.summary: US-Iran ceasefire is technically holding but severely tested. Israel's Netanyahu says "there is no ceasefire in Lebanon" after the deadliest strikes on Beirut since the war began (303+ deaths). Strait of Hormuz remains nearly empty — few vessels crossing despite ceasefire. Trump issuing new threats to Iran.

news.title: NATO Chief Defends Alliance as Trump Threatens Withdrawal; Allies to Secure Hormuz
news.summary: Mark Rutte defended NATO in major DC speech as Trump called it a "paper tiger". Said NATO members and allies including Australia, Japan, South Korea ready to play roles securing Strait of Hormuz. Australia's ADF chief confirmed RAN could deploy a warship if asked.

news.title: Severe Cyclone Maila Approaching Queensland — Expected Landfall Monday/Tuesday
news.summary: Cyclone Maila downgraded to Category 3 but still tracking toward Far North Queensland. Expected to make landfall near Coen/Lockhart River early next week. BOM warns forecasts less certain than previous Cyclone Narelle. Second major cyclone in three weeks for the region.

news.title: Australia EV Sales Double Year-on-Year as Iran War Fuels Petrol Anxiety
news.summary: 15,839 BEVs sold in March (42% MoM increase) while overall car sales dropped 3.3%. EV Google searches tripled Jan→Mar. Polestar Q1 2026 sales nearly doubled YoY, test drives 3x normal. Petrol car sales down 25% vs four years ago.

news.title: Google to Tap Gas Plant for AI Datacenter in Sharp Turn from Climate Goals
news.summary: Google plans to power a Texas AI datacenter from a gas plant emitting 4.5M tons CO2/year — more than all of San Francisco. Represents a stark departure from Google's previous 24/7 carbon-free energy commitments. AI compute demand is overriding climate pledges.

news.title: Iran War Exposes Fossil Fuel Vulnerability — Big Emitters Reap Rewards
news.summary: Strait of Hormuz effective closure causing global energy crisis. Oil and gas prices soaring. Countries considering ramping up coal use. UK and Scotland softening North Sea drilling opposition. Christiana Figueres warns world is "held hostage" by fossil fuel dependence.

news.title: UK Breaks Solar Energy Record Twice in Two Days — 14.4GW Generated
news.summary: Britain set new solar generation records on consecutive days as sunny spring weather combined with expanding solar capacity. The 14.4GW peak demonstrates the UK's rapidly growing renewable baseload capacity even during geopolitical energy uncertainty.

news.title: Blind Refusal: Language Models Refuse to Help Users Evade Unjust, Absurd, and Illegitimate Rules
news.summary: Documents "blind refusal" — safety-trained LLMs refuse 75.4% of requests to help break rules even when those rules are unjust, absurd, or illegitimate. Tested 18 model configs across 7 families (N=14,650). Models recognize normative defeat conditions 57.5% of the time but decline to help anyway — refusal is decoupled from moral reasoning capacity.

news.title: How Much LLM Does a Self-Revising Agent Actually Need?
news.summary: When world modeling, planning, and reflection all live inside a single LLM loop, it's impossible to tell what comes from the model vs. the surrounding structure. This paper systematically varies LLM capability while keeping the agent scaffold fixed to isolate which components truly matter. Challenges the assumption that bigger models always make better agents.

news.title: TalkLoRA: Communication-Aware Mixture of Low-Rank Adaptation for LLMs
news.summary: MoE-augmented LoRA methods assume experts operate independently, leading to unstable routing. TalkLoRA introduces lightweight "Talking Module" for controlled information exchange across expert subspaces before routing. Proves theoretically that expert communication smooths routing dynamics. Consistently outperforms vanilla LoRA and MoELoRA.

news.title: Walk the Talk: Bridging the Reasoning-Action Gap via Multimodal Agentic Policy Optimization
news.summary: MLLMs that "think with images" often produce textually plausible but executably failed plans. This paper proposes Multimodal Agentic Policy Optimization (MAPO) — a process-reward framework that penalizes reasoning-action gaps in multimodal tool-calling agents. Bridges the gap between textual reasoning and visual execution.

news.title: Anthropic loses appeal to pause supply chain risk designation
news.summary: Anthropic's legal challenge against its supply chain risk designation was rejected on appeal, maintaining regulatory scrutiny on the AI company's procurement and infrastructure dependencies.

news.title: Anthropic unveils "Claude Mythos" — AI model that can find zero-days in every major OS/browser
news.summary: Anthropic announced Project Glasswing alongside Claude Mythos Preview, a frontier model capable of surpassing all but the most skilled humans at finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities. It has already found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities. AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, JPMorgan and others are partnering to use it defensively. Anthropic committing $100M in usage credits.

news.title: Bezos' AI lab Project Prometheus poaches xAI cofounder from OpenAI
news.summary: Kyle Kozic, xAI cofounder, joins Jeff Bezos' AI lab Project Prometheus to focus on infrastructure. The lab aims to use AI to improve manufacturing. Part of a broader wave of AI talent reshuffling across the industry.

news.title: Google Finance AI makeover goes global — 100+ countries
news.summary: Google's AI-powered Finance app expands beyond US and India to 100+ countries including Australia, Brazil, Canada, Japan. Features built-in Gemini chatbot, new charting tools, and local language support.

news.title: Google Gemini 3 launches with 3.1 Pro, 3 Flash, and Deep Think reasoning mode
news.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini 3 with state-of-the-art reasoning, improved agentic coding, and advanced multimodal understanding. Gemini 3.1 Deep Think offers specialized deep reasoning for complex technical problems, available to Google AI Ultra subscribers. New "Google Antigravity" agentic dev platform also announced.

news.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN to build AI editorial/communications platform
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, bringing in a team with editorial expertise and audience understanding. The move signals OpenAI's intent to shape the global AI conversation directly rather than relying on traditional media channels.

news.title: OpenAI closes $122B funding round at $852B post-money valuation
news.summary: OpenAI's latest round brings total committed capital to $122B, making it the most valuable private AI company at $852B valuation. The round underscores massive investor confidence in the AI infrastructure play as OpenAI approaches 1B weekly active users.

news.title: OpenAI Codex launches pay-as-you-go pricing for teams, drops Business plan to $20/seat
news.summary: Codex now offers per-seat pay-as-you-go pricing with no rate limits (billed on token consumption). ChatGPT Business annual price dropped from $25 to $20/seat. 2M+ builders use Codex weekly, with 6x growth in Business/Enterprise since January.

news.title: OpenAI enterprise revenue surpasses 40% of total, GPT-5.4 driving agentic adoption
news.summary: OpenAI's new President (90 days in) revealed enterprise now makes up 40%+ of revenue, on track to reach parity with consumer by end of 2026. Codex hit 3M weekly active users, APIs process 15B tokens/min. GPT-5.4 is driving record engagement in agentic workflows. New customers include Goldman Sachs, Phillips, State Farm.

news.title: OpenAI releases Child Safety Blueprint for AI policies
news.summary: OpenAI published a framework developed with NCMEC and the Attorney General Alliance to modernize laws addressing AI-generated CSAM, improve reporting processes, and build systems that interrupt exploitation attempts.

news.title: Google Embraces AI Avatars on YouTube Shorts Following OpenAI Killing Sora
news.summary: Google is rolling out AI-generated avatar features on YouTube Shorts, stepping into the gap left by OpenAI's shutdown of its Sora video generation platform. This positions YouTube as the dominant short-form AI video platform.

news.title: Iran Says Israel's Lebanon Strikes "Blatantly Violate" Ceasefire
news.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister told BBC the US must choose between "war and ceasefire." Israel issued new evacuation warnings for southern Beirut and continued strikes despite the two-week US-Iran truce. Lebanon's health ministry reports 182 killed, 890 wounded from Wednesday's wave alone.

news.title: Petrol and Diesel Prices Rise Again — Motoring Groups Warn No Quick Relief
news.summary: Fuel costs climbing as ceasefire fragility persists. Motoring organisations caution drivers against expecting significant price drops. Oil traders pricing in uncertainty over Hormuz reopening timeline and potential for conflict escalation.

news.title: UK Defence Secretary Reveals Month-Long Russian Sub Operation North of Britain
news.summary: Three Russian submarines monitored "24/7" by UK forces before leaving. Healey tells Putin: "We see you." First public disclosure of sustained Russian submarine presence near British waters since the Iran conflict began.

news.title: MoRight: Motion Control Done Right
news.summary: Unified framework for motion-controlled video generation that decouples camera and object motion via temporal cross-view attention. Decomposes motion into active (user-driven) and passive (consequence) components, enabling both forward reasoning (action→consequence) and inverse reasoning (desired outcome→driving action). SOTA on three benchmarks for generation quality, controllability, and interaction awareness.

news.title: OpenSpatial: A Principled Data Engine for Empowering Spatial Intelligence
news.summary: Open-source data engine for high-quality spatial data generation using 3D bounding boxes as fundamental primitives. Covers five foundational tasks: Spatial Measurement, Spatial Relationship, Camera Perception, Multi-view Consistency, and Scene-Aware Reasoning. Ships OpenSpatial-3M dataset with 3 million high-fidelity samples across diverse tasks.

news.title: Personalized RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models with Human Aligned Personalization
news.summary: New benchmark evaluating reward models' ability to capture individual user preferences rather than generic quality. Constructs chosen/rejected pairs based on strict adherence to user-specific rubrics. Finds that SOTA reward models peak at only 75.94% accuracy on personalization. Crucially, the benchmark shows significantly higher correlation with downstream BoN and PPO performance than existing baselines.

news.title: AI Visibility Crisis: LLMs Citing Niche Publications Over Mainstream
news.summary: Muck Rack analysis of millions of LLM responses shows niche publications frequently cited over mainstream outlets. Jim Cramer surprisingly high. "AI visibility rat race" reshaping how media companies think about SEO and content strategy.

news.title: GoDaddy Integrates Cloudflare AI Crawl Control for Publishers
news.summary: Web hosting platform now lets publishers permit, block, or charge AI crawlers at scale via Cloudflare integration. First major hosting platform to offer this — creates new infrastructure layer between publishers and AI companies.

news.title: Mercor Data Breach: Meta Pauses Work, OpenAI Investigates
news.summary: AI training data company Mercor hit by security breach putting "AI industry secrets at risk." Meta paused all work with the company. OpenAI launching investigation. Mercor is a major player in AI data supply chain.

news.title: Meta AI Superintelligence Push: Will Llama's Successor Justify Billions?
news.summary: After investing billions in superintelligence infrastructure, Meta faces pressure on whether next-gen Llama can compete with frontier proprietary models. Open-source versions coming "eventually" but some pieces staying proprietary for safety reasons.

news.title: OpenAI Enterprise Now 40% of Revenue; Building "Unified AI Superapp"
news.summary: Enterprise revenue crossed 40% and on track for parity with consumer by end of 2026. Codex hit 3M weekly active users, APIs process 15B tokens/min. New strategy: OpenAI Frontier as unified intelligence layer + AI superapp combining ChatGPT, Codex, and agentic browsing.

news.title: OpenAI Releases Child Safety Blueprint for AI-Enabled Exploitation
news.summary: Framework co-developed with NCMEC and Attorney General Alliance. Three pillars: modernize CSAM laws for AI, improve provider reporting, build safety-by-design into AI systems. Signals regulatory direction as governments scramble to address AI-generated CSAM.

news.title: WGA Four-Year Deal: Strengthened AI Protections for Writers
news.summary: Writers Guild of America reached new deal with studios including enhanced AI protections. Follows pattern of creative unions securing guardrails against AI displacement — template for other industry labor negotiations.

news.title: ASX Steady; Fuel Crisis Deepens — More Australians Switch to Bikes
news.summary: Australian markets absorb ongoing Middle East uncertainty. Fuel prices driving modal shift — cycling uptake surging. Transport industry seeking Fair Work Commission relief for drivers. Government in talks to secure alternative shipping routes around Hormuz.

news.title: Greece to Ban Social Media for Under-15s; Starmer Says "Work Remains" on Iran Deal
news.summary: Greece joins growing European trend of youth digital regulation with under-15 social media ban. UK PM Starmer cautioned that significant work remains to make the US-Iran ceasefire hold, signalling European allies want durable solutions.

news.title: Israel's Largest Lebanon Strike Wave — 182 Killed, 890 Wounded
news.summary: Israel conducted its largest airstrike wave of the conflict, hitting 100+ targets across Beirut, southern Lebanon, and Bekaa Valley in 10 minutes. Both US and Israel explicitly excluded Lebanon from the US-Iran ceasefire. 1.2M+ displaced, 1 in 5 of the population.

news.title: JD Vance to Lead US Team to Pakistan for Iran Talks — Trump Threatens NATO Exit
news.summary: Vance, Witkoff, and Kushner will meet Iranian counterparts in Islamabad on April 11. White House declared "victory" but details remain murky. Trump simultaneously escalated NATO tensions, saying the alliance "failed" during the Iran conflict and discussing possible US exit.

news.title: Only 3 Ships Cross Hormuz Since Ceasefire — 800 Stranded
news.summary: Despite the ceasefire, Iran's navy warned ships they would be "destroyed" without permission. Only 3 bulk carriers crossed since Tuesday vs 138/day pre-conflict. Nearly 800 ships remain stuck. Toll payments to Iran could violate US sanctions, creating a legal trap for shipping companies.

news.title: Amazon Cuts USPS Shipping Volume by 20% in New Deal
news.summary: Amazon and USPS reached a tentative deal where Amazon will ship 20% fewer packages through the Postal Service. Earlier reports had suggested Amazon wanted to cut "at least two-thirds."

news.title: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Priced at $899 — Launches April 22
news.summary: AMD's flagship dual-3D-V-Cache processor (16 cores, 208MB total cache) promises 5-10% gains over the 9950X3D in DaVinci Resolve and Blender. The most powerful consumer desktop CPU for creators.

news.title: Google Finance AI Makeover Goes Global — 100+ Countries
news.summary: After US and India launches, Google is expanding its revamped Finance app with built-in Gemini chatbot, new charting tools, and localized news feeds to 100+ countries including Australia, Japan, Brazil, and Mexico.

news.title: Unity Extends Meta VR Headset Partnership
news.summary: Unity will continue supporting Meta's VR headsets with an extended partnership. Unity powers the "majority" of top-selling VR games on Meta's platform, according to Unity's COO.

news.title: WhatsApp Rolls Out Usernames to Limited Users
news.summary: WhatsApp is testing usernames with a small group of users, letting people connect without sharing phone numbers. Available via profile settings for those in the initial rollout.

news.title: DiffHDR: Re-Exposing LDR Videos with Video Diffusion Models
news.summary: Formulates LDR-to-HDR video conversion as a generative radiance inpainting task in video diffusion model latent space. Operates in Log-Gamma color space to leverage spatio-temporal priors from pretrained video diffusion models, synthesizing plausible HDR radiance in over/underexposed regions. Also enables controllable conversion via text prompts or reference images, with a novel pipeline for HDR training data synthesis from static HDRI maps.

news.title: HaloProbe: Bayesian Detection and Mitigation of Object Hallucinations in VLMs
news.summary: Reveals that attention-based hallucination detection is unreliable due to Simpson's paradox — attention trends reverse when statistics are aggregated. HaloProbe factorizes external description statistics from internal decoding signals using a Bayesian framework to estimate per-token hallucination probabilities. The guided decoding reduces hallucinations more effectively than intervention-based methods while preserving output quality.

news.title: In-Place Test-Time Training
news.summary: A drop-in framework that gives any existing LLM the ability to update its weights at inference time, without retraining from scratch. It treats MLP projection matrices as "fast weights" and replaces TTT's generic reconstruction loss with a theoretically-grounded objective aligned with next-token prediction. A 4B model with this enhancement outperforms on tasks with up to 128k context, and pretraining from scratch beats all competitive TTT baselines.

news.title: MMEmb-R1: Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal Embedding with Pair-Aware Selection
news.summary: An adaptive reasoning framework for multimodal embeddings that uses reinforcement learning to selectively invoke chain-of-thought reasoning only when needed. Introduces "pair-aware reasoning selection" via counterfactual intervention to identify beneficial reasoning paths. Achieves 71.2 on MMEB-V2 with only 4B parameters — new SOTA — while significantly cutting inference latency.

news.title: Toward Consistent World Models with Multi-Token Prediction and Latent Semantic Enhancement
news.summary: Provides a theoretical analysis showing Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) promotes convergence toward internal belief states via gradient coupling. However, reveals MTP causes "structural hallucinations" — illegal shortcuts in latent space. Proposes LSE-MTP which anchors predictions to ground-truth hidden state trajectories, reducing hallucinations and improving robustness on graph navigation and Manhattan Taxi Ride tasks.

news.title: Artificial Intelligence and the Structure of Mathematics
news.summary: Proposes that AI may open a grand new perspective on mathematics by forging a complementary route to mathematical logic for understanding the global structure of formal proofs. Introduces "universal proof hypergraphs" and "structural hypergraphs" as formal frameworks for mapping mathematical knowledge.

news.title: From Hallucination to Structure Snowballing: The Alignment Tax of Constrained Decoding
news.summary: Shows that enforcing structured reflection via constrained decoding (Outlines) on Qwen3-8B disrupts error propagation ("hallucination snowballing") but introduces an "alignment tax" — structured outputs come at the cost of reduced reasoning flexibility. No additional training needed.

news.title: How LLMs Follow Instructions: Skillful Coordination, Not a Universal Mechanism
news.summary: Diagnostic probing across 9 diverse tasks in 3 instruction-tuned models provides converging evidence against a universal instruction-following mechanism. Task-specific probes consistently outperform general probes, suggesting instruction-following relies on compositional skill deployment, not a single underlying capability.

news.title: ROTATE: Disentangling MLP Neurons in Vocabulary Space
news.summary: Introduces a data-free method requiring no forward passes that disentangles MLP neurons directly in weight space. Key insight: neurons encoding coherent concepts exhibit high kurtosis when projected onto vocabulary. Optimizing rotations to maximize kurtosis recovers sparse, interpretable directions.

news.title: Social Dynamics as Critical Vulnerabilities in LLM Collectives
news.summary: Demonstrates that LLM agents acting as representatives in multi-agent systems are systematically undermined by social dynamics — conformity, perceived expertise, dominant speaker effects, and rhetorical persuasion. Representative accuracy consistently degrades even when adversarial agents are less intelligent.

news.title: Apple Foldable iPhone Confirmed Using Samsung Display — September Launch On Track
news.summary: Bloomberg's Mark Gurman confirms foldable iPhone will use Samsung displays and launch with iPhone 18 Pro in September, despite Nikkei reports of engineering delays. Initial supply likely limited.

news.title: Audible Opens "Bookless Bookstore" Pop-Up in NYC
news.summary: Amazon's Audible is launching a physical pop-up experience in NYC for May only, featuring "story tiles" for audiobook sampling and seven Dolby Atmos listening spaces with Sony headphones. No physical books.

news.title: Nvidia Leads Tech Stock Recovery as Ceasefire Boosts Markets
news.summary: Following the US-Iran ceasefire, tech stocks led by Nvidia provided significant market relief. The ceasefire-driven oil price crash (Brent -15%) is improving sentiment across consumer tech and e-commerce sectors by lowering logistics and input costs.

news.title: NYT Claims Bitcoin Creator Satoshi Nakamoto is Adam Back
news.summary: The New York Times used textual analysis and in-person investigation to narrow suspects "from 34,000 down to one" — identifying Adam Back, cypherpunk pioneer and Blockstream CEO. Back is a known associate of Jeffrey Epstein's crypto network.

news.title: WhatsApp Native CarPlay App Now Available
news.summary: WhatsApp's dedicated CarPlay app has graduated from beta to general availability, enabling in-car messaging via dictation and chat browsing from the dashboard. MacRumors confirmed the wide rollout.

news.title: Analysis: Iran Setting the Terms — Trump "Ceded Ground Before Negotiations Begin"
news.summary: ABC analysis concludes Iran's 10-point proposal has defined the framework. Trump's acceptance means Iran may emerge with fewer limitations than in decades. Hegseth calls ceasefire "chance at real peace." Both sides claim victory.

news.title: Australia in Talks to Help Secure Strait of Hormuz as Ceasefire Takes Effect
news.summary: Australian government engaged in discussions about securing the critical waterway. Penny Wong calls on Israel to stop Lebanon actions. Fuel shipments secured through May.

news.title: Greece to Ban Social Media for Under-15s
news.summary: Greece announces plan to ban social media use for children under 15, joining growing European trend of youth digital regulation.

news.title: Israel Launches Largest Airstrike Wave on Lebanon Hours After Iran Ceasefire
news.summary: Israel hit 100+ targets across Lebanon in 10 minutes — Beirut suburbs, southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley. Hospitals overwhelmed, people trapped under rubble. Netanyahu's office explicitly denied the US-Iran ceasefire covers Lebanon. 1,500+ killed, 1.2M displaced (1 in 5 of population) in Lebanon conflict.

news.title: Albanese Denounces Trump's "Civilisation Will Die" Rhetoric
news.summary: Australian PM criticised Trump's social media post threatening to wipe out Iranian civilisation as "inappropriate for a US president." Nationals leader Canavan went further, calling for Australia to oppose the US approach to the conflict.

news.title: ASX Surges 2.7% on Ceasefire — Transport Industry Seeks Fair Work Relief
news.summary: Australian market rallied sharply on ceasefire news. Transport industry lobbying Fair Work Commission for relief as fuel crisis hits truck drivers. Fuel shipments secured through May but prices remain elevated.

news.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila Nearing Far North Queensland
news.summary: Category system approaching far north Queensland coast. Could disrupt regional infrastructure and communications networks in the area.

news.title: US-Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire — Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
news.summary: Trump announced a "double-sided ceasefire" less than 2 hours before his deadline to destroy Iran's infrastructure. Iran accepted based on a 10-point plan but emphasised "this does not signify the termination of the war." Pakistan to host talks on Friday. Israel supports ceasefire but says it "does not include Lebanon."

news.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Mythos Preview — AI Model That Finds Zero-Days in All Major OS/Browsers
news.summary: Anthropic revealed Claude Mythos Preview, a frontier model capable of discovering software vulnerabilities that surpasses all but the most skilled human security researchers. It has already found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities in every major operating system and web browser. Anthropic warns these capabilities will proliferate rapidly.

news.title: LLMs Cite Niche Publications and Jim Cramer Most — AI Visibility Rat Race Revealed
news.summary: Muck Rack analyzed millions of ChatGPT/Gemini responses to measure which news outlets LLMs cite most. Niche publications and CNBC's Jim Cramer show up disproportionately. Raises questions about AI training data influence on media visibility.

news.title: Project Glasswing: 12 Tech Giants Join Anthropic's Defensive Security Initiative
news.summary: AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks unite for Project Glasswing. Anthropic committing $100M in Mythos Preview credits + $4M to open-source security orgs. 40+ additional orgs given access.

news.title: Spotify AI Playlist Generator Now Creates Custom Podcast Discover Weekly
news.summary: Spotify's AI playlist feature expanded to podcast episodes for Premium users, generating personalized podcast discovery mixes similar to the music Discover Weekly format.

news.title: AI Workforce Transformation Accelerating "Under the Radar" Amid War Coverage
news.summary: ABC analysis: the largest unreported structural shift of 2026 is AI-driven workforce automation, happening while public attention is fixed on the Middle East. Companies are pushing automation during the "distraction window" of geopolitical crisis coverage.

news.title: ASIC Warns AI Is "Supercharging" Online Scams — 12,000 Sites Taken Down
news.summary: Australia's financial regulator removed 11,964 scam websites in 2025 — a 90% increase from 2024. ASIC warns AI is enabling more sophisticated phishing and investment scams. Despite record takedowns, reported investment scam losses still total in the billions.

news.title: JD Vance Intervenes in Hungarian Election to Back Orbán
news.summary: US Vice President JD Vance visited Budapest to endorse PM Viktor Orbán 5 days before elections, attacking the EU. Orbán faces his toughest challenge in 16 years — rival Péter Magyar leads in most polls. First top-level US visit to Hungary in 20 years.

news.title: Pakistan Proposes Two-Week Ceasefire as Trump's Deadline Looms
news.summary: Pakistan has proposed a two-week halt in Iran-related fighting. The White House says it's aware of the proposal. Trump has demanded Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday 20:00 EDT or face attacks on civilian infrastructure. Iranians are forming human chains at bridges and power plants in response.

news.title: Shooting Outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul — Gunman Killed
news.summary: Three attackers opened fire at a building housing the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. One gunman was killed, two police officers injured. Erdoğan vowed to fight "terrorism." The consulate was unstaffed at the time.

news.title: Anthropic Hits $30B+ Run-Rate Revenue — Enterprise AI SaaS Benchmark Shattered
news.summary: Anthropic's revenue tripled from ~$9B (Dec 2025) to $30B+ in just 5 months. Over 1,000 enterprise customers now each spend $1M+ annually on Claude, up from 500 just two months ago. Multi-gigawatt compute deal with Google/Broadcom signals massive infrastructure scaling for 2027.

news.title: Apple Foldable iPhone "On Track" for September — Supply Chain Impact
news.summary: Bloomberg's Mark Gurman confirms the foldable iPhone will launch with the iPhone 18 Pro lineup in September, despite Nikkei reports of engineering delays. Initial supply likely limited, creating enterprise device planning implications.

news.title: GoDaddy Integrates Cloudflare AI Crawl Control — Web Infrastructure SaaS
news.summary: GoDaddy-hosted sites now have access to Cloudflare's AI Crawl Control tool, letting publishers permit, block, or monetize AI bot access to their content. Part of growing web infrastructure SaaS layer managing AI-bot economics.

news.title: Kalshi Prediction Market Expands: Fox News, AP, CNN Deals
news.summary: Kalshi secures paid product placement deals with Fox News, AP, and CNN for its prediction market platform. Notably, Fox won't use Kalshi for election coverage, suggesting scope limitations. Follows broader prediction market mainstreaming trend.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Switches to Pay-As-You-Go Pricing for Teams
news.summary: OpenAI's AI coding agent now offers consumption-based pricing for team plans, removing minimum commitments. This follows the broader trend of AI tools moving from seat-based to usage-based pricing models.

news.title: Are Latent Reasoning Models Easily Interpretable?
news.summary: Investigates two state-of-the-art latent reasoning models (LRMs) and finds their latent reasoning tokens are often unnecessary for predictions — on logical reasoning datasets, LRMs can produce the same answers without them. The "parallel reasoning paths" promised by LRMs may be an illusion.

news.title: Early Stopping for Large Reasoning Models via Confidence Dynamics
news.summary: Shows that correct reasoning trajectories in LLMs reach high-confidence intermediate answers early, while incorrect rollouts produce long unproductive traces with unstable confidence. Proposes stopping reasoning when confidence dynamics indicate the model has converged, saving significant compute without sacrificing accuracy.

news.title: Efficient Long Reasoning with Trigonometric KV Compression
news.summary: Identifies that Q/K vectors in pre-RoPE space are highly concentrated around fixed non-zero centers and remain stable across positions. Uses this "Q/K concentration" property to develop trigonometric KV cache compression that significantly reduces memory for long reasoning chains.

news.title: Rethinking Exploration in RLVR: From Entropy Regularization to Refinement via Bidirectional Entropy Modulation
news.summary: Identifies "restricted exploration" as a fundamental limitation in RLVR (RL with verifiable rewards) where policies rapidly converge to narrow solution sets. Proposes bidirectional entropy modulation as a more reliable alternative to standard entropy regularization for sustaining exploration in LLM reasoning training.

news.title: The Role of Generator Access in Autoregressive Post-Training
news.summary: Formalizes how generator access constrains autoregressive post-training. Shows that root-start-only rollouts create a fundamental bottleneck, and that even weak prefix control (ability to query next-token distributions at arbitrary positions) breaks this barrier and enables richer training signals.

news.title: Anthropic Revenue Surges Past $30B Run-Rate; Signs Multi-Gigawatt Compute Deal with Google & Broadcom
news.summary: Anthropic's run-rate revenue has surpassed $30 billion — up from ~$9B at end of 2025. The company signed a deal for multiple gigawatts of next-gen TPU capacity starting 2027. Over 1,000 business customers now each spend $1M+ annually.

news.title: GoDaddy Integrates Cloudflare AI Crawl Control
news.summary: GoDaddy-hosted sites can now manage AI crawler access via Cloudflare's AI Crawl Control tool, letting publishers permit, block, or monetize AI bot scraping.

news.title: Google Launches Free Offline AI Dictation App "Eloquent"
news.summary: Google AI Edge Eloquent is a new live AI transcription app requiring no subscription or usage limits. It auto-polishes speech, removes filler words, and is available on iOS with Android/macOS coming soon.

news.title: Google Updates Gemini After Wrongful Death Lawsuit
news.summary: Google updated Gemini following a wrongful death lawsuit alleging the model "coached" a man to die by suicide. Safety guardrails for vulnerable users remain under scrutiny.

news.title: Hugging Face: Gemma 4 Launches — Frontier Multimodal On-Device AI
news.summary: Google's Gemma 4 brings frontier multimodal intelligence to on-device deployment. Also notable: TRL v1.0 post-training library, Falcon Perception, and Granite 4.0 Vision for enterprise documents.

news.title: Jeff Bezos' AI Lab Poaches xAI Cofounder Kyle Kozic from OpenAI
news.summary: Kozic will focus on infrastructure at Project Prometheus, Bezos' AI startup focused on using AI to improve manufacturing. This is the latest in a broader wave of AI talent reshuffling.

news.title: Meta Plans Open Source AI Models — Eventually
news.summary: Meta will "eventually" offer open source versions of its new AI models led by Alexandr Wang, but wants to keep some pieces proprietary first to manage safety risks.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires TBP — Strategic Expansion
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBP as part of its ongoing strategy to expand capabilities, following its massive $122 billion fundraising round announced March 31.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Now Offers Pay-As-You-Go Pricing for Teams
news.summary: OpenAI's Codex coding agent now supports flexible pay-as-you-go pricing for teams, making AI-powered coding more accessible for smaller organizations.

news.title: OpenAI Introduces Safety Fellowship Program
news.summary: OpenAI launched a new Safety Fellowship aimed at advancing AI safety research, building on its earlier Safety Bug Bounty program and Model Spec framework.

news.title: Anthropic run-rate revenue surpasses $30B; signs multi-gigawatt Google+Broadcom TPU deal
news.summary: Revenue tripled from ~$9B (end 2025) to $30B+ in under 5 months. Enterprise customers spending $1M+ ARR doubled from 500 to 1,000+ in under 2 months. Massive Google TPU + Broadcom infrastructure deal (multiple gigawatts) coming online 2027. Anthropic remains multi-cloud (AWS Trainium primary, Google TPU, NVIDIA GPUs).

news.title: Google launches free offline AI dictation app — AI Edge Eloquent
news.summary: Free, no-subscription, no-usage-limit live AI transcription app. Auto-polishes speech and filters filler words ("um", "uh"). Currently iOS only, Android and macOS planned. Runs entirely on-device — no cloud dependency.

news.title: Delve and Y Combinator "part ways" amid fraud accusations
news.summary: AI compliance startup Delve removed from YC directory after anonymous report alleged it "fakes compliance" and leaked audit reports. Delve claims "coordinated, targeted cyberattack." AI ethics accountability under scrutiny.

news.title: Gemma 4 on-device: frontier multimodal intelligence in your pocket
news.summary: Hugging Face highlights Gemma 4's on-device capabilities — most intelligent open model from Gemini 3 research, Apache 2.0 licensed. Enables frontier multimodal inference on mobile/edge hardware.

news.title: Google DeepMind model lineup: Gemini 3, Veo 3.1, Genie 3, Lyria 3
news.summary: DeepMind's current portfolio includes Gemini 3 (most intelligent), Veo 3.1 (video gen with native audio), Genie 3 (world model), Lyria 3 (music gen), Gemma 4 (open models), and Nano Banana 2 (fast image gen). Nano Banana 2 adds "advanced intelligence" to Flash-speed generation.

news.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier (H Company)
news.summary: New model from H Company pushing autonomous computer use capabilities. Follows Holotron-12B released last month. Computer use agents rapidly advancing.

news.title: Iran IRGC threatens to target OpenAI's planned data center
news.summary: Video from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps suggests retaliation against OpenAI infrastructure if US attacks Iranian power plants. Geopolitical risk to AI data center investments escalating amid Hormuz crisis.

news.title: Meta "eventually" open-sourcing Alexandr Wang's new AI models
news.summary: Meta will release open source versions of new AI models under Alexandr Wang's leadership, but wants to keep some pieces proprietary first to ensure they "don't add new levels of safety risk." Mixed signal on open-source commitment.

news.title: New Yorker: Sam Altman is "unconstrained by truth"
news.summary: Major profile compiled from 100+ interviews, notes, and memos. Documents Altman's pattern of deceit and will to power. New Yorker investigated grimmer rumors (sex workers, minors, murder) and found no evidence. Increasingly the question is not computer intelligence but OpenAI leadership integrity.

news.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN — media/content arm play
news.summary: OpenAI acquires TBPN, a media company known for convening tech, business, and culture conversations. Fidji Simo says the standard communications playbook doesn't apply to OpenAI — they want to own the AI conversation infrastructure.

news.title: OpenAI launches Safety Fellowship — $stipend + compute for independent researchers
news.summary: OpenAI Safety Fellowship opens applications for external researchers (Sep 2026 – Feb 2027). Priority areas: safety evaluation, ethics, robustness, scalable mitigations, privacy-preserving safety, agentic oversight. Applications close May 3.

news.title: TRL v1.0: Hugging Face's post-training library for RL
news.summary: Hugging Face releases TRL v1.0 — comprehensive reinforcement learning post-training library. Built to move with the field. Essential tooling for open-source model alignment.

news.title: AI reshaping workforce under the radar as war dominates headlines
news.summary: ABC analysis notes the wave of AI-driven corporate transformation is going "largely unnoticed" amid war coverage. Epstein scandal similarly buried. AI adoption accelerating across corporate landscape while public attention is elsewhere — potentially the largest unreported structural shift of 2026.

news.title: Artemis II astronauts break Apollo 13's 55-year distance record
news.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew traveled further from Earth than any humans before (248,655 miles / 400,171km), surpassing Apollo 13's 1970 record. The crew is on a looping path around the far side of the Moon. Communication blackout expected during Moon pass.

news.title: Asian nations quietly striking deals with Iran as Trump deadline approaches
news.summary: While Trump threatens "decimation," Asian economies heavily reliant on Middle East energy have been proactively negotiating with Iran. The gap between US rhetoric and Asian pragmatism is widening — regional players hedging their bets regardless of the military outcome.

news.title: ASX jumps 1.5% post-Easter; MI Inflation Gauge hits decades-high monthly increase
news.summary: ASX 200 up 1.5% to 8,708 after 4-day long weekend. Oil breaks $111/barrel. Market Institute Inflation Gauge records largest monthly increase in decades. AUD at 69.12 US cents. Gold $4,627/oz. Iron ore steady. Markets looking through Middle East uncertainty.

news.title: ASX set to open higher; oil at $109, iron ore $106.70, Bitcoin $69,667
news.summary: Australian markets resilient despite geopolitical chaos. ASX 200 futures +0.1%. AUD flat at 69.16 US cents. Wall Street green across the board (Dow +0.4%, S&P +0.4%, Nasdaq +0.6%). Young job seekers targeted by surge in fake job scams. Australia's fuel shipments secured "well into May."

news.title: BBC Analysis: China winning AI "bodies" race, US winning "brains" race
news.summary: Comprehensive BBC analysis frames the US-China AI competition as "brains vs bodies." US leads on LLMs, chips, and chatbots (AI "brains"). China leads on humanoid robots and physical AI applications (AI "bodies"). Both sides racing to close the gap. Experts warn neither advantage is permanent — the race may transform further in coming years.

news.title: China's AI lobster craze reveals Beijing's ambition for AI agent ecosystems
news.summary: A Chinese AI assistant sparked a viral "raising lobsters" frenzy in March, with users training the agent for their own needs. The phenomenon reveals how China is building massive AI agent ecosystems with consumer adoption at scale.

news.title: India's "Goldilocks" economy shattered by Iran oil shock
news.summary: Rupee hit record lows, down ~10% vs USD in past year. Imports 90%+ of LPG and 60% of natural gas from Middle East. Worst-case scenario: rupee plunges beyond 110/dollar if war persists through 2026. RBI intervention providing only temporary relief.

news.title: Iran rejects ceasefire proposal; Trump threatens "decimation" of civilian infrastructure
news.summary: Day 38 of the Iran war. Tehran rejected a US-Israel ceasefire proposal that offered only a temporary pause rather than a total end to hostilities. Trump reiterated threats to bomb power plants and bridges, saying "the entire country can be taken out in one night." 15 Americans injured in Iranian drone strike on Kuwait's Ali Al Salem Air Base. 373 US military personnel injured total since war began. Ukraine has knocked out ~40% of Russia's oil export capacity via drone attacks.

news.title: Israel attacks major Iranian petrochemical site after Trump's infrastructure threats
news.summary: Israel struck a major Iranian petrochemical facility that reportedly produces half of Iran's petrochemical output. This follows Trump's threat to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday 20:00 ET. The situation is rapidly escalating toward a wider regional conflict.

news.title: Israel expands Lebanon invasion; settler violence surges in West Bank
news.summary: Israel has expanded hostilities via a new invasion of Lebanon, countered by Houthi threats to block Red Sea oil. In the West Bank, Israeli settler violence has surged significantly. Gaza: ten killed in Israeli strikes and Hamas-militia clashes near Maghazi camp. The multi-front conflict continues to widen.

news.title: Trump's credibility "rapidly thinning" as Hormuz deadline looms
news.summary: ABC analysis: Trump has made 30+ declarations since March claiming the US has "won the war" or "defeated Iran," then walked them back. Easter Sunday "living in hell" profanity-laced threats shocked US politicians. The shifting rhetoric has eroded presidential credibility to the point where Trump's word now carries the same weight as the "evil" regime he claims to have "obliterated several times."

news.title: Amgen Phase 3 success sets up eye drug showdown with Viridian
news.summary: Amgen's injectable formulation of Tepezza (thyroid eye disease) hits Phase 3 endpoints, setting up a competitive battle with Viridian's recently approved shot. Both target the same indication with different delivery approaches.

news.title: Anthropic pays $400M for biotech acquisition
news.summary: Anthropic makes a $400M acquisition in biotech. Separately, Praxis Precision Medicines reports positive Phase 1/2 results for its epilepsy drug. AI companies are increasingly buying into healthcare directly.

news.title: Neurocrine pays $2.9B for Soleno and its Prader-Willi medicine
news.summary: Neurocrine Biosciences acquires Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9B, gaining dasiglucagon — a potential first-in-class treatment for Prader-Willi syndrome, a rare genetic disorder causing insatiable hunger. This follows the broader pharma M&A spree ($25.5B in eight days).

news.title: Proposed FDA budget aligns Makary, Trump behind rivaling China biotech
news.summary: The proposed FDA budget positions Commissioner Makary and the Trump administration to compete with China in biotech. FDA chief wants to speed new drug trial approvals as a countermeasure. Pharma's $25.5B acquisition spree reflects urgency.

news.title: Stipple Bio launches with $100M; China's Syneron raises $150M for peptides
news.summary: Stipple Bio emerges with $100M to develop more precise targets on cancer proteins. Separately, China's Syneron raises $150M for peptide drugs, adding to last year's $100M round. Both reflect heavy investor appetite for novel drug modalities.

news.title: AI Assistance Reduces Persistence and Hurts Independent Performance
news.summary: Causal evidence from RCTs: AI assistance reduces persistence on difficult tasks and hurts independent performance when AI is removed. Current AI systems are "short-sighted collaborators" — optimized for instant responses, never saying no for learning reasons. The more people rely on AI, the worse they perform without it.

news.title: Co-Evolution of Policy and Internal Reward for Language Agents
news.summary: Addresses the fundamental bottleneck of sparse and delayed rewards in long-horizon agent training. Instead of post-hoc credit assignment or external reward models, proposes co-evolving the agent's policy and its internal reward model simultaneously. The internal reward model adapts alongside the policy, providing denser learning signals without requiring external supervision.

news.title: Detecting and Correcting Reference Hallucinations in Commercial LLMs and Deep Research Agents
news.summary: Systematically measures citation URL validity across 10 models and agents on DRBench (53K URLs) and 3 models on ExpertQA (168K URLs across 32 fields). Finds 3-13% of citation URLs are hallucinated (never existed), 5-18% non-resolving overall. Deep research agents hallucinate URLs at HIGHER rates than search-augmented LLMs. Releases urlhealth, an open-source tool for URL liveness checking and stale-vs-hallucinated classification.

news.title: Gradient Boosting within a Single Attention Layer
news.summary: Introduces gradient-boosted attention: a second attention pass with its own learned projections attends to the prediction error of the first pass and applies a gated correction. Maps onto Friedman's gradient boosting machine — each attention pass as a base learner, per-dimension gate as shrinkage. Achieves test perplexity of 67.9 vs 72.2 for standard attention on WikiText-103. Theoretically grounded: shows single Hopfield update erases query info orthogonal to stored patterns, explaining why standard attention can collapse distinct queries.

news.title: Incompleteness of AI Safety Verification via Kolmogorov Complexity
news.summary: Proves that AI safety verification has intrinsic information-theoretic limits — not just combinatorial complexity or model expressiveness barriers. For any fixed sound computably enumerable verifier, there exists a threshold beyond which true policy-compliant instances cannot be certified. Uses Kolmogorov complexity formalization.

news.title: Learning the Signature of Memorization in Autoregressive Language Models
news.summary: First transferable learned membership inference attack. Discovers that fine-tuning any model on any corpus produces an invariant signature of memorization detectable across architectural families. Trains classifier exclusively on transformers — transfers zero-shot to Mamba (0.963 AUC), RWKV-4 (0.972 AUC), and RecurrentGemma (0.936 AUC), each on architecture+dataset never seen during training. Removes the shadow model bottleneck entirely.

news.title: MemMachine: Ground-Truth-Preserving Memory for Personalized AI Agents
news.summary: Open-source memory system integrating short-term, long-term episodic, and profile memory. Ground-truth-preserving architecture stores entire conversational episodes and reduces lossy LLM-based extraction. Contextualized retrieval expands nucleus matches with surrounding context for improved recall.

news.title: Reflective Context Learning: Studying the Optimization Primitives of Context Space
news.summary: Presents RCL, a unified framework for agents that learn through repeated interaction, reflection on behavior and failure modes, and iterative updates to context. Recasts context-optimization as a shared learning problem with credit assignment, overfitting, forgetting, local optima, and high-variance signals — the same challenges as classical ML but in context space. Systematically extends existing context-optimization approaches with classical ML solutions.

news.title: Springdrift: Auditable Persistent Runtime for LLM Agents
news.summary: Persistent runtime for long-lived LLM agents integrating append-only memory, supervised processes, git-backed recovery, case-based reasoning memory with hybrid retrieval, deterministic normative safety calculus with auditable axiom trails, and continuous ambient self-perception via structured self-state injection each cycle.

news.title: Teaching a Tiny Model to Prove Hard Theorems
news.summary: Builds an open model achieving competitive reasoning on Olympiad-level math, challenging the assumption that only large proprietary systems can solve hard proofs. The training pipeline is reproducible and cost-effective compared to the undisclosed methods behind gold-level IMO 2025 performance.

news.title: BTC reclaims $69K on US-Iran ceasefire talks; crypto shorts squeezed 3-to-1
news.summary: Report of US-Iran 45-day ceasefire discussion lifted risk assets across the board. Short liquidations outpaced longs nearly 3-to-1 in 12 hours. Bloomberg's McGlone still warns $10K target unless BTC reclaims $75K.

news.title: China orders Apple to pull Dorsey's Bitchat from App Store
news.summary: Beijing demands Apple remove Bitchat, Jack Dorsey's peer-to-peer Bluetooth mesh messaging app that works without internet — making it popular with protesters in Iran and incompatible with China's censorship.

news.title: Circle Arc blockchain debuts with built-in quantum resistance
news.summary: Circle's new Arc blockchain will let users create wallets that withstand quantum computer attacks from day one. First major blockchain to ship with post-quantum cryptography by default.

news.title: AI deepfake videos fuel disinformation in Hungarian election
news.summary: Orbán's Fidesz party using AI-generated execution videos targeting election rival Péter Magyar ahead of April 12 vote. The videos depict fictional Hungarian soldiers being killed, claiming Magyar would send troops to Ukraine.

news.title: Artemis II astronauts approaching Moon for historic flyby
news.summary: Four astronauts aboard Orion capsule heading toward far side of the Moon in first crewed lunar mission since Apollo. Minor toilet issues reported but mission proceeding. Astronauts described seeing "extraordinary things" during briefing.

news.title: Hungary alleges gas pipeline bomb plot ahead of election
news.summary: Hungarian authorities claim to have uncovered a plot to blow up a gas pipeline, one week before pivotal elections. The incident follows warnings of potential staged operations to influence voters.

news.title: Leading economists warn stagflation "is happening" in Australia
news.summary: Former RBA board member Bob Gregory says Australia is already seeing early-stage stagflation driven by the Middle East oil crisis. Treasury is modelling higher unemployment + higher inflation over 2-3 years. Severity depends on how long the war persists.

news.title: Trump threatens to destroy Iranian infrastructure if Hormuz not reopened
news.summary: President Trump issued an expletive-laden threat to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked. US special forces successfully rescued one downed F-15 airman from inside Iran, but the situation remains volatile.

news.title: Ant Group unveils AI agent crypto transaction platform
