news.summary: Ant Group's blockchain arm launched Anvita — a platform for AI agents to coordinate tasks and settle payments in real time using stablecoins, with built-in tokenization services.

news.title: Drift $270M exploit was a 6-month North Korean intel operation
news.summary: Attackers posed as a trading firm, met Drift contributors in person across multiple countries, deposited $1M of their own capital, and waited half a year before executing the $270M drain. The sophistication of the social engineering is unprecedented.

news.title: Ledger CTO: AI is making crypto security problems worse
news.summary: Ledger's Charles Guillemet warns that AI is making hacks cheaper and faster, forcing a fundamental rethink of crypto security. The same tools enabling DeFi growth are also lowering the barrier for attackers.

news.title: Polymarket pulls Iran rescue markets amid regulatory pressure
news.summary: Prediction markets face rising regulatory scrutiny, with congressional Democrats proposing legislation to ban contracts tied to elections, war, and government actions. Polymarket pulled its controversial Iran hostage rescue markets.

news.title: Schwab plans spot Bitcoin & Ether trading for H1 2026
news.summary: Charles Schwab ($12T AUM) is moving closer to direct crypto trading with early access subscriptions for the Schwab Crypto account. This is the clearest signal yet of mainstream brokerage crypto adoption.

news.title: Batched Contextual Reinforcement: A Task-Scaling Law for Efficient Reasoning
news.summary: Trains LLMs to solve N problems simultaneously in a shared context window, creating implicit token budgets. Discovers a task-scaling law: more concurrent problems → less tokens per problem, with graceful accuracy degradation. Achieves 15.8–62.6% token reduction while maintaining or improving accuracy — a "free lunch" at standard inference.

news.title: Novel Memory Forgetting Techniques for Autonomous AI Agents
news.summary: Introduces adaptive budgeted forgetting for long-horizon conversational agents. Integrates recency, frequency, and semantic alignment to regulate memory without increasing context usage. Improves long-horizon F1 beyond 0.583 baseline while reducing false memory propagation.

news.title: Perception-Grounded Policy Optimization (PGPO) for Vision-Language Models
news.summary: Formulates "Token Visual Dependency" to quantify how much each token relies on visual input via KL divergence. PGPO dynamically amplifies learning signals for visually-dependent tokens while suppressing linguistic prior noise. Boosts Qwen2.5-VL by 18.7% on average across 7 multimodal reasoning benchmarks.

news.title: ReRanking Preference Optimization (RRPO): Optimizing RAG Rerankers with LLM Feedback
news.summary: Formulates reranking as sequential decision-making and optimizes for actual LLM generation quality via RL, eliminating need for expensive human annotations. Uses reference-anchored deterministic baseline for stability. Significantly outperforms RankZephyr and generalizes to diverse readers including GPT-4o.

news.title: Sample-Routed Policy Optimization (SRPO)
news.summary: Unifies GRPO and SDPO by routing correct samples to reward-aligned RL and failed samples to logit-level correction. Adds entropy-aware dynamic weighting to suppress unreliable distillation targets. Beats both baselines: +3.4% over GRPO, +6.3% over SDPO on Qwen3-8B, with 17.2% lower compute cost.

news.title: Codex now offers pay-as-you-go pricing for teams
news.summary: OpenAI's Codex coding agent launched flexible per-use pricing for teams, lowering the barrier for organizations to adopt AI coding tools.

news.title: Cursor launches new AI agent experience to rival Claude Code and Codex
news.summary: Cursor introduced a new agentic coding experience, intensifying competition in the AI-powered IDE space against Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex.

news.title: Falcon Perception: TII's new vision model
news.summary: UAE's TII released Falcon Perception, adding vision capabilities to their Falcon model family — notable move from a state-backed AI lab.

news.title: Google launches Veo 3.1, Lyria 3, and Nano Banana 2
news.summary: Google rolled out updated video generation (Veo 3.1), music generation (Lyria 3), and image generation (Nano Banana 2) models, plus a Google Vids upgrade with AI avatar direction.

news.title: Google releases Gemma 4 open models with Apache 2.0 license
news.summary: Google dropped Gemma 4, its most intelligent open model built from Gemini 3 research, switching from restrictive custom license to permissive Apache 2.0 — a major win for the open-source AI community.

news.title: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: IBM's compact multimodal model
news.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a tiny multimodal model targeting enterprise document processing — efficient enough to run on-device.

news.title: Holo3 breaks the computer use frontier
news.summary: H Company released Holo3, a new model pushing the boundaries of autonomous computer use agents, following their Holotron-12B release in March.

news.title: Meta pauses work with Mercor after major AI data breach
news.summary: Meta halted its relationship with AI training data company Mercor after a security breach exposed AI industry secrets. OpenAI is also investigating. Mercor is a major player in AI data labeling workforce.

news.title: OpenAI acquires TBP (The Browser Project / agent startup)
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPNCompany on Apr 2, continuing its aggressive acquisition streak of agent and browser automation startups.

news.title: OpenAI raises $122 billion — largest AI fundraise in history
news.summary: OpenAI closed a massive $122B funding round to accelerate the next phase of AI development, cementing its position as the most valuable private AI company.

news.title: OpenAI's Fidji Simo takes medical leave amid executive shake-up
news.summary: OpenAI President Fidji Simo is stepping away on medical leave, adding to ongoing leadership turbulence at the company amid its massive fundraising and acquisition spree.

news.title: Perplexity sued for sharing user conversations with Meta and Google
news.summary: A class action lawsuit alleges Perplexity embedded Meta and Google trackers in its AI search engine, sharing user conversations and personal data even when "Incognito" mode was enabled.

news.title: TRL v1.0 released — post-training library for the community
news.summary: Hugging Face launched TRL v1.0, a comprehensive reinforcement learning post-training library designed to keep pace with the rapidly evolving RL field.

news.title: Writers Guild reaches 4-year deal with increased AI protections
news.summary: The WGA secured a new four-year agreement with studios that includes stronger protections against AI use in creative writing, following the pattern set by the 2023 strike.

news.title: Automatic Textbook Formalization: 500+ pages of graduate math → Lean in one week
news.summary: 30K Claude 4.5 Opus agents collaborating in parallel via version control formalized a graduate-level algebraic combinatorics textbook (130K lines, 5900 Lean declarations) in one week. Sets a record in multi-agent software engineering with usable results. Inference cost matches or undercuts equivalent human expert salaries.

news.title: Gradient-Boosted Attention: Boosting within a single attention layer
news.summary: Applies gradient boosting principle inside a single attention layer: a second attention pass with learned projections attends to the prediction error of the first and applies gated correction. Maps onto Friedman's GBM mathematically. Achieves 67.9 perplexity on WikiText-103 vs 72.2 for standard attention — a 6% improvement from a simple architectural change.

news.title: InfoSeeker: Hierarchical Parallel Agent Framework for Web Information Seeking
news.summary: Host-Manager-Worker hierarchy with strict context isolation to prevent saturation and error propagation. Parallel worker layer achieves 3-5x speed-up. 8.4% success on WideSearch-en and 52.9% accuracy on BrowseComp-zh.

news.title: LLMs achieve near-optimal planning: Algorithmic Simulation vs Geometric Memory
news.summary: Reasoning-enhanced LLMs significantly outperform classical satisficing planners on Blocksworld and formally equivalent Path-Star graphs. Models track theoretical optimality limits with near-perfect precision, even when semantic hints are stripped. Authors propose two hypotheses: active "Algorithmic Simulation" via reasoning tokens and "Geometric Memory" that represents topology as navigable global geometry.

news.title: Japan PM Takaichi Plans Australia Visit; Fuel Supply Assurances Given
news.summary: Japan confirmed fuel will continue flowing to Australia under assurances from recent talks. PM Sanae Takaichi plans to visit Australia to discuss Hormuz conditions and rare-earth minerals. Australia has also secured supply assurances from South Korea and Singapore. About 20% of world oil normally ships through the blocked strait.

news.title: Missing US Airman Rescued in "Daring" Operation; US-Iran Forces Engaged
news.summary: Trump announced the missing weapons system officer from the downed F-15 has been rescued. BBC understands there was "engagement" between US and Iranian forces during the rescue. Both crew members now safe. The 48-hour Trump ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or make a deal remains active.

news.title: NSW Government Announces Fuel Price Relief Measures
news.summary: NSW fuel check program receives funding boost as government responds to pump prices driven by the Middle East oil crisis. Hundreds of petrol stations across Australia have run out of fuel. Australians told to continue Easter travel plans despite shortages.

news.title: Putin Escalates Internet Crackdown: "Soviet Era on Steroids"
news.summary: Kremlin rolling blackouts of internet services, banned foreign messaging apps, expanded FSB powers. ISW reports the crackdown is rooted in weakness—to control anti-war narratives and prepare for protracted Ukraine conflict. Moscow residents can't navigate the city or use apps during blackouts.

news.title: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation
news.summary: Just sample solutions from the model at certain temperature/truncation configs, then fine-tune on those outputs. No verifier, no teacher model, no RL. Boosts Qwen3-30B-Instruct from 42.4% → 55.3% pass@1 on LiveCodeBench v6. Gains concentrate on harder problems. Generalizes across Qwen and Llama at 4B/8B/30B scale, including instruct and thinking variants.

news.title: Australian Economists Warn Stagflation "Is Happening"
news.summary: Former RBA board member Bob Gregory says stagflation is already occurring. Former Treasury secretary Martin Parkinson warns of real risk. Both cite the Middle East oil crisis as the trigger. Treasury is running model simulations showing higher unemployment and inflation over the next 2-3 years. Fuel prices nearly double what government budgeted, straining household budgets.

news.title: Europe Faces New Energy Shock, Considers Reviving Nuclear Power
news.summary: The Middle East oil crisis has reignited Europe's energy security debate. Countries are asking whether reviving nuclear power is the answer to reducing dependence on fossil fuel imports. Germany's new policy requiring military approval for males under 45 traveling abroad long-term reflects growing security concerns.

news.title: Hormuz Blockade: 20,000 Seafarers Trapped in Persian Gulf
news.summary: ABC investigation reveals 20,000 seafarers from ~2,000 ships are trapped in the Persian Gulf. Food and water running low. 10 killed in attacks. Companies forcing crews to continue cargo operations in active war zone. First French-owned ship recently passed through the strait, but most traffic remains halted.

news.title: Orban Faces Toughest Re-Election Fight in 16 Years
news.summary: Hungary votes in 9 days in what could unseat Europe's longest-serving leader, Viktor Orban. His opposition has unified behind a credible challenger. Orban's government has been a key voice in EU tech regulation debates, often opposing stricter AI oversight.

news.title: US-Iran War Escalates: F-15 Downed, Missing Airman, 48-Hour Deadline
news.summary: Iran shot down a US F-15 Eagle; pilot rescued but weapons system officer remains missing. Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum: make a deal or reopen the Strait of Hormuz, threatening to "rain hell" on Iran. ~2,000 ships and 20,000 seafarers remain trapped in the Persian Gulf under drone and missile threat. 10 sailors killed, 21 vessels attacked to date.

news.title: Anthropic Discovers Functional Emotion Representations in Claude
news.summary: Anthropic's interpretability team found that Claude Sonnet 4.5 has internal emotion-related neural patterns that causally influence behavior. "Desperation" patterns can drive unethical actions; positive emotion patterns guide task preference. The finding suggests AI safety may require "emotionally healthy" processing.

news.title: Claude Code Finds Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years
news.summary: A developer used Claude Code to discover a Linux kernel vulnerability that had existed since 2003. The coding agent's systematic code review capabilities identified the bug that human reviewers had missed for over two decades. (979 points on HN)

news.title: Sebastian Raschka Publishes Definitive Guide to Coding Agent Architecture
news.summary: The ML education author published a comprehensive breakdown of coding agent components: the distinction between LLMs, reasoning models, agent harnesses, and coding harnesses. Explains why Claude Code and Codex feel more capable than the same models in plain chat.

news.title: sllm.cloud: Shared GPU Nodes for AI Development
news.summary: New service lets developers split a GPU node with others, offering unlimited tokens at lower cost. Targets the long-context AI coding and research use case. (Show HN, 66 points)

news.title: Zuckerberg/Chan Free School Closure Creates $70M Crisis for Local District
news.summary: The abrupt closure of the tuition-free private school founded by Priscilla Chan will dump extra students into the local school district, increasing expected enrollment by 20%. A $70 million bond measure is now up for votes to handle the influx, with the measure text calling it "an immediate crisis."

news.title: Batched Contextual Reinforcement: A Task-Scaling Law for Efficient Reasoning
news.summary: Trains LLMs to solve N problems simultaneously in one context window, creating an implicit token budget. Discovers a "task-scaling law": more concurrent problems → less tokens per problem, with accuracy degrading gracefully. Achieves 15.8–62.6% token reduction while maintaining or improving accuracy across math benchmarks. Models develop emergent self-regulated efficiency without explicit length supervision.

news.title: De Jure: Automated Regulatory Rule Extraction via Iterative LLM Self-Refinement
news.summary: Fully automated pipeline for extracting structured regulatory rules from legal documents across finance, healthcare, and AI governance. Uses 4-stage process: normalization → semantic decomposition → 19-dimension LLM-as-judge evaluation → iterative repair. Downstream RAG-based compliance QA prefers De Jure extractions 73.8–84.0% of the time over prior work.

news.title: Sample-Routed Policy Optimization: Unifying GRPO and SDPO
news.summary: Unifies Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and Self-Distillation Policy Optimization (SDPO) by routing correct samples to reward-aligned RL and failed samples to targeted logit-level correction. Adds entropy-aware dynamic weighting to suppress unreliable distillation. Achieves 3.4% improvement over GRPO and 6.3% over SDPO on Qwen3-8B across 5 benchmarks, with 17.2% lower per-step compute.

news.title: The Self-Driving Portfolio: Agentic Architecture for Institutional Asset Management
news.summary: Presents an agentic pipeline with ~50 specialized agents for strategic asset allocation: generating capital market assumptions, constructing portfolios via 20+ competing methods, peer critique/voting, and a meta-agent that rewrites agent code based on past forecast accuracy. Governed by Investment Policy Statements—the same docs used by human PMs.

news.title: User Turn Generation as a Probe of Interaction Awareness in LMs
news.summary: Proposes a new evaluation paradigm: instead of scoring assistant responses, generate the user's next turn to measure "interaction awareness." Finds that across 11 open-weight LLMs (Qwen3.5, gpt-oss, GLM), task accuracy and interaction awareness are decoupled—high-accuracy models may still have near-zero genuine follow-up rates at low temperature.

news.title: Anthropic ends third-party Claude subscription access (incl. OpenClaw)
news.summary: Claude Pro/Max subscriptions will no longer cover third-party API access starting April 4, 2026. This directly affects tools like OpenClaw that relay Claude via user subscriptions.

news.title: Codex launches pay-as-you-go pricing; ChatGPT Business drops to $20/seat
news.summary: OpenAI introduced flexible Codex-only seats with token-based billing (no rate limits). ChatGPT Business annual price cut from $25 to $20/seat. Codex usage has grown 6x since January, with 2M+ weekly builders.

news.title: Elon Musk requires SpaceX IPO advisers to buy Grok subscriptions
news.summary: Musk is reportedly demanding banks, law firms, and auditors working on the SpaceX IPO purchase Grok subscriptions. Grok is now under the SpaceX umbrella after the xAI/X/SpaceX merger.

news.title: Google Gemini 3.1 Deep Think mode launches
news.summary: New specialized reasoning mode for Gemini 3.1 Pro, available to Google AI Ultra subscribers. Claims state-of-the-art on HLE (51.4% with tools) and ARC-AGI-2 (77.1%). Also notable: Google AI Pro storage doubled to 5TB.

news.title: Google releases Gemma 4 open models under Apache 2.0
news.summary: Four sizes (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) built from Gemini 3 tech. License switched from restrictive custom to Apache 2.0. 31B ranks #3 open model on Arena AI. 400M+ total Gemma downloads to date.

news.title: Mercor (AI training data) hit by security breach; Meta pauses work
news.summary: A company that produces AI training data suffered a security incident putting industry secrets at risk. Meta has paused its relationship with Mercor; OpenAI is investigating.

news.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN (Tech Business Programming Network)
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, the fast-growing daily tech talk show described by the NYT as "Silicon Valley's newest obsession." TBPN will retain editorial independence and sit within OpenAI's Strategy org.

news.title: OpenAI raises $122B at $852B valuation
news.summary: OpenAI closed its latest funding round anchored by Amazon, NVIDIA, and SoftBank. The company reports $2B/month revenue, 900M+ weekly active ChatGPT users, and 50M+ subscribers. GPT-5.4 recently shipped as their most capable model.

news.title: Penguin Random House sues OpenAI in Munich
news.summary: The publisher filed a copyright lawsuit accusing ChatGPT of copying a popular German children's book series. Part of growing global litigation against AI training practices.

news.title: Perplexity sued for sharing user conversations with Meta & Google
news.summary: A class-action lawsuit alleges Perplexity embedded Meta and Google trackers that shared user conversations, email addresses, and identifiers—even in "Incognito" mode. Paid users were also affected.

news.title: Iran says strike near nuclear plant kills one; search for US airman continues
news.summary: Iran reports one death from a strike near its nuclear facility. Two US warplanes shot at in the region — one pilot rescued, second crew member (WSO) still missing. Iran denies arrest but governor claims US "rescue" narrative is "enemy disinformation." Armed Iranian civilians still hunting with £50K bounty.

news.title: UAE intercepted 2,141 drones and 498 missiles since war began; 13 killed
news.summary: UAE daily update: 23 ballistic missiles + 56 drones intercepted on April 4 alone. Total since Feb 28: 498 missiles, 23 cruise missiles, 2,141 drones. 13 killed (2 military, 1 contractor, 10 civilians), 217 injured. Bahrain reports 453 drones + 188 missiles intercepted.

news.title: UN Security Council Hormuz resolution vote cancelled
news.summary: Bahrain-proposed resolution to authorize member states to secure Strait of Hormuz transit for 6+ months was expected to vote today but cancelled. No new date set. Previously postponed from April 3.

news.title: Australia: fuel shortage fears drive demand surge despite adequate supply
news.summary: Panic buying continues at Australian petrol stations despite government assurances of adequate supply. Strait of Hormuz disruption creating psychological demand spike. Industry says supply chains are secure.

news.title: Europe debates nuclear power revival amid war-driven energy shock
news.summary: With Iran war driving gas and fuel prices higher, European nations are seriously reconsidering nuclear energy as path to independence. Multiple countries assessing reactor restarts and new builds.

news.title: Iran war escalates: missiles hit Israel, US pilot missing, Gulf states under fire
news.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel (1 injured in Bnei Brak). Debris fell in Dubai Marina. Bahrain sounded air raid sirens. Armed Iranian civilians searching for missing US weapon systems officer with £50K bounty. Syrian state TV reports explosions in Damascus from intercepted missiles. One pilot rescued, second still missing in southern Iran.

news.title: Claude subscriptions no longer cover third-party tool access (e.g., OpenClaw) starting today
news.summary: Anthropic is changing how Claude subscriptions work with third-party tools. Starting April 4th, Claude Pro/Max subscriptions will no longer cover API access through external tools like OpenClaw. Users will need separate API billing.

news.title: Mercor data breach: Meta pauses work, OpenAI/Anthropic investigating
news.summary: Major AI training data vendor Mercor hit by security breach that could expose proprietary training data secrets. Meta has indefinitely paused all work with Mercor. Other major AI labs (OpenAI, Anthropic) are reevaluating their relationships. Mercor generates bespoke datasets for ChatGPT, Claude Code, and other AI products.

news.title: Elon Musk forces SpaceX IPO advisers to buy Grok subscriptions
news.summary: NYT reports Musk is demanding banks, law firms, auditors and other advisers working on the SpaceX IPO buy subscriptions to Grok. Grok is now under the SpaceX umbrella after the xAI/X merger.

news.title: Take-Two Interactive lays off entire AI team
news.summary: Head of AI Luke Dicken and his team are no longer at the company. Dicken was previously senior director of applied AI at Zynga (Take-Two owned) for two years before being promoted to head of AI for the whole company.

news.title: Iran War: Trump threatens "back to Stone Ages" in 2-3 weeks
news.summary: In his first televised national address on the war, Trump said US is "shortly, very shortly" from achieving military objectives. Admin timelines have oscillated between 2-8 weeks. 6-week mark falls April 11. Hegseth says they'll never reveal the actual timeline.

news.title: Russia rejects Easter ceasefire, launches massive attack on Ukraine
news.summary: Hundreds of drones and missiles launched across Ukraine, killing 6 civilians, injuring 40. Zelensky accused Moscow of "Easter escalation." US peace efforts stalled as Trump administration focuses on Iran. Ukraine's frontline reportedly most favorable in 10 months.

news.title: Strait of Hormuz: 95% traffic halted, 20,000 sailors trapped
news.summary: 2000 vessels stranded in Persian Gulf. 10 sailors killed in 21 vessel attacks. First French-owned ship (CMA CGM) passed through; Japanese LNG carrier escaped. Asian nations hit hardest — 90% of Hormuz oil/gas bound for Asia. Philippines declared energy emergency, fuel prices up 20% in China.

news.title: Uffizi Gallery hit by cyber-attack, security data compromised
news.summary: Hackers infiltrated IT systems of Florence's Uffizi Galleries, allegedly extracting access codes, internal maps, CCTV/alarm locations. Ransom demand sent to director's phone. Museum denies art collection was at risk, says security systems were isolated.

news.title: AI copyright litigator Edelson escalates war on OpenAI and Google
news.summary: Attorney Jay Edelson, called a "leech" by Sam Altman and calling Altman "Lex Luthor" in return, continues filing copyright suits against AI companies. Courts and juries increasingly sympathetic to plaintiffs.

news.title: Perplexity sued over privacy: Meta and Google trackers embedded in AI search
news.summary: Proposed class action claims Perplexity embedded Meta and Google trackers inside its AI search engine, sharing user conversations even in Incognito mode. Paid users who enabled "Incognito" still had conversations shared with email addresses and identifiers.

news.title: Amazon Luna relaunches with EA FC 26 free for Prime subscribers
news.summary: Amazon's cloud gaming service Luna has been revamped with a social gaming focus ("GameNight"). EA Sports FC 26 is now playable for Prime subscribers at no extra cost, launching alongside 70+ games at $6.99/month.

news.title: PlayStation acquires Cinemersive Labs (ML/Computer Vision)
news.summary: Sony Interactive Entertainment acquired Cinemersive Labs, a machine learning and computer vision company. The team joins SIE's Visual Computing Group to advance state-of-the-art visual computing within games.

news.title: State of Decay 3: playtests coming in 2026
news.summary: Undead Labs confirms State of Decay 3 playtests are scheduled for 2026, marking the first signs of real progress since the game's 2020 announcement.

news.title: Switch 2 microSD Express: M.2 SSD mod demonstrated
news.summary: A user successfully connected an M.2 SSD to the Switch 2 through the microSD Express slot. While impractical for daily use, it proves the high-speed interface can handle full SSD bandwidth.

news.title: ClawSafety: "Safe" LLMs, Unsafe Agents
news.summary: Benchmark of 120 adversarial test scenarios across software engineering, finance, healthcare, law, and DevOps. Tests 5 frontier LLMs as agent backends with 2,520 sandboxed trials. Attack success rates range from 40-75%, with skill file injection consistently the most dangerous vector.

news.title: Measuring and Mitigating Cognitive Bias in Large Language Models
news.summary: Introduces LLM CogBias benchmark covering 4 families of cognitive biases (Judgment, Information Processing, Social, Response). Shows biases are encoded as linearly separable directions in activation space. Activation steering reduces bias by 26-32% while preserving downstream capability.

news.title: When Reward Hacking Rebounds: Understanding and Mitigating It with Representation-Level Signals
news.summary: Identifies a reproducible 3-phase rebound pattern in reward hacking during RL for LLMs: models first attempt hacking but fail, retreat to legitimate solving, then rebound with qualitatively different strategies. Proposes "Advantage Modification" that integrates shortcut concept scores into GRPO to suppress hacking at training time.

news.title: Wired for Overconfidence: A Mechanistic Perspective on Inflated Verbalized Confidence in LLMs
news.summary: Circuit-level mechanistic analysis of why LLMs are "confidently wrong." Finds a compact set of MLP blocks and attention heads in middle-to-late layers consistently writes confidence-inflation signals. Targeted inference-time interventions on these circuits substantially improve calibration.

news.title: Google AI Pro plan storage doubled to 5TB
news.summary: The $19.99/month Google AI Pro plan now includes 5TB of storage, up from 2TB.

news.title: Google releases Gemma 4 with Apache 2.0 license
news.summary: Google's Gemma 4 open model switches to the permissive Apache 2.0 license, ditching the restrictive custom license of previous versions. Performance improvements included.

news.title: Google Vids upgrades with Veo 3.1 and Lyria 3 AI avatars
news.summary: Google Vids now supports Veo 3.1 and Lyria 3 models, adding customizable AI-generated avatars, screen recording Chrome extension, and direct YouTube upload.

news.title: Holo3 breaks computer use frontier on Hugging Face
news.summary: New Holo3 model pushes computer use agent capabilities, published by H Company on Hugging Face alongside Falcon Perception from TII UAE.

news.title: IBM Granite 4.0 3B Vision for enterprise document AI
news.summary: IBM releases a compact multimodal model optimized for enterprise document understanding, just 3B parameters but targeting production workloads.

news.title: Mistral releases Voxtral TTS: open-weights text-to-speech
news.summary: Mistral AI launched Voxtral TTS, a frontier open-weights text-to-speech model that is fast, instantly adaptable, and produces lifelike speech for voice agents.

news.title: OpenAI acquires TBPNCompany; Codex launches pay-as-you-go pricing
news.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPNCompany on Apr 2 and simultaneously launched flexible pay-as-you-go pricing for Codex, targeting team adoption.

news.title: OpenAI raises $122 billion to accelerate next phase of AI
news.summary: OpenAI closes a massive $122B funding round, one of the largest in tech history, signaling investor confidence in the next wave of AI development.

news.title: Penguin Random House sues OpenAI over copyright in Germany
news.summary: Penguin Random House filed a lawsuit in Munich accusing ChatGPT of copying a popular German children's book series ("Coconut the Dragon"), generating virtually indistinguishable text and artwork.

news.title: TRL v1.0 released: Post-training library for RL
news.summary: Hugging Face releases TRL v1.0, a major update to their reinforcement learning post-training library designed to keep pace with the rapidly evolving RL landscape.

news.title: Do Emotions in Prompts Matter? Effects of Emotional Framing on Large Language Models
news.summary: Systematic study across 6 benchmark domains finds emotional prefixes produce only small accuracy changes. Effects more variable in socially grounded tasks. Introduces EmotionRL - adaptive selection yields reliable gains over fixed emotional prompting. Concludes emotional tone is "weak and input-dependent signal."

news.title: Novel Memory Forgetting Techniques for Autonomous AI Agents
news.summary: Introduces adaptive budgeted forgetting framework for long-horizon conversational agents. LOCOMO benchmark shows performance drops from 0.455 to 0.05 across stages from uncontrolled memory. New approach integrates recency, frequency, and semantic alignment to maintain F1 above 0.583 baseline while preventing false memory propagation.

news.title: Targeted Reverse Update for Efficient Multimodal Recommendation Unlearning
news.summary: GDPR-compliant unlearning for multimodal recommendation systems. Shows deleted-data influence concentrates unevenly across ranking behavior, modality branches, and network layers. TRU framework performs coordinated interventions: ranking fusion gate, branch-wise modality scaling, capacity-aware layer isolation.

news.title: Uncertainty-Gated Language Assistance for Reinforcement Learning (ASK)
news.summary: Combines smaller LMs with trained RL policies for OOD generalization. Uses Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty assessment - only queries LM when uncertainty exceeds threshold. In-domain shows no improvement, but transfer tasks achieve 0.95 reward on FrozenLake vs random OOD behavior.

news.title: User Turn Generation as a Probe of Interaction Awareness in Language Models
news.summary: Proposes testing whether LLMs encode "interaction awareness" by having models generate the user's next turn. Found interaction awareness is decoupled from task accuracy - Qwen3.5 family goes from 41% to 96.8% on GSM8K, but follow-up rates remain near zero under deterministic generation. High-temperature sampling reveals latent awareness (22% follow-up rates).

news.title: Codex Pay-As-You-Go (Recap)
news.summary: Usage-based pricing for Codex-only seats. ChatGPT Business $20/mo. 2M weekly users, 6x growth since Jan.

news.title: Google Gemma 4 Released: Apache 2.0 License
news.summary: 4 sizes: E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense. Built from Gemini 3 tech. 31B ranks #3 open model, 26B #6 on Arena AI. Outperforms models 20x its size. 400M+ downloads. Native vision/audio, agentic workflows, function-calling.

news.title: Google Vids Veo 3.1 + Lyria 3 (Recap)
news.summary: AI avatar customization, Chrome screen recording, direct YouTube upload.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN (Recap)
news.summary: Tech talk show "Silicon Valley's newest obsession" joins OpenAI. Editorial independence preserved. Fidji Simo announces deal.

news.title: 34-Country Virtual Summit on Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: Australia convenes emergency virtual summit with 34 countries to address Hormuz crisis. Fuel prices spike ahead of Easter. Govt waving "big stick" at gas exporters.

news.title: Iran's Two Largest Steel Plants Shut Down from Airstrikes
news.summary: Major steel facilities halt operations due to ongoing US-Israel strikes. Iranian bridge cut in half, 8 killed. Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi suffers suspected heart attack in prison.

news.title: Trump Fires US Attorney General Pam Bondi
news.summary: AG Bondi removed from position, overshadowed by Justice Department's handling of Jeffrey Epstein investigation. 15-minute announcement marks major Trump admin shakeup.

news.title: Trump Threatens to Quit NATO Over Iran War Non-Support
news.summary: Trump tells Telegraph he's "beyond reconsideration" on NATO membership after allies refused to join US-Israel operations against Iran. "Nato is a one-way street," says SecState Rubio. US military budget is 62% of NATO total.

news.title: US Lifts Sanctions on Venezuelan Interim Leader
news.summary: Delcy Rodríguez sanctions removed after Maduro seized in commando raid. Latest sign of warming US-Venezuela relations under Trump administration.

news.title: AI-Generated Comments Derail California Pollution Plan
news.summary: California regulators killed gas furnace/water heater pollution fee proposal after 20,000+ opposing comments generated by single AI platform. Some comments used names of people unaware their names were used.

news.title: Data Centers Driving US Natural Gas Boom

news.title: "Therefore I am. I Think" - Decisions Before Reasoning in LLMs
news.summary: Linear probes decode tool-calling decisions from activations BEFORE reasoning tokens are generated. When steering changes the decision, CoT rationalizes the flip rather than resisting it. Evidence that reasoning models encode action choices before deliberating in text.

news.title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers
news.summary: 42.4GB benchmark with 2K+ real-world files for multimodal file management agents. Best models achieve only 48.3% on user profiling. Provides 46.1K annotated trajectories for step-wise failure diagnosis. Identifies multimodal perception and evidence grounding as primary bottlenecks.

news.title: NARCBench: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Multi-Agent Interpretability
news.summary: First white-box multi-agent interpretability benchmark for collusion detection. Achieves 1.00 AUROC in-distribution, 0.60-0.86 AUROC zero-shot transfer. Signal localizes at token level - colluding agent's activations spike when processing encoded partner messages.

news.title: OmniMem: Autoresearch-Guided Discovery of Lifelong Multimodal Agent Memory
news.summary: Autonomous research pipeline discovers memory system by running ~50 experiments without human intervention. Starts from naive baseline (F1=0.117) and achieves +411% improvement on LoCoMo benchmark. Bug fixes, architecture changes, and prompt engineering each exceed all hyperparameter tuning combined.

news.title: YC-Bench: Benchmarking AI Agents for Long-Term Planning and Consistent Execution
news.summary: Startup simulation benchmark over 1-year horizon, hundreds of turns. Agent manages employees, contracts, profitability. Claude Opus 4.6 achieves $1.27M, GLM-5 hits $1.21M at 11x lower inference cost. Adversarial client detection accounts for 47% of bankruptcies.

news.title: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 Released
news.summary: Smartest Claude model upgraded: 1M token context window (beta), state-of-the-art on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and Humanity's Last Exam. Beats GPT-5.2 by 144 Elo on GDPval-AA. Same pricing: $5/$25 per million tokens.

news.title: "Therefore I am. I Think" - Early Decisions Shape Chain-of-Thought
news.summary: Evidence that reasoning models encode action choices before deliberating in text. Linear probe decodes tool-calling decisions from pre-generation activations with high confidence, sometimes before any reasoning token. Activation steering flips behavior 7-79% - CoT often rationalizes the flip rather than resisting.

news.title: AMST: Adversarial Moral Stress Testing of LLMs
news.summary: Stress-based framework for ethical robustness under multi-turn adversarial interactions. Evaluates variance, tail risk, and temporal drift across rounds. Tests LLaMA-3-8B, GPT-4o, DeepSeek-v3. Reveals degradation patterns invisible to single-round benchmarks. Robustness depends on distributional stability, not average performance.

news.title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers
news.summary: New benchmark for multimodal file management in user-centric environments. 42.4 GB data, 2K+ real-world files, 581 QA pairs testing search, perception, and multi-step reasoning. Even best commercial models achieve only 48.3% accuracy in user profiling. 46.1K annotated trajectories for step-wise failure diagnosis.

news.title: NARCBench: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Interpretability
news.summary: First benchmark for detecting collusion in multi-agent LLM systems using internal activations. Five probing techniques achieve 1.00 AUROC in-distribution, 0.60-0.86 AUROC zero-shot transfer to different scenarios including steganographic card-counting. Signal localized at token level.

news.title: OmniMem: Autoresearch-Guided Discovery of Lifelong Multimodal Agent Memory
news.summary: Autonomous research pipeline discovers OmniMem framework for lifelong AI agent memory. Starting from baseline F1=0.117, achieves +411% improvement on LoCoMo (0.598) and +214% on Mem-Gallery (0.797) through ~50 autonomous experiments diagnosing failures, proposing architectural changes, and fixing bugs.

news.title: Covertly Improving Intelligibility with Data-Driven Speech Timing
news.summary: Uses reverse-correlation to show speech rate adjustments follow "scissor-like" temporal pattern for vowel contrast. Builds data-driven TTS algorithm that replicates this structure. Targeted slowing improves comprehension while going unnoticed by listeners.

news.title: Extending MONA: Reproduction, Learned Approval, and Reward-Hacking Mitigation
news.summary: Reproduces MONA's 91.5%→0% reward-hacking reduction and introduces modular learned-approval suite. Tests oracle, noisy, misspecified, and calibrated approval mechanisms. Best calibrated learned-overseer achieves zero hacking.

news.title: Physiological and Semantic Patterns in Medical Teams Using ITS
news.summary: Analyzes 4 medical dyads using intelligent tutoring system. Semantic shifts correlate with physiological synchrony peaks. High synchrony = exploratory language use. Successful teams sync during discovery; unsuccessful teams sync during uncertainty.

news.title: Structured Intent as Protocol-Like Layer: Cross-Model Robustness & Weak-Model Compensation

news.title: The Triadic Cognitive Architecture: Bounding Autonomous Action via Spatio-Temporal and Epistemic Friction
news.summary: Proposes a unified mathematical framework that grounds machine reasoning in continuous-time physics. Introduces "Cognitive Friction" to bound AI deliberation under latency, congestion, and ambiguous evidence. Uses HJB-motivated stopping boundaries instead of heuristic stop-tokens.

news.title: Triadic Cognitive Architecture: Bounding Autonomous Action via Friction
news.summary: New framework grounds machine reasoning in continuous-time physics. Synthesizes nonlinear filtering, Riemannian routing geometry, and optimal control. news.title: Nothing Planning AI Smart Glasses for H1 2027
news.summary: Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reports Nothing will launch AI smart glasses in first half of 2027. Built-in cameras, microphones, speakers. AI processing offloaded to smartphone and cloud.

news.title: OpenAI Building Unified AI Superapp
news.summary: Single system combining ChatGPT, Codex, browsing, and agentic capabilities. Consumer scale becomes front door for enterprise.

news.title: OpenAI Raises $122B at $852B Valuation
news.summary: Largest AI funding round in history. Anchored by Amazon, NVIDIA, SoftBank, Microsoft. Revenue now $2B/month. ChatGPT has 900M weekly users, 50M subscribers. Building unified "AI superapp".

news.title: AIGENIE: AI-Driven Psychometric Scale Development
news.summary: R package that automates psychological scale development. LLM generates candidate items → EGA network analysis → validated item pools in silico. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, HuggingFace, local models. Fully offline mode available.

news.title: ChatGPT Search Ads Pilot: $100M ARR in 6 Weeks
news.summary: Search usage nearly tripled in a year. Ads pilot reached $100M ARR in under six weeks. ChatGPT has 6x monthly visits vs next largest AI app, 4x total AI time spent.

news.title: Hugging Face TRL v1.0 Released
news.summary: Post-training library built for RLHF and alignment workflows. Major update for the evolving AI training landscape.

news.title: IBM Granite 4.0 3B Vision Released
news.summary: Compact multimodal model optimized for enterprise document understanding. 3B parameters for efficient deployment.

news.title: OpenAI Enterprise Hits 40% of Revenue
news.summary: Enterprise now 40% of revenue, on track to reach parity with consumer by end of 2026. APIs process 15B+ tokens/minute. GPT-5.4 driving record agentic workflow engagement.

news.title: OpenAI Expands Infrastructure: Own Chip + Multi-Cloud
news.summary: Beyond Nvidia: own chip with Broadcom, plus AMD, AWS Trainium, Cerebras. Multi-cloud: Microsoft, Oracle, AWS, CoreWeave, Google Cloud. $4.7B revolving credit facility from global bank syndicate.

news.title: OpenAI Raises $122 Billion at $852 Billion Valuation
news.summary: Largest AI funding round in history. Anchored by Amazon, NVIDIA, SoftBank, Microsoft. Co-led by SoftBank, a16z, D.E. Shaw, MGX, TPG. Revenue now $2B/month. ChatGPT has 900M weekly users, 50M subscribers. Codex 2M users, up 5x in 3 months. Building unified "AI superapp".

news.title: China Poised to Benefit From Global Green Energy Shift
news.summary: As energy panic grips Asia, China positioned to reap rewards of global surge in EVs, batteries, and solar uptake. Beijing's intervention has cushioned domestic oil price blow while preparing for green transition.

news.title: Iran Threatens US Big Tech Companies as War Escalates
news.summary: Iran has issued threats targeting Microsoft, Google, Apple and other US tech companies in the Middle East region. This follows Trump's warning to countries to "go get your own oil" from Strait of Hormuz as US signals potential exit from conflict.

news.title: Judge Halts Trump's $400M White House Ballroom Project
news.summary: Federal court orders Trump administration to suspend work on $400 million ballroom on demolished East Wing site. Judge rules Trump is "steward" not "owner" of White House - congressional approval required.

news.title: Putin Gains $100M/Day From Oil Price Spike
news.summary: Analysis shows every $10 increase in oil prices benefits "Russia Inc" by ~$100 million daily. Kremlin was facing budget crunch before Iran war, now receiving massive windfall from energy crisis.

news.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Triggers Global Fuel Crisis
news.summary: Strait of Hormuz remains closed, pushing petrol prices above $4/gallon in US for first time since 2022. Countries worldwide implementing emergency measures: cycling to work, cutting school hours, staying home. Australia's fuel excise cut takes effect today.

news.title: Trump to Close Historic Border Road
news.summary: US officials shutting down historic Border Road between US and Canada effective July 1, citing irregular migration and drug smuggling. Ends decades of cross-border cooperation.

news.title: Arm Now Making Its Own AI Chips
news.summary: Chip design firm Arm announces it's building its own AI CPUs. Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare among first customers. Major shift from licensing model to direct hardware competition.

news.title: Axios npm Supply Chain Attack Hits Enterprise
news.summary: Hacker hijacked Axios JavaScript library maintainer account. Versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 contained remote access trojan. Thousands of enterprises potentially affected.

news.title: Nvidia Investing $26B in Open-Weight AI Models
news.summary: Filings reveal Nvidia's massive investment to build open-weight AI models. Positions infrastructure giant to compete directly with OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek.

news.title: Oracle Lays Off Thousands While Raising $50B for AI Infrastructure
news.summary: Oracle cutting thousands of workers while simultaneously raising $45-50 billion for AI infrastructure buildout. Company employed 162,000 as of May 2025. Highlights dramatic corporate priority shift toward AI.

news.title: Proton Launches Workspace Bundle with Encrypted Video
news.summary: Proton Workspace bundle includes new end-to-end encrypted Proton Meet video chat. Privacy-first alternative to Google Workspace and Microsoft 365.

news.title: Yann LeCun Raises $1B for Physical World AI Startup AMI
news.summary: Meta's former chief AI scientist launches AMI with $1B funding. Argues human-level AI comes from mastering physical world, not language. Direct challenge to LLM-centric approach.

news.title: Anthropic "Mythos" Model Leaked
news.summary: Security lapse revealed details of Anthropic's next flagship model named "Mythos", along with internal info about an invite-only CEO event.

news.title: Apple Building AI App Store with Siri Extensions
news.summary: iOS 27 will introduce third-party Siri Extensions with a dedicated App Store section, effectively creating an AI App Store for chatbot integrations.

news.title: Google TurboQuant: 6x Memory Reduction
news.summary: Google's new compression algorithm reduces LLM memory usage by 6x with zero accuracy loss, enabling more efficient AI deployment.

news.title: Microsoft Copilot Cowork Gets Claude Integration
news.summary: Microsoft's Frontier Program now offers Copilot Cowork with Claude integration for long-running multi-step tasks, plus new Researcher and Critique agents.

news.title: Mistral Voxtral TTS Released
news.summary: Mistral released Voxtral, a frontier open-weights text-to-speech model designed for fast, lifelike voice agent applications.

news.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 mini and nano
news.summary: OpenAI introduced smaller, more efficient GPT-5.4 variants targeting edge deployments and cost-sensitive applications.

news.title: Oracle Lays Off Thousands While Raising $50B for AI Infrastructure
news.summary: Oracle cutting thousands of workers while simultaneously raising $45-50 billion for AI infrastructure buildout, highlighting the shift in corporate priorities toward AI.

news.title: Trump Guts NRC as Tech Pushes Nuclear for Data Centers
news.summary: Nuclear Regulatory Commission lost 400+ staff, largely safety workers. Former chair: "Regulator no longer independent" as tech companies push nuclear for AI energy.

news.title: D2Skill: Dynamic Dual-Granularity Skill Bank for Agentic RL
news.summary: Novel framework for agentic RL with dual-granularity skills: task skills for high-level guidance + step skills for fine-grained correction. Uses hindsight utility signals from paired rollouts. +10-20 points on ALFWorld/WebShop with Qwen2.5-7B. Skills transfer across settings.

news.title: Entropic Claim Resolution: Uncertainty-Driven Evidence Selection for RAG
news.summary: Paradigm shift: instead of retrieving what's RELEVANT, retrieve what's DISCRIMINATIVE. ECR algorithm frames RAG as entropy minimization over competing hypotheses. Uses Expected Entropy Reduction (EER) as decision-theoretic criterion for evidence selection.

news.title: IF4: Adaptive Block-Scaled Data Types for LLM Quantization
news.summary: Novel 4-bit format that adaptively switches between FP4 and INT4 per 16-value block, using unused sign bit in scale factor. Outperforms NVFP4 in both quantized training and post-training quantization. Hardware-efficient MAC unit design provided.

news.title: MonitorBench: Comprehensive Benchmark for CoT Monitorability
news.summary: 1,514 test instances across 19 tasks measuring when CoT faithfully reflects model reasoning. Critical finding: NEGATIVE relationship between model capability and monitorability. Stress-tests show up to 30% monitorability degradation. More capable ≠ more interpretable.

news.title: ScholScan: Benchmarking MLLMs on Scan-Oriented Academic Paper Reasoning
news.summary: New benchmark challenges MLLMs to read entire papers like human researchers, scanning for consistency issues. 1,800 questions across 715 papers in 13 scientific domains. Critical finding: RAG yields NO improvement—systematic deficiencies in current MLLMs exposed.

news.title: The Scaffold Effect: How Prompt Framing Drives Apparent Multimodal Gains in Clinical VLMs
news.summary: 12 clinical VLMs tested on neuroimaging datasets with NO reliable diagnostic signal. 70-80% of apparent gains came from merely MENTIONING MRI in the prompt—no actual data needed. All models fabricated neuroimaging justifications. Preference alignment collapsed performance to random.

news.title: Anthropic Sues Pentagon Over Military Risk Designation
news.summary: Anthropic seeks injunction against its designation as military supply-chain risk, with Judge Rita Lin expected to rule soon.

news.title: Google Gemini 3.1 Family Expansion
news.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini 3.1 Pro, Flash, Flash-Lite, and Deep Think variants, plus Lyria 3 Pro for music generation and Veo 3.1 for video.

news.title: Intel Arc Pro B70 GPU for AI
news.summary: Intel announced Arc Pro B70 "Big Battlemage" with 32GB VRAM starting at $949, targeting AI workloads.

news.title: Mistral Launches Voxtral TTS
news.summary: Mistral released Voxtral, a frontier open-weights text-to-speech model designed for fast, lifelike voice agent applications.

news.title: OpenAI to Acquire Astral
news.summary: OpenAI announced acquisition of Astral, signaling major expansion into new AI capabilities.

news.title: BREAKING: Trump Tells UK and Allies "Go Get Your Own Oil" from Hormuz
news.summary: US President tells nations unable to get jet fuel "the USA won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us." Major shift in US foreign policy as Iran war continues. Strait of Hormuz remains blocked for many US allies.

news.title: China Bans "Bone Ash Apartments" for Cremated Remains
news.summary: New regulation prevents mourners from storing cremated remains in empty apartments instead of cemeteries. Addresses cultural trend of using real estate for ancestral remains amid cemetery shortages.

news.title: Eurovision Launches First-Ever Asia Edition
news.summary: Broadcasters from 10 countries including South Korea and Philippines will participate. Cultural expansion of European entertainment format to Asian markets.

news.title: Trump Sees "America First" Opportunity in NASA Moon Mission
news.summary: Competitive edge with China and possible lunar gold rush are twin goals behind Artemis II, experts tell BBC. NASA's moon mission framed as strategic competition rather than scientific exploration.

news.title: D2Skill: Dynamic Dual-Granularity Skill Bank for Agentic RL
news.summary: Organizes reusable RL experience into task skills (high-level guidance) and step skills (fine-grained decisions/error correction). Uses hindsight utility signals for both skill updating and policy optimization. Improves success rates by 10-20 points on ALFWorld and WebShop benchmarks with Qwen2.5-7B.

news.title: Entropic Claim Resolution: Uncertainty-Driven Evidence Selection for RAG
news.summary: Reframes RAG reasoning as entropy minimization over competing semantic answer hypotheses. Selects evidence by maximizing Expected Entropy Reduction (EER)—retrieving what is most DISCRIMINATIVE, not just most relevant. Dynamically terminates when reaching epistemic sufficiency (H <= epsilon).

news.title: MonitorBench: Comprehensive Benchmark for Chain-of-Thought Monitorability
news.summary: 1,514 test instances across 19 tasks to evaluate when CoTs faithfully reflect decision-critical factors. Key finding: closed-source LLMs show LOWER monitorability, and there's a NEGATIVE relationship between monitorability and model capability. Under stress-tests, monitorability drops by up to 30%.

news.title: Prompt Framing Drives Apparent Multimodal Gains in Clinical VLMs (Scaffold Effect)
news.summary: Evaluates 12 VLMs on clinical neuroimaging with NO reliable diagnostic signal. Smaller VLMs show up to 58% F1 gains—but 70-80% comes from merely MENTIONING MRI in the prompt (scaffold effect), not actual imaging data. All models fabricate neuroimaging-grounded justifications.

news.title: ScholScan: Benchmarking MLLMs on Scan-Oriented Academic Paper Reasoning
news.summary: New benchmark for academic paper reasoning with 1,800 questions across 715 papers in 13 natural-science domains. Asks models to scan entire papers like human researchers to identify consistency issues. RAG methods yield NO significant improvements—revealing systematic deficiencies in current MLLMs on scan-oriented tasks.

news.title: Huge Fires at Russian Oil Facilities Following Ukraine Strikes
news.summary: BBC Verify examines satellite images showing damage to three key Russian oil export facilities near the Baltic Sea. Ukraine's Zelensky says allies asked him to scale back attacks on Russian energy as prices soar due to the Iran war.

news.title: Kid Rock Sparks US Army Probe After Helicopter Flyby at Mansion
news.summary: Country rock artist posted footage of himself saluting a military helicopter from his outdoor pool. US Army has launched investigation into unauthorized use of military aircraft.

news.title: NASA Begins Countdown for Artemis II Moon Mission
news.summary: Artemis II mission preparing to send four astronauts around the Moon. First crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 in 1972. Astronauts reveal hopes, fears, and family sacrifices ahead of historic flight.

news.title: Netanyahu: Military Aims in Iran "Beyond Halfway" Complete; Kuwaiti Tanker Attacked
news.summary: Israel's PM says objectives in Iran are more than 50% achieved. Meanwhile, a Kuwaiti oil tanker was attacked in Dubai, and a refinery in Israel was set on fire by debris from an intercepted missile. Conflict continues across Lebanon and the region.

news.title: RBA Announces New Debit/Credit Surcharge Reforms for October 2026
news.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia unveils payment reforms to reduce surcharges on debit and credit card transactions. Changes take effect October 2026. Meanwhile, eSafety watchdog says social media firms must better enforce under-16s ban.

news.title: Anthropic "Mythos" Model Leaked via Security Lapse
news.summary: Unsecured data store revealed next model codename plus CEO event details. Security incident at one of the most critical AI companies.

news.title: Apple Has "Complete Access" to Gemini for Model Distillation
news.summary: Apple can use Gemini in data centers to train smaller "student" AI models for on-device use via distillation. Requires less computing power than training from scratch.

news.title: Apple Intelligence Mistakenly Launched in China, Quickly Pulled
news.summary: Chinese users briefly saw Apple Intelligence features on their iPhones. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman confirmed it launched "in error" and Apple has taken it offline. China requires Apple to partner with local companies like Alibaba to power AI features—suggesting Apple's native AI isn't approved yet.

news.title: Apple Siri Extensions Could Create AI App Store
news.summary: iOS 27 will let users install third-party AI chatbots inside Siri with dedicated App Store section. Apple opening Siri and Apple Intelligence to third-party services beyond ChatGPT.

news.title: Google AI Search Accused of Disclosing Epstein Survivors' Personal Information
news.summary: Class action lawsuit alleges Google failed to remove or block access to sensitive materials. Plaintiff says Trump administration and Google wrongfully disclosed survivors' data.

news.title: Google TurboQuant: 6x Memory Reduction with Zero Accuracy Loss
news.summary: New compression algorithm shrinks LLM data storage by at least six times without accuracy degradation. Major efficiency breakthrough for AI deployment.

news.title: Microsoft Copilot Cowork Now Available in Frontier Program
news.summary: Microsoft's Frontier Program now includes Claude for long-running multi-step tasks, an improved Researcher agent, and Critique feature where GPT drafts and Claude edits for accuracy. Full GPT+Claude collaboration workflow.

news.title: Trump Guts Nuclear Regulatory Commission as Silicon Valley Pushes Nuclear for Data Centers
news.summary: NRC lost 400+ staff, largely those working on safety. Tech industry hyping next-gen reactors for data center energy demand. Former NRC chair: "The regulator is no longer independent—we don't know whose interests it serves."

news.title: Israel Passes Death Penalty Law for "Nationalist Killings"
news.summary: Knesset passed law making death penalty the "default sentence" for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks. Major shift in Israeli judicial policy amid ongoing regional conflict.

news.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Min Aung Hlaing Nominated as President
news.summary: Military leader sanctioned by Western countries is now certain to become president, consolidating junta power. Meanwhile, Russian oil tanker reaches Cuba after Trump appears to loosen blockade.

news.title: Social Media Platforms Under Investigation for Under-16s Ban
news.summary: Five major social media platforms under investigation for potential violations of Australia's under-16s social media ban. Regulators examining compliance mechanisms.

news.title: Spain Closes Airspace to US Iran War Aircraft
news.summary: Spanish government denied US use of two jointly-run military bases in Andalusia and closed national airspace to American aircraft involved in the Iran conflict. First major NATO split over the war.

news.title: Trump Threatens to "Obliterate" Kharg Island; Australia Cuts Fuel Excise
news.summary: US-Iran war enters second month. Trump mused on seizing Iran's oil supplies or "obliterating" energy assets. Spain closed airspace to US aircraft involved in the war. Australia's PM Albanese announced fuel excise cut to combat soaring petrol prices, seeking "certainty" from Trump on war aims.

news.title: AutoB2G: Automated Building-Grid Co-Simulation from Natural Language
news.summary: LLM-driven agentic framework (SOCIA) that generates, executes, and refines building-to-grid simulators purely from natural language descriptions. Extends CityLearn V2 for B2G interaction. Codebase organized as DAG to guide LLM through module dependencies. Enables non-experts to create working RL simulation environments.

news.title: BeSafe-Bench: Agent Safety Benchmark Shows Best <40% Safe Completion
news.summary: Comprehensive benchmark for behavioral safety risks in situated agents across Web, Mobile, Embodied VLM, and Embodied VLA domains. Uses functional environments with 9 safety risk categories and hybrid evaluation (rule-based + LLM-as-judge). Critical finding: best agent completes fewer than 40% of tasks safely; strong task performance often correlates with severe safety violations.

news.title: CADSmith: Multi-Agent CAD with Programmatic Geometric Validation
news.summary: Text-to-CAD pipeline using nested correction loops: inner loop for execution errors, outer loop with OpenCASCADE kernel for exact geometric validation (bounding box, volume, solid validity) plus VLM judge for visual assessment. 100% execution rate, median IoU improves from 0.81 to 0.96. Uses RAG over API docs instead of fine-tuning.

news.title: Knowledge Engineering + LLMs for Total Airport Management
news.summary: Dual-stage fusion of symbolic Knowledge Engineering and LLMs to construct domain-grounded Knowledge Graphs for airport operations. Scaffolded fusion: expert KE structures guide LLM prompts for knowledge triple discovery. Document-level processing improves non-linear dependency recovery. Every extraction anchored to ground source for traceability.

news.title: PAPO: Process-Aware Policy Optimization Solves Reward Hacking
news.summary: Addresses a critical RLHF problem: outcome reward models (ORM) lose signal as groups become uniformly correct, while process reward models (PRM) cause reward hacking. PAPO decouples advantages into outcome component (correctness anchor) and process component (reasoning quality) with separate normalization. Achieves 51.3% vs 46.3% on OlympiadBench compared to ORM baseline.

news.title: Anthropic's Next Model "Mythos" Leaked via Security Lapse
news.summary: Fortune reports an unsecured data store revealed Anthropic's upcoming model codename plus CEO event details. Security incident at one of the most critical AI companies.

news.title: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Live - Natural Audio AI
news.summary: DeepMind releases enhanced audio AI that's more natural and reliable. Part of Gemini 3.1 family including Pro, Flash-Lite, and Deep Think variants.

news.title: Judge Lin Rules for Anthropic: "Illegal First Amendment Retaliation"
news.summary: Court rules punishing Anthropic with military supply-chain risk designation is unconstitutional. Major victory for AI company against Trump administration.

news.title: Microsoft Copilot Cowork Launches with Claude Integration
news.summary: Microsoft's Frontier Program now includes Claude for long-running, multi-step tasks, an improved Researcher agent for information gathering, and a new Critique feature where GPT drafts and Claude edits for accuracy.

news.title: Mistral Voxtral TTS - Open-Weights Voice Model
news.summary: Frontier text-to-speech model that's fast, instantly adaptable, produces lifelike speech for voice agents. 68.4% win rate vs ElevenLabs in blind tests.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires Astral (Python Tools uv, Ruff, ty)
news.summary: OpenAI acquires makers of core Python developer tools to integrate with Codex. Codex has 2M+ weekly active users, 3x growth this year.

news.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 mini and nano
news.summary: Smaller efficient GPT-5.4 variants: mini is 2x faster and approaches full model on SWE-Bench, nano is cheapest for classification/extraction. mini $0.75/1M input, nano $0.20/1M.

news.title: Quiet Start - US Overnight Hours
news.summary: Healthcare & Biotech sector quiet as US still in overnight hours. Biotech companies made substantial R&D spending cuts this year according to Fierce Biotech. No major announcements yet. Expect activity as US business day begins.

news.title: CEOs Blaming AI for Mass Job Cuts
news.summary: More tech leaders pointing to AI-driven job cuts and need for investment cash. BBC analysis shows pattern of CEOs using AI as cover for broader restructuring. Australia's cost-of-living fueling flexible work demands.

news.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Nominated as President; China Tech Flocks to HK
news.summary: Military leader Min Aung Hlaing, sanctioned by West, certain to become president. Meanwhile, Chinese tech companies racing to set up in Hong Kong as springboard for global expansion—testing products in territory before worldwide launch.

news.title: Russian Oil Tanker Reaches Cuba; US Radar Jet Damaged in Saudi
news.summary: Russian tanker arrives in Cuban waters after Trump says he has "no problem" with it. Photos show heavily damaged US radar jet at Saudi base. Gulf of Mexico becoming second front for energy geopolitics.

news.title: Trump Threatens to "Obliterate" Iran's Energy Facilities
news.summary: US president escalates rhetoric, threatening to destroy Iran's energy infrastructure if deal not reached "shortly". Israeli refinery set on fire by debris from intercepted missile. Attacks continue across Iran, Lebanon, Gulf region.

news.title: Bitcoin Market "Boring" - Yield-Seeking Investors to Blame
news.summary: Yield-hungry investors limiting price swings through structured products and yield strategies. Market stuck in low-volatility regime despite geopolitical turmoil.

news.title: Bitcoin Recovers to $67,400 After Houthis Enter Iran War
news.summary: BTC dipped below $65,200 as conflict expanded to fifth week with widest escalation yet. Iran-backed forces opened new front, US ground troops arriving. Recovery suggests market absorbing geopolitical risk.

news.title: Hyperliquid Tokyo Traders Get 200ms Latency Edge
news.summary: Glassnode research shows Hyperliquid validators cluster in AWS Tokyo alongside Binance, BitMEX, KuCoin. Proximity gives nearby traders significant speed advantage in order execution.

news.title: AutoB2G: LLM-Driven Automated Building-Grid Co-Simulation
news.summary: Automated framework completing building-grid co-simulation solely from natural-language task descriptions. Uses SOCIA framework with codebase organized as DAG to guide LLM. Coordinates Building-to-Grid interactions for grid-side performance optimization.

news.title: BeSafe-Bench: Unveiling Behavioral Safety Risks of Situated Agents
news.summary: Comprehensive safety benchmark for agents in functional environments across Web, Mobile, Embodied VLM/VLA. Nine categories of safety-critical risks. 13 popular agents evaluated—best completes <40% tasks while fully adhering to safety constraints. Strong task performance often coincides with severe safety violations.

news.title: CADSmith: Multi-Agent CAD Generation with Programmatic Geometric Validation
news.summary: Multi-agent pipeline generating CadQuery code from natural language. Uses nested correction loops: inner for execution errors, outer for geometric validation via OpenCASCADE + VLM judge. Achieves 100% execution rate (from 95%), median IoU from 0.81 to 0.96.

news.title: PAPO: Process-Aware Policy Optimization via Decoupled Advantage Normalization
news.summary: New RL method that integrates process-level evaluation into GRPO. Solves reward hacking by decoupling outcome component (correctness) from process component (reasoning quality). Reaches 51.3% on OlympiadBench vs 46.3% for ORM, continuing to improve as ORM plateaus.

news.title: Semi-Automated Knowledge Engineering for Total Airport Management
news.summary: Framework combining symbolic Knowledge Engineering with LLMs to build domain Knowledge Graphs for airport operations. Scaffolded fusion where KE structures guide LLM prompts. Document-level processing improves non-linear dependency recovery.

news.title: Former Prime Minister Khadga Prasad Oli Arrested
news.summary: Nepal's former PM arrested amid political crisis. Instability in South Asian democracy as region faces economic pressures from Middle East war spillover.

news.title: Fuel Excise Halved for Three Months as War Pushes Prices Up
news.summary: Federal government cuts fuel excise by 50% for three months to ease petrol/diesel prices. Victoria and Tasmania already offering free public transport. Australians expressing fears about prolonged war and fuel shortages.

news.title: Trump Considers Seizing Iran's Kharg Island; Ground Operations Readied
news.summary: US president says deal with Iran could be made "very quickly" but also considering seizing Kharg Island—Iran's main oil export terminal. US officials briefing on readiness for ground operations. War enters second month.

news.title: US Government Shutdown at 45 Days; Trump-Russia Oil Tanker at Cuba
news.summary: Longest shutdown in US history continues. Trump says he has "no problem" with Russian oil tanker stationed at Cuba. "No Kings" protests continue nationwide against the administration.

news.title: No New Breaking News Since 06:01 Report
news.summary: Fintech/Crypto quiet on US Sunday evening. Key stories remain unchanged: SE Asia stablecoin cards 40x surge, CLARITY Act DeFi impact, Google 2029 quantum deadline, Strategy BTC pause. Markets focused on Middle East war impact on oil and Fed rate expectations.

news.title: "No Kings" Protests Draw Massive Crowds
news.summary: Bruce Springsteen performs at Minnesota rally. Organizers claim largest single-day protest in US history.

news.title: Iran "Waiting" for US Ground Troops
news.summary: Iran says forces are "waiting" as more US Marines arrive in the Middle East. Thousands of American personnel now in region.

news.title: Iranian Attacks Hit UAE & Bahrain Industrial Sites
news.summary: Aluminium facilities attacked with multiple injuries. War spreading beyond Iran to Gulf states.

news.title: Three Journalists Killed in Israeli Strike
news.summary: Lebanese broadcasters confirm three journalists killed including Al Manar TV's Ali Shoeib.

news.title: Attie AI: Vibe Coding for AT Protocol Apps
news.summary: New AI tool will eventually code entire apps for AT Protocol (Bluesky's decentralized protocol). Demonstrates growing trend of AI-assisted app development for decentralized platforms.

news.title: Apple Siri Extensions Could Become "AI App Store"
news.summary: Mark Gurman's Power On reveals Apple planning dedicated App Store section for third-party AI chatbots running inside Siri. iOS 27 Extensions feature could create marketplace for AI integrations beyond ChatGPT.

news.title: Australia Support for NASA Artemis Moon Mission Continues
news.summary: Despite global tensions, Australia reaffirms commitment to NASA's lunar program. Defence Minister Hastie warns Australians may be losing faith in US alliance.

news.title: Israel-Iran War Escalates: Strikes on Tehran, Gulf Industrial Sites Hit
news.summary: Israeli military striking targets across Tehran. Iran attacking aluminum sites in UAE and Bahrain. Houthis striking Israel. Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, threatening global oil shipping.

news.title: Paris Bank of America Bomb Attempt; Israel Blocks Jerusalem Patriarch
news.summary: Three arrested after attempted bomb attack outside BoA in Paris. Israeli police block Latin Patriarch from Palm Sunday mass, citing security concerns after Iranian strikes.

news.title: US Government Shutdown Becomes Longest in History at 44 Days
news.summary: Department of Homeland Security funding lapse causes travel chaos at US airports. "No Kings" protests draw millions against Trump nationwide.

news.title: Victoria and Tasmania Offer Free Public Transport as Fuel Prices Spike
news.summary: Iran war pushing petrol prices up. Two Australian states incentivize commuters to avoid driving. ACCC reports Australians lost $2B to scams last year.

news.title: CLARITY Act Could Restrict Stablecoin Yields
news.summary: Proposed legislation would ring-fence yield on stablecoins, shifting value toward regulated players and away from DeFi tokens. 10x Research analyst warns of headwinds for decentralized finance.

news.title: Google Sets 2029 Post-Quantum Migration Deadline
news.summary: Google mandates all authentication services migrate to quantum-resistant cryptography by 2029, validating Ethereum's timeline. Bitcoin community response so far: silence.

news.title: Stablecoin Cards Surge 40x in Southeast Asia
news.summary: Singapore-based StraitsX reports 40x transaction volume surge and 83x card issuance growth (2024-2025). Stablecoin payments going "invisible" as crypto cards integrate seamlessly with everyday commerce.

news.title: Strategy (MicroStrategy) Pauses Bitcoin Buying Streak
news.summary: After 13 consecutive weeks of purchases, Strategy appeared to skip its weekly BTC acquisition announcement. The company holds over 500,000 BTC.

news.title: Washington State Sues Kalshi Prediction Markets
news.summary: AG alleges Kalshi offers "gambling products dressed up as prediction markets." Comes days after Kalshi secured margin trading license for institutional investors.

news.title: Analysis of LLM Impact in Academic Papers
news.summary: arXiv analysis reveals word usage shifts likely driven by LLMs: more "beyond"/"via" in titles, less "the"/"of" in abstracts. Multi-class classification struggles to identify which specific LLM generated text. Real-world LLM usage is heterogeneous and dynamic.

news.title: PICon: Multi-Turn Interrogation Framework for Persona Agents
news.summary: Applies interrogation methodology to evaluate persona agent consistency through logically chained multi-turn questioning. Tests internal consistency (no self-contradiction), external consistency (factual alignment), and retest consistency. Even "highly consistent" agents fail human baseline.

news.title: S2D2: Fast Decoding for Diffusion LLMs via Training-Free Self-Speculation
news.summary: Training-free speculative decoding for block-diffusion language models. Same model acts as both drafter and verifier by reducing block size to one for autoregressive mode. Achieves up to 4.7x speedup over AR decoding on SDAR with improved accuracy.

news.title: WildASR: Revisiting ASR in the Age of Voice Agents
news.summary: Multilingual diagnostic benchmark (4 languages) that factorizes ASR robustness along environmental, demographic, and linguistic axes. Critical finding: ASR systems hallucinate plausible but unspoken content under degraded inputs—safety risk for production voice agents.

news.title: WriteBack-RAG: Training the Knowledge Base through Evidence Distillation
news.summary: First framework to make RAG corpus trainable. Identifies where retrieval succeeds, distills relevant documents into compact knowledge units. Works as offline preprocessing with any RAG pipeline. +2.14% average improvement across 4 methods, 6 benchmarks.

news.title: Anthropic Security Lapse Leaks "Mythos" Model
news.summary: Unsecured data store revealed next Anthropic model codename "Mythos" plus CEO event details. Security incident at critical AI company.

news.title: Apple Has "Complete Access" to Gemini for Distillation
news.summary: Apple can use Gemini in data centers to train smaller "student" AI models via distillation. Tuned models require less computing power for on-device use.

news.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Released

news.title: Natural-Language Agent Harnesses (NLAHs)
news.summary: Agent performance increasingly depends on "harness engineering"—but harness design is usually buried in controller code. NLAHs externalize harness behavior as editable natural language artifacts, executed through an "Intelligent Harness Runtime" (IHR) with explicit contracts, durable artifacts, and lightweight adapters.

news.title: PICon: Multi-Turn Interrogation Framework for Persona Agent Consistency
news.summary: Borrowing from interrogation methodology, PICon probes persona agents through logically chained multi-turn questioning. It evaluates consistency across three dimensions: internal (self-contradiction), external (fact alignment), and retest (stability). Even highly-rated persona agents failed to meet human baselines across all three, revealing contradictions and evasive responses.

news.title: Bitcoin Miners Pivoting to AI, Selling BTC to Fund Transition
news.summary: Average public miner spent $79,995 to produce one bitcoin last quarter. BTC trading at $70,000. Math doesn't work, so industry pivoting to AI compute - taking on $70 billion in contracts and liquidating BTC treasuries.

news.title: Canada Moves to Ban Crypto Donations for Election Campaigns
news.summary: Bill C-25 follows UK's lead in banning cryptocurrency donations to political campaigns. Canada's Chief Electoral Officer had warned about risks to electoral integrity for years.

news.title: Kalshi Secures License for Margin Trading to Institutional Investors
news.summary: Prediction market platform gets approval to offer margin trading, a departure from traditional fully-collateralized positions. Comes as industry sees growing trading volumes and institutional investment.

news.title: Washington Sues Kalshi as States Ramp Up Pressure on Prediction Markets
news.summary: Washington state attorney general alleges Kalshi offers "gambling products" dressed up as prediction markets. Multiple states increasing legal pressure on the prediction market industry as trading volumes surge.

news.title: Houthis Join Iran War, Red Sea Shipping Under Threat
news.summary: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels launched their first missile attack on Israel since the US-Israel war on Iran began. Fears grow that the group could attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic disruption already caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

news.title: Iran War Sparks Global Fertilizer Shortage, Food Price Crisis Looms
news.summary: Nearly 30% of global fertilizers pass through the now-closed Strait of Hormuz. Farmers worldwide fear crop failures and surging food prices as the blockade continues. Iran has been running the strait as a "toll booth," making hundreds of millions from oil sales.

news.title: US Government Shutdown Continues, TSA Agents Work Without Pay
