news.summary: Codex Security focuses on identifying and fixing app security issues. Codex Open Source Fund now includes conditional access to Codex Security for open source developers.

news.title: OpenAI's head of robotics quits over company's Pentagon deal
news.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski posted on X that she resigned from OpenAI, saying the company's contract didn't do enough to protect Americans from warrantless surveillance and that granting AI lethal autonomy without human authorization deserved more deliberation.

news.title: Qualcomm announces Arduino Ventuno Q for robotics and AI
news.summary: After acquiring Arduino last October, Qualcomm announced a new single-board computer called Ventuno Q. Powered by Dragonwing IQ8 processor, 16GB of RAM, and a 40 TOPS NPU. Designed for building robots and machines that function autonomously in response to input from connected sensors.

news.title: Unable to fetch industry news due to Bloomberg bot protection
news.summary: Bloomberg Technology requires CAPTCHA verification (robot detection) blocking access. TechCrunch tag pages return generic Founder Summit promotion instead of specific healthcare/biotech news. No major funding rounds, product launches, or M&A activity detected in this sector.

news.title: National poll shows voters like AI less than ICE
news.summary: NBC News poll reveals that 56% of voters say they've used AI platforms like ChatGPT or Copilot in previous month. The finding contrasts with negative public sentiment toward Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).

news.title: No new AI developments detected since 14:01 PM update
news.summary: All major stories from earlier today (GPT-5.4 release, Pentagon vs. Anthropic, OpenAI acquisitions, Claude Cowork, etc.) remain current. The Verge, TechCrunch, and Anthropic blog are returning older content or generic descriptions without new breaking news.

news.title: AI for Climate-Resilient Transport: Reinforcement Learning Framework
news.summary: Novel RL-based decision-support framework for long-term flood adaptation planning. IAM (Integrated Assessment Model) combines rainfall projection, flood modeling, transport simulation, and quantification of impacts. Evaluates framework through Copenhagen case study over 2024-2100 period.

news.title: AllScAIP: A Recipe for Scalable Attention-Based MLIPs
news.summary: MLIPs have advanced rapidly, but as models scale to larger systems like biomolecules and electrolytes, they struggle to accurately capture long-range (LR) interactions, leading current approaches to rely on explicit physics-based terms. We propose AllScAIP, a straightforward, attention-based, and energy-conserving MLIP model that scales to O(100M) training samples.

news.title: BEVLM: Distilling Semantic Knowledge from LLMs into Bird's-Eye View Representations
news.summary: Connects spatially consistent BEV representations with LLMs for cross-view driving. Enables LLMs to reason more effectively by 46% and improves closed-loop performance by 29% in safety-critical scenarios.

news.title: Ensemble GNNs for SST Forecasting via Input Perturbations
news.summary: Investigates ensemble learning strategies for sea surface temperature forecasting using Graph Neural Networks. Implements homogeneous ensemble approach inspired by bagging with diversity during inference via initial state perturbations. Assesses forecasts over 15-day horizon using deterministic (RMSE, bias) and probabilistic metrics (CRPS, Spread-skill ratio).

news.title: Fly360: Omnidirectional Obstacle Avoidance within Drone View
news.summary: Lightweight perception-decision pipeline with fixed random-yaw training for panoramic drones. Achieves stable omnidirectional obstacle avoidance and outperforms forward-view baselines across all tasks.

news.title: Multimodal Large Language Models as Image Classifiers
news.summary: Studies show MLLMs suffer from flawed evaluation protocols. Reveals inflated results from weak multiple-choice distractors and poor output mapping. ReGT evaluation shows MLLMs benefit most from corrected labels (+10.8%), narrowing perceived gap with supervised models.

news.title: Omni-Diffusion: Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation
news.summary: First any-to-any multimodal LLM built on mask-based discrete diffusion models. Supports complex multi-modal scenarios. Outperforms existing multimodal systems on diverse benchmarks.

news.title: Penguin-VL: Exploring Efficiency Limits of VLM with LLM-based Vision Encoders
news.summary: Vision Language Model (VLM) development has largely relied on scaling model size, which hinders deployment on compute-constrained mobile and edge devices. We explore performance limits of compact (2B and 8B) VLMs. Penguin-VL's vision encoder, initialized from a text-only LLM, serves as a superior alternative to contrastive pretraining, unlocking a higher degree of visual fidelity and data efficiency for multimodal understanding.

news.title: SCOPE: Scene-Contextualized Incremental 3D Segmentation
news.summary: Plug-and-play background-guided prototype enrichment for IFS. Achieves +6.98% novel-class IoU, +3.61% and +1.70% mean IoU, while maintaining low forgetting on ScanNet and S3DIS.

news.title: Stem: Rethinking Causal Information Flow in Sparse Attention
news.summary: Addresses quadratic computational complexity of self-attention in LLMs during pre-filling. Proposes Stem, a plug-and-play sparsity module aligned with information flow. Employs Token Position-Decay strategy and Output-Aware Metric to retain initial tokens while achieving superior accuracy with reduced computation.

news.title: SUREON: A Benchmark and Vision-Language Model for Surgical Reasoning
news.summary: Large-scale video QA dataset (134.7K clips, 170 procedures) systematically harvesting surgical reasoning from academic lectures. Introduces SureonVLM models achieving 84%+ accuracy on benchmark while outperforming general-domain models.

news.title: Unable to fetch industry news due to source access restrictions
news.summary: TechCrunch, 36Kr, and other news sources are returning limited content or access errors (404, bot protection). No specific SaaS/Enterprise news articles could be extracted from the current session.

news.title: AllScAIP: Unlocking Long-Range Accuracy with All-to-All Node Attention
news.summary: Novel attention-based, energy-conserving MLIP model scales to O(100 million) training samples. Addresses long-range challenge using data-driven all-to-all node attention. Achieves state-of-the-art energy/force accuracy on molecular systems and OMol25 benchmark, while being competitive on materials and catalysts.

news.title: Backdoor Modality Collapse in Multimodal Diffusion Models
news.summary: Investigates phenomenon where backdoor mechanisms degenerate to rely predominantly on subset of modalities, rendering others redundant. Introduces Trigger Modality Attribution (TMA) and Cross-Trigger Interaction (CTI) metrics to rigorously quantify this behavior.

news.title: Hierarchical Industrial Demand Forecasting with Temporal and Uncertainty Explanations
news.summary: Novel interpretability method for large hierarchical probabilistic time-series forecasting, adapting generic interpretability techniques while addressing challenges with hierarchical structures and uncertainty. Evaluated on semi-synthetic datasets from real-world scenarios of explaining hierarchical demands for over ten thousand products at large chemical company.

news.title: RAMoEA-QA: Hierarchical Specialization for Robust Respiratory Audio Question Answering
news.summary: Hierarchically routed generative model for respiratory audio QA that unifies multiple question types and supports both discrete and continuous targets. Two-stage conditional specialization: Audio Mixture-of-Experts routes recordings to suitable audio encoder, and Language Mixture-of-Adapters selects LoRA adapter to match query intent.

news.title: Schema-Gated Agentic AI for Flexible and Reproducible Scientific Workflows
news.summary: Proposes schema-gated orchestration as resolving principle for agentic AI: schema becomes mandatory execution boundary at composed-workflow level. Operationalizes execution determinism (ED) and conversational flexibility (CF) axes to review 20 systems with multi-model LLM scoring.

news.title: Australia announces military support commitment to Gulf states
news.summary: Federal government confirms deployment of military assets to support Gulf region amid escalating tensions. Part of broader regional security engagement.

news.title: CSIRO funding cuts loom as scientists call for urgent support
news.summary: Australia's national science agency faces significant budget uncertainty, with researchers warning that job cuts could impact critical research programs across multiple disciplines.

news.title: Iran conflict escalates as US-Israel strikes target key infrastructure
news.summary: Iran announces new supreme leader as US-Israel attacks continue targeting critical infrastructure. Oil markets volatile as conflict disrupts supply routes through Strait of Hormuz.

news.title: Oil prices surge 20%, impacting transport and logistics industries
news.summary: Middle East conflict driving oil price spikes, with trucking and logistics sectors already facing cost pressures. ASX markets showing volatility.

news.title: No major Healthcare & Biotech developments detected
news.summary: Unable to fetch fresh news from TechCrunch Healthcare, Bloomberg Technology, and 36Kr due to bot protection/access restrictions. No significant funding rounds, product launches, or M&A activity detected in this sector since the last update cycle.

news.title: Beyond Rows to Reasoning: Agentic Retrieval for Multimodal Spreadsheet Understanding and Editing
news.summary: BRTR is a multimodal agentic framework for spreadsheet understanding that replaces single-pass retrieval with an iterative tool-calling loop. Achieves SOTA performance across three benchmarks, surpassing prior methods by 25 percentage points on FRTR-Bench. The planner-retrieval-reasoning loop enables reliable multi-step reasoning over complex enterprise workbooks.

news.title: Boosting Deep Reinforcement Learning using Pretraining with Logical Options
news.summary: H²RL is a hybrid approach injecting symbolic structure into neural-based reinforcement learning agents via logical option-based pretraining. Steers learning away from short-term reward loops toward goal-directed behavior while allowing final policy refinement via standard environment interaction.

news.title: CODEC: Causal Interpretation of Neural Network Computations with Contribution Decomposition
news.summary: CODEC uses sparse autoencoders to decompose network behavior into sparse motifs of hidden-neuron contributions, revealing causal processes undetectable by analyzing activations alone. Applied to benchmark image-classification networks, finding contributions grow in sparsity and dimensionality across layers.

news.title: NOBLE: Nonlinear Low-Rank Branch for Linear Enhancement
news.summary: NOBLE adds nonlinear low-rank branches to transformer linear layers, designed for pretraining from scratch. Using CosNet (two-layer cosine nonlinearity), achieves 1.47x step speedup to baseline eval loss with only 4% additional parameters and 7% step time overhead.

news.title: PONTE: Personalized Orchestration for Natural Language Trustworthy Explanations
news.summary: PONTE is a human-in-the-loop framework for adaptive XAI narratives, combining low-dimensional preference models, preference-conditioned generators grounded in structured artifacts, and verification modules enforcing numerical faithfulness and informational completeness. User feedback iteratively updates preference state.

news.title: Anthropic launches Claude Cowork AI agent feature
news.summary: Claude Cowork can read, edit, and create files on a user's computer, handling tasks like organizing downloads, creating spreadsheets from screenshots, and drafting reports. Currently available only for Claude Max subscribers ($100-200/month).

news.title: Anthropic usage surges despite Pentagon conflict
news.summary: Claude is breaking daily signup records and topping App Store charts for free and AI apps in dozens of countries following the Pentagon's "supply-chain risk" designation. The designation appears to have driven demand rather than suppressed it.

news.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to cancel NEH grants
news.summary: Elon Musk's short-lived agency used a ChatGPT prompt to cancel National Endowment for the Humanities grants deemed contrary to anti-DEI agenda. The simple prompt asked if grant descriptions related to DEI in under 120 characters.

news.title: Microsoft integrating Claude Cowork into Copilot
news.summary: The Cowork integration, built in collaboration with Anthropic, aims to help Copilot perform "long-running, multi-step tasks." Feature is in testing and will be available for preview through Microsoft's Frontier program later this month.

news.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT's "adult mode"
news.summary: ChatGPT's long-promised "adult mode" will miss its Q1 2026 launch. OpenAI says they're focusing on "higher priority" work including gains in intelligence, personality improvements, personalization, and making the experience more proactive.

news.title: OpenAI head of robotics quits over Pentagon deal
news.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski resigned from OpenAI, stating the Pentagon contract didn't do enough to protect Americans from warrantless surveillance and that granting AI "lethal autonomy without human authorization" deserved more deliberation.

news.title: OpenAI reaches Pentagon deal, but critics say it's softer than Anthropic's
news.summary: OpenAI announced new terms with the Pentagon using "any lawful use" language. Sources say the deal is much softer than Anthropic's rejected terms, potentially allowing mass surveillance under "technically legal" definitions.

news.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.4 with native computer-use capabilities
news.summary: GPT-5.4 is OpenAI's most capable frontier model, featuring state-of-the-art computer-use capabilities enabling AI agents to operate computers and carry out complex workflows across applications. Achieves 83% on GDPval benchmark and 75% on OSWorld-Verified, surpassing human performance.

news.title: Pentagon labels Anthropic a "supply-chain risk"
news.summary: The Defense Department formally designated Anthropic as a supply-chain risk after the company refused to allow Claude to be used for autonomous lethal weapons without human oversight and mass surveillance. First time an American company has received this label.

news.title: Qualcomm announces Arduino Ventuno Q for AI robotics
news.summary: New single-board computer powered by Dragonwing IQ8 processor with 16GB RAM and 40 TOPS NPU, designed for building autonomous robots and machines that respond to connected sensor input. Pricing and availability TBD.

news.title: BEVLM: Distilling Semantic Knowledge from LLMs into Bird's-Eye View Representations
news.summary: Connects spatially consistent BEV representations with LLMs for cross-view driving. Enables LLMs to reason more effectively by 46% and improves closed-loop end-to-end performance by 29% in safety-critical scenarios.

news.title: Fly360: Omnidirectional Obstacle Avoidance within Drone View
news.summary: Lightweight perception-decision pipeline with fixed random-yaw training for panoramic drones. Achieves stable omnidirectional obstacle avoidance and outperforms forward-view baselines across all tasks.

news.title: Multimodal Large Language Models as Image Classifiers
news.summary: Studies show MLLMs suffer from flawed evaluation protocols. Evaluation on ReGT reveals MLLMs benefit most from corrected labels (+10.8%), narrowing the perceived gap with supervised models. MLLMs can assist human annotators in difficult cases (50% integration rate).

news.title: Omni-Diffusion: Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation
news.summary: First any-to-any multimodal LLM built on mask-based discrete diffusion models. Supports not only bimodal but complex multi-modal scenarios. Outperforms existing multimodal systems on diverse benchmarks.

news.title: SCOPE: Scene-Contextualized Incremental 3D Segmentation
news.summary: Plug-and-play background-guided prototype enrichment for IFS. Achieves +6.98% novel-class IoU, +3.61% and +1.70% mean IoU, while maintaining low forgetting on ScanNet and S3DIS.

news.title: Iran names Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader; oil prices surge above $110
news.summary: The 56-year-old Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been named as Iran's new supreme leader. Oil prices have surged above $110 a barrel as major energy supply disruptions threaten global markets.

news.title: Muscat airport limits private jet flights as wealthy flee Gulf
news.summary: Oman's Muscat International Airport has placed restrictions on private jet flights as wealthy individuals flee Gulf states following conflict escalation. The move prioritizes commercial aviation.

news.title: Norway investigating Oslo US embassy explosion as possible terrorism
news.summary: Norwegian police are investigating a blast at the US embassy in Oslo that caused minor damage to the building, with terrorism being a motive under consideration.

news.title: Trump raises stakes in Iran war by weighing US ground forces deployment
news.summary: President Donald Trump is raising the stakes in Iran conflict by considering deployment of US ground forces. The move would significantly escalate US military involvement beyond current advisory role.

news.title: UK opens charter flights to Dubai as airlines cancel Middle East flights
news.summary: UK government opened charter flight booking portal for Britons in Dubai after thousands of flights were cancelled due to Middle East conflict. The move comes as airspace restrictions continue affecting commercial aviation.

news.title: HALP: Detecting Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models without Generating a Single Token
news.summary: Demonstrates that hallucination risk can be predicted before any token is generated by probing a model's internal representations. Probes trained on internal states achieve strong performance without decoding, reaching up to 0.93 AUROC on multiple models including Gemma-3-12B and Molmo 7B.

news.title: Reasoning Theater: Disentangling Model Beliefs from Chain-of-Thought
news.summary: Provides evidence of "performative" CoT where models become confident early but continue generating tokens. Probe-guided early exit reduces tokens by up to 80% on MMLU and 30% on GPQA-Diamond with similar accuracy, positioning attention probing as efficient tool.

news.title: Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline
news.summary: Introduces MM-Lifelong, a dataset of 181.1 hours across Day, Week, and Month scales. Reveals critical failure modes: end-to-end MLLMs suffer from Working Memory Bottleneck, while agentic baselines experience Global Localization Collapse on month-long timelines. Proposes Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA) with dynamic memory management.

news.title: Iran war escalates: Nearly 400 killed in Israeli strikes, oil depots targeted
news.summary: Lebanon reports nearly 400 casualties from Israeli strikes as Iran continues attacks across the Middle East. Oil depots in Tehran and Karaj were hit overnight, with videos showing massive fires and smoke plumes, while Kuwaiti government high-rise was also struck by Iranian drones.

news.title: Nepal election: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah wins after government toppled
news.summary: General election in Nepal follows violent youth-led protests that toppled the government in September. Balendra Shah, a former rapper, is heading for victory in what marks a significant political transition.

news.title: Tropical low moves south-east towards Queensland
news.summary: Weather system heading towards south-east Queensland, with communities preparing for potential impacts from the tropical system. Australian authorities monitoring development.

news.title: UK rejects Trump's criticism: "We do not agree on every issue"
news.summary: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper hit back at President Trump's criticism of UK response to Iran conflict. Tensions rising as Starmer and Trump speak for first time since president criticized UK stance.

news.title: US embassy explosion in Oslo under terrorism investigation
news.summary: Norwegian police are investigating a blast at the US embassy in Oslo that caused minor damage to the building, with terrorism being a motive under consideration.

news.title: Bitcoin purist Jack Dorsey says that his firm is reluctantly giving in to stablecoin craze
news.summary: The shift comes as stablecoins surge in popularity and competitors like Stripe and PayPal add stablecoin options, increasing market pressure. Dorsey's Block is embracing stablecoins despite his earlier purist stance.

news.title: Circle moves $68 million in just 30 minutes by using its own stablecoin for internal payments
news.summary: The stablecoin issuer used its Mint platform for intercompany transfers, replacing bank wires that often take days to settle. CEO Jeremy Allaire demonstrated the speed and efficiency of using USDC for treasury operations.

news.title: Coinbase says new U.S. tax-reporting rules for crypto are cluttered and confusing
news.summary: The IRS's 1099-DA tax form for reporting digital asset gains comes with a burden of over-reporting, Coinbase's tax experts warned. The exchange says the rules create unnecessary compliance complexity for users.

news.title: Hopes rising that Clarity Act may soon see some forward motion
news.summary: The Clarity Act, aimed at providing regulatory framework for crypto markets, may advance after stalled progress. Market participants are optimistic about potential legislative clarity.

news.title: Kalshi, Polymarket seeking $20 billion valuations in fundraising talks
news.summary: Kalshi, approved by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, was last valued at $11 billion, while Polymarket was valued at $9 billion. Both prediction market platforms are pursuing significant capital raises as the sector gains mainstream attention.

news.title: Latin America's crypto user growth outpaced U.S. by 3x in 2025, report shows
news.summary: Brazil and Argentina are leading growth, with Brazil dominating by transaction size and Argentina seeing increasing adoption driven by cross-border payments and stablecoin use. The region is emerging as a key crypto adoption hub.

news.title: Tokenized assets exceed $25 billion after nearly quadrupling in a year
news.summary: Treasurys, private credit, and commodities are driving growth in tokenized assets. However, most tokenized assets remain isolated from DeFi markets, suggesting institutional integration is still early stage.

news.title: Top Wall Street minds see AI rotation ahead as bitcoin seeks role in new cycle
news.summary: BlackRock's Rick Rieder, UBS's Ulrike Hoffmann-Burchardi and Third Point's Daniel Loeb see steady economic growth but a tougher market environment. There's discussion of capital rotating from AI bets to other opportunities including crypto.

news.title: Trump's cyber strategy vows to 'support security' of cryptocurrencies and blockchain
news.summary: Places blockchain security in context of national technology competition alongside AI and quantum computing. The strategy includes supporting cryptocurrency security as part of broader tech competition efforts.

news.title: Censored LLMs as a Natural Testbed for Secret Knowledge Elicitation
news.summary: Studies censored Chinese LLMs (like Qwen3) as a natural testbed for secret knowledge, evaluating a suite of elicitation and lie detection techniques. The strongest honesty elicitation techniques transfer to frontier open-weights models including DeepSeek R1.

news.title: Leveraging LLM Parametric Knowledge for Fact Checking without Retrieval
news.summary: Introduces INTRA, a method that exploits interactions between internal representations for fact-checking without retrieval, achieving state-of-the-art performance across 9 datasets with strong generalization. Establishes fact-checking without retrieval as a promising research direction.

news.title: POET-X: Memory-efficient LLM Training by Scaling Orthogonal Transformation
news.summary: POET-X is a scalable and memory-efficient variant that enables pretraining of billion-parameter LLMs on a single Nvidia H100 GPU. The approach performs orthogonal equivalence transformations with significantly reduced computational cost while maintaining the generalization and stability benefits of the original POET framework.

news.title: The Spike, Sparse and Sink: Anatomy of Massive Activations and Attention Sinks
news.summary: Studies two recurring phenomena in Transformer language models: massive activations where a small number of tokens exhibit extreme outliers, and attention sinks where certain tokens attract disproportionate attention mass regardless of semantic relevance. Shows that co-occurrence is largely an architectural artifact and that the two phenomena serve distinct functions.

news.title: Anthropic releases Claude Sonnet 4.6
news.summary: Sonnet 4.6 delivers frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work at scale, representing a significant upgrade to the Claude model family.

news.title: Anthropic usage surges despite "supply-chain risk" designation
news.summary: Claude is breaking daily signup records in every country where available, topping App Store charts for free and AI apps in dozens of countries including the US, Canada, and Europe, despite being designated a "supply chain risk" by the US Department of War.

news.title: GGML and llama.cpp join Hugging Face
news.summary: Major integration to ensure the long-term progress of Local AI, bringing popular local LLM inference frameworks directly to the Hugging Face ecosystem.

news.title: Hugging Face: Training a Text-to-Image Model in 24 hours
news.summary: Photoroom's PRX Part 3 demonstrates how to train a text-to-image model in just 24 hours, showcasing accelerated AI training techniques and efficiency improvements.

news.title: Meta to temporarily allow rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp in EU
news.summary: In a move to appease European Commission antitrust regulators, Meta will support "general purpose AI chatbots using the WhatsApp Business API in Europe" for the next 12 months for a fee.

news.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT "adult mode"
news.summary: The feature expected to launch this quarter is being pushed back to focus on higher priority work including gains in intelligence, personality improvements, personalization, and making the experience more proactive.

news.title: OpenAI developing GitHub competitor
news.summary: Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository, with completion still months away, potentially putting ChatGPT creator in competition with Microsoft.

news.title: OpenAI head of robotics quits over Pentagon deal
news.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski resigned, stating the contract didn't do enough to protect Americans from warrantless surveillance and that granting AI "lethal autonomy without human authorization" deserved more deliberation.

news.title: OpenAI launches Codex Security
news.summary: New research preview AI agent focused on identifying and fixing app security issues, with conditional access available through ChatGPT Pro with Codex subscriptions for open source developers.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview available on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face releases v4 preview of Transformers.js, now available on NPM, enabling JavaScript developers to run transformer models directly in the browser and Node.js.

news.title: Mistral AI Releases Voxtral: Transcription at Speed of Sound
news.summary: Mistral AI introduces Voxtral, a precision diarization, real-time transcription, and new audio playground. Focuses on speed and accuracy for audio processing tasks.

news.title: Memory-efficient LLM Training by Scaling Orthogonal Transformation
news.summary: Introduces POET-X, a scalable and memory-efficient variant of Reparameterized Orthogonal Equivalence Training (POET). While POET provides strong training stability through orthogonal transformations, its original implementation has high memory and computational overhead. POET-X maintains generalization and stability benefits while achieving substantial improvements in throughput and memory efficiency. Enables pretraining of billion-parameter LLMs on a single Nvidia H100 GPU.

news.title: Anthropic CEO claims refusal to pander to Trump caused Pentagon blowup
news.summary: Dario Amodei suggested Anthropic's relationship with government soured because, unlike OpenAI, "we haven't donated to Trump" and "we haven't given dictator-style praise to Trump."

news.title: Anthropic usage booms despite Pentagon "supply-chain risk" designation
news.summary: Claude has been breaking daily signup records in every country where available since early last week. AppFigures data shows it topping App Store charts in US, Canada, and much of Europe.

news.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 released with frontier performance across coding and agents
news.summary: Sonnet 4.6 delivers frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work at scale, marking a significant update to Anthropic's model family.

news.title: Claude will remain ad-free as Anthropic expands access
news.summary: Anthropic explains why advertising incentives are incompatible with a genuinely helpful AI assistant, and outlines plans to expand access without compromising user trust.

news.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to cancel National Endowment for the Humanities grants
news.summary: Elon Musk's agency rolled into NEH with a mandate to cancel grants based on a simple ChatGPT prompt asking if projects relate to DEI. The AI made sweeping and sometimes bizarre decisions without human review.

news.title: GGML and llama.cpp join Hugging Face ecosystem
news.summary: The popular local AI inference frameworks are now part of Hugging Face to ensure the long-term progress of Local AI, enabling better integration with the HF ecosystem.

news.title: Meta to allow rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp in EU for 12 months
news.summary: In an effort to appease EU antitrust regulators, Meta will support general purpose AI chatbots using WhatsApp Business API in Europe "for a fee" for the next year.

news.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT's "adult mode" indefinitely
news.summary: The feature expected to launch Q1 2026 is being postponed to focus on higher priority work including intelligence gains, personality improvements, personalization, and making the experience more proactive.

news.title: OpenAI launches Codex Security preview and Open Source Fund
news.summary: New AI agent focused on identifying and fixing app security issues, plus expanded access for open source developers through the ChatGPT Pro with Codex program.

news.title: OpenAI's head of robotics quits over Pentagon deal
news.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski resigned from OpenAI, stating the company's Pentagon contract didn't do enough to protect Americans from warrantless surveillance and that granting AI "lethal autonomy without human authorization" deserved more deliberation.

news.title: Bringing Robotics AI to Embedded Platforms
news.summary: New research on dataset recording, VLA fine-tuning, and on-device optimizations for robotics AI on embedded platforms.

news.title: Google DeepMind: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
news.summary: New model built for intelligence at scale, expanding the Gemini 3.1 family with optimized performance.

news.title: Hugging Face: Modular Diffusers for composable diffusion pipelines
news.summary: Introduction of Modular Diffusers - composable building blocks for diffusion pipelines, enabling greater flexibility and reuse.

news.title: Memory-efficient LLM Training with POET-X
news.summary: POET-X enables training of billion-parameter LLMs on a single Nvidia H100 GPU, while standard optimizers like AdamW run out of memory in the same settings.

news.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT's "adult mode"
news.summary: Feature expected this quarter is being postponed to focus on "higher priority" work including gains in intelligence, personality improvements, personalization, and proactive features.

news.title: OpenAI launches Codex Security research preview
news.summary: New AI agent focused on identifying and fixing app security issues, with conditional access available through ChatGPT Pro with Codex subscriptions.

news.title: The Spike, the Sparse and the Sink: Transformer Language Model Analysis
news.summary: Study of massive activations and attention sinks in Transformers, showing co-occurrence is largely an architectural artifact of pre-norm configuration.

news.title: Censored LLMs as a Natural Testbed for Secret Knowledge Elicitation
news.summary: Using Chinese-developed open-weights LLMs (Qwen3) that censor politically sensitive topics as a natural testbed, the authors evaluate honesty elicitation and lie detection techniques. They find that sampling without chat templates, few-shot prompting, and fine-tuning on honesty data most reliably increase truthful responses. Linear probes trained on unrelated data offer a cheaper alternative for lie detection.

news.title: Memory-efficient LLM Training by Scaling Orthogonal Transformation (POET-X)
news.summary: POET-X is a scalable variant of the Reparameterized Orthogonal Equivalence Training (POET) framework that dramatically reduces memory and computational overhead. It enables pretraining of billion-parameter LLMs on a single Nvidia H100 GPU, where standard optimizers like AdamW run out of memory under the same constraints.

news.title: Reasoning Theater: Disentangling Model Beliefs from Chain-of-Thought
news.summary: This paper analyzes "performative chain-of-thought" where LLMs continue generating reasoning tokens even after becoming confident in their answer. Using DeepSeek-R1 671B and GPT-OSS 120B, the authors show that models often decodable their final answers from activations much earlier than their CoT would suggest, especially for easy questions, while genuine reasoning occurs on difficult multihop tasks.

news.title: The Spike, the Sparse and the Sink: Anatomy of Massive Activations and Attention Sinks
news.summary: Through systematic experiments, this work demonstrates that massive activations (extreme outliers in specific channels) and attention sinks (tokens attracting disproportionate attention) serve related but distinct functions. Massive activations operate globally as implicit parameters, while attention sinks operate locally to modulate attention patterns. The pre-norm configuration enables their co-occurrence.

news.title: Transformer-Based Inpainting for Real-Time 3D Streaming in Sparse Multi-Camera Setups
news.summary: The authors propose a novel multi-view aware transformer network for real-time 3D streaming inpainting. Using spatio-temporal embeddings, the method ensures consistency across frames while preserving fine details. An adaptive patch selection strategy balances inference speed and quality, achieving real-time performance.

news.title: Anthropic usage booming despite "supply-chain risk" designation
news.summary: The designation from the US Department of War is driving up demand for Claude, which has been breaking daily signup records in every country where Claude is available, topping App Store charts globally.

news.title: ArXiv: Study on massive activations and attention sinks in Transformers
news.summary: New research analyzing two recurring phenomena in Transformer language models: massive activations (extreme outliers in a few channels) and attention sinks (tokens attracting disproportionate attention regardless of semantic relevance).

news.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 released with 1M token context window
news.summary: Major upgrade improving coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, and agent planning. Features frontier-level performance at Sonnet pricing ($3/$15 per million tokens), with users preferring it over Sonnet 4.5 70% of the time.

news.title: Dario Amodei clarifies Pentagon designation scope
news.summary: Anthropic's CEO stated the supply chain risk designation applies only to Claude's use in direct Department of War contracts, not all uses by customers with such contracts. The company will provide models to the Department of War during transition.

news.title: Defense contractors backing off on Claude after Pentagon designation
news.summary: Companies doing business with the US military are pivoting away from Anthropic's AI after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the supply chain risk designation, abandoning Claude preemptively "out of an abundance of caution."

news.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to gut National Endowment for the Humanities
news.summary: Elon Musk's short-lived agency used ChatGPT prompts to determine which grants to cancel based on DEI criteria, with decisions made without careful analysis - just short summaries fed into the AI chatbot.

news.title: Meta will temporarily allow rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp in EU
news.summary: Meta announced that "for a fee" it will support general-purpose AI chatbots using the WhatsApp Business API in Europe for the next 12 months to appease European Commission antitrust regulators.

news.title: OpenAI launches Codex Security for app security
news.summary: New research preview AI agent focused on identifying and fixing app security issues, plus Codex Open Source Fund updates now include conditional access to Codex Security for open source developers.

news.title: Iran conflict escalates with Amazon data center strikes, AI infrastructure affected
news.summary: Iran has targeted Amazon data centers in a "jolt to Gulf AI drive," disrupting cloud infrastructure in the region. Meanwhile, Oracle and OpenAI have scrapped their deal to expand a flagship Texas data center. The US is drawing up strict new AI guidelines amid an Anthropic clash. Israeli strikes on Iranian oil depots have sent plumes of smoke over Tehran, and Kuwait City has seen a major tower fire amid ongoing conflict.

news.title: Iran warns it will hit US bases across region after president's apology
news.summary: Iran has issued warnings that it will target US military bases across the region hours after the US president issued an apology. Israeli strikes hit Beirut hotel killing four and wounding 10, with damage to civilian facilities in Kuwait from falling debris. The conflict continues to escalate with oil infrastructure being targeted.

news.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah defeats Nepal's ex-PM in landslide
news.summary: Balendra Shah, 35, has unseated former Nepalese Prime Minister Sharma KP Oli with 68,348 votes compared to Oli's 18,734. Thursday's general election, the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September, pitted establishment against a new generation of Gen Z politicians. The unrest was triggered by Oli's banning of social media platforms, with 77 killed during protests.

news.title: Tornadoes kill six across Michigan and Oklahoma
news.summary: At least six people have died and dozens injured after tornadoes struck Michigan and Oklahoma. Four died in southern Michigan including a 12-year-old boy, and two in Oklahoma. The storms uprooted trees, downed power lines, damaged buildings, and ripped roofs off houses. The National Weather Service warns severe weather could continue with flash flooding from Great Plains to Texas.

news.title: Trump officials question Australian Jewish leader on gun policies
news.summary: Trump administration officials, including the president's antisemitism envoy, asked visiting Australian Jewish leader Alex Ryvchin why Australian Jews aren't armed following the Bondi Beach terror attack that killed 15 people. The US officials showed interest in whether the event was "left vulnerable" by being unarmed, contrasting American individualistic security approaches with Australian culture.

news.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to gut the National Endowment for the Humanities
news.summary: Elon Musk's short-lived agency used ChatGPT prompts to cancel NEH grants based on DEI criteria, making sweeping and sometimes bizarre decisions without careful analysis of funded projects. Instead of thorough review, they pulled short summaries and fed them into ChatGPT with a simple prompt asking if content related to DEI.

news.title: OpenAI's head of robotics quit over the company's Pentagon deal
news.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski resigned from OpenAI, posting on X that the company's Pentagon contract didn't do enough to protect Americans from warrantless surveillance. She criticized granting AI "lethal autonomy without human authorization" as a line that deserved more deliberation.

news.title: Memory-efficient LLM Training by Scaling Orthogonal Transformation
news.summary: POET-X introduces a memory-efficient variant of orthogonal transformation training that maintains stability while dramatically reducing computational overhead. The method enables pretraining billion-parameter LLMs on a single Nvidia H100 GPU, where standard optimizers like AdamW run out of memory.

news.title: Armed robots deployed on Ukraine battlefield
news.summary: Ukraine has begun deploying armed robots on the battlefield against Russian forces, marking a significant escalation in autonomous weapons use. The programme represents one of the first large-scale operational deployments of lethal autonomous systems in modern warfare.

news.title: China unveils technology blueprint to close US gap
news.summary: China released its latest strategic blueprint aimed at accelerating technological development and closing the gap with US tech leadership. The document emphasizes breaking old development habits and achieving breakthroughs in critical technology sectors.

news.title: Indonesia passes law banning social media for under 16s
news.summary: Indonesia has enacted legislation banning social media and other online platforms for users under 16 years old. The law is the latest in a global trend of countries curbing young people's access to digital spaces, aiming to protect minors from abuse and mental health risks.

news.title: Iran war escalates: Amazon data centres targeted, oil surges to 2023 highs
news.summary: The US-Iran conflict intensified in the last 8 hours as Iran struck Amazon data centres in the Gulf region, disrupting AI infrastructure expansion. Oil prices have surged to their highest level since 2023 as the war triggers historic supply concerns. Dubai Airport operations partially resumed amid ongoing missile threats.

news.title: US draws up strict new AI guidelines amid Anthropic clash
news.summary: The US government is preparing to introduce significantly stricter AI regulations following tensions with Anthropic. The new guidelines mark a shift in the federal approach to AI oversight and could establish new compliance frameworks for the entire industry.

news.title: Anthropic Designated as "Supply Chain Risk" by Department of War
news.summary: Pentagon designates Anthropic as a national security supply chain risk, but company responds challenging the designation as legally unsound. Defense contractors are reportedly pivoting away from Claude despite Anthropic's clarification that the designation has narrow scope.

news.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6: 1M Token Context at Sonnet Pricing
news.summary: Anthropic's most capable Sonnet model delivers frontier-level performance with 1M token context window beta, while maintaining Sonnet 4.5 pricing at $3/$15 per million tokens. Major improvements in coding, computer use, and long-context reasoning approach Opus 4.5 performance levels at significantly lower cost.

news.title: Hugging Face Introduces Modular Diffusers
news.summary: New framework for diffusion pipelines allows composing reusable blocks instead of writing entire pipelines from scratch. Blocks can be inspected independently, swapped freely, and composed into custom workflows, with integration to Mellon's node-based visual interface.

news.title: Meta Temporarily Allows Rival AI Chatbots on WhatsApp in EU
news.summary: Meta will allow general-purpose AI chatbots using WhatsApp Business API in Europe for the next 12 months in a bid to stave off EU antitrust action. The move follows previous blocking of competitor chatbots and comes with a fee structure for access.

news.title: OpenAI Launches Codex Security in Research Preview
news.summary: New application security agent identifies complex vulnerabilities with high confidence, reducing false positives by 50% and noise by 84%. Rolling out to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, Business, and Edu customers with free access for the first month; has already found critical vulnerabilities in OpenSSH, GnuTLS, and other major open-source projects.

news.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.3 Instant with Reduced Hallucinations
news.summary: Update to ChatGPT's most-used model delivers 26.8% reduction in hallucinations with web access, 19.7% without. Features smoother conversational tone, fewer unnecessary refusals, and better web search synthesis while maintaining speed. Available now to all users.

news.title: PRX: Training Competitive Text-to-Image Model in 24 Hours
news.summary: Photoroom demonstrates how far the field has evolved with a 24-hour speedrun using 32 H200 GPUs and ~$1500 compute budget. Combines x-prediction, pixel-space training, perceptual losses (LPIPS + DINO), token routing with TREAD, and representation alignment with REPA/DINOv3. Code open-sourced on GitHub.

news.title: Agentic AI Can Complete Whole Courses for Students. Now What?
news.summary: A 22-year-old entrepreneur launched Einstein, an agentic AI tool that connects to Canvas learning management systems and can watch lectures, write papers, participate in discussions, and submit homework automatically. The tool has since received a cease-and-desist from Instructure (Canvas owner) and been taken down, but sparked intense debate about the future of online education.

news.title: In-Person Classes Aren't Safe From the AI Cheating Boom
news.summary: A study at Arizona State University found that 45% of points in in-person biology courses can be easily earned through digital cheating methods including AI-powered tools, electronic clickers, and smart devices. Students report gaming participation systems, snapping pictures of test materials, and using agentic AI to complete assignments.

news.title: Journal Submissions Riddled With AI-Created Fake Citations
news.summary: Academic journals are increasingly plagued by AI-generated fake citations that are nearly impossible to detect, with editors reporting phantom references appearing in submissions. One journal editor noted seeing more AI-generated citations now than when she started just a year ago, while Springer has implemented integrity screening due to the rising issue.

news.title: Legal Advocacy Group Raises Concern About AI in Federal Student Aid
news.summary: Student Defense is using FOIA requests to investigate how the Trump administration is using AI to handle federal student aid programs, fearing inadequate safeguards could compromise student data privacy. The group has submitted 12 requests exploring AI use in handling student inquiries, establishing new regulations on loan forgiveness and repayment plans, and processing veterans' education benefits.

news.title: With Teens Comfortable Confiding in AI, Should Schools Embrace It for Mental Health Care?
news.summary: Schools like Interlachen Jr.-Sr. High in Florida are using AI platforms such as Alongside to monitor student mental health, with the tool flagging potential self-harm or harm to others based on student chats. At least 9 companies in this space have received funding since 2022, and Alongside is used by more than 200 schools nationwide.

news.title: Bringing Robotics AI to Embedded Platforms: Dataset Recording, VLA Fine-Tuning, and On-Device Optimizations
news.summary: Hugging Face published a comprehensive guide on deploying robotics AI on embedded platforms. The flow covers dataset recording (OpenHAB), VLA model fine-tuning (Octo), and on-device optimizations for performance.

news.title: Epic v. Google Case Heading to Supreme Court
news.summary: Both Epic and Google have agreed to withdraw the case, meaning the antitrust battle won't proceed to the Supreme Court. Rule 46.1 reads that if all parties file in writing that a case be dismissed, "the Clerk, without further reference to the Court, will enter an order of dismissal."

news.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Virtual Boy Lineup Expands
news.summary: Nintendo is adding Virtual Boy versions of Mario Tennis and a game called Mario Clash to Switch Online + Expansion Pack subscription starting March 10th (that's Mario Day). Nintendo is also adding Game Boy Advance version of Mario vs. Donkey Kong to the service.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Open Source Fund Announced
news.summary: Separately, it announced [Codex Open Source Fund](https://developers.openai.com/codex/community/codex-for-oss), also now including "conditional access" to Codex Security as part of a six-month program. The subscriptions will integrate into OpenAI's $200/month paid tiers.

news.title: OpenAI Launches Codex Security Research Preview Agent
news.summary: OpenAI introduces a new research preview AI agent focused on identifying and fixing app security issues, marking a shift toward more open and collaborative security research. Available in research preview for open source developers.

news.title: PlayStation 5 Pro Receiving PSSR Upscaling Upgrade
news.summary: An improved version of Playstation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR) is rolling out to PS5 Pro "in the coming weeks," starting with Resident Evil Requiem. In March, "multiple existing games" will also get support for new version of PSSR, which could deliver upscaling quality similar to AMD's FSR 4.

news.title: Xbox Ally X Adds AI Screen Recording with Highlight Reels
news.summary: Any Xbox Insider PC can opt into Microsoft's new "postgame recaps," but Xbox Ally X handheld's integrated NPU takes it further with Copilot-generated "highlight reels." To start, it'll only work with Among Us, Elden Ring, Fortnite, Forza Horizon 5, Lies of P, Overwatch, and Palworld. Let me know if it's better than a normal recap?

news.title: Censored LLMs as a Natural Testbed for Secret Knowledge Elicitation
news.summary: Studies open-weights LLMs from Chinese developers (e.g., Qwen3) trained to censor politically sensitive topics. These models frequently produce falsehoods about suppressed topics while occasionally answering correctly, indicating knowledge they are trained to hide. Evaluated various elicitation techniques.

news.title: Effective Amortized Optimization Using Inexpensive Labels
news.summary: Proposes a novel framework that first collects "cheap" imperfect labels, performs supervised pretraining, then refines the model through self-supervised learning. Empirical validation across nonconvex constrained optimization, power-grid operation, and stiff dynamical systems shows faster convergence; improved accuracy, feasibility, and optimality; and up to 59x reductions in total offline cost.

news.title: Leveraging LLM Parametric Knowledge for Fact Checking without Retrieval
news.summary: Introduces the task of fact-checking arbitrary natural language claims without retrieval. Across 9 datasets and 18 methods, experiments show that logit-based approaches often underperform compared to methods leveraging internal model representations. INTRA, a new method exploiting internal representation interactions, achieves state-of-the-art performance with strong generalization.

news.title: Memory-efficient LLM Training by Scaling Orthogonal Transformation (POET-X)
news.summary: POET-X is a scalable, memory-efficient variant that enables pretraining of billion-parameter LLMs on a single Nvidia H100 GPU, unlike standard optimizers like AdamW that run out of memory.

news.title: Reasoning Theater: Disentangling Model Beliefs from Chain-of-Thought
news.summary: Provides evidence of "performative" chain-of-thought in reasoning models where models become confident in final answers early but continue generating tokens. Analysis shows that final answers are decodable from activations far earlier than a monitor can detect, and probe-guided early exit reduces tokens by up to 80% on MMLU with similar accuracy.

news.title: The Spike, Sink, and Sparse: Anatomy of Massive Activations and Attention Sinks
news.summary: Studies two recurring phenomena in Transformer language models: massive activations (extreme outliers in a few channels) and attention sinks (tokens attracting disproportionate attention regardless of semantic relevance). Shows that their co-occurrence is largely an architectural artifact of modern Transformer design, and that two phenomena serve related but distinct functions: massive activations operate globally as implicit parameters, while attention sinks operate locally to modulate attention outputs.

news.title: Epic Sues Fortnite Leaker Over NDA Violation
news.summary: Epic Games filed lawsuit against Hayden Cohen, a former contractor who leaked details of upcoming collaborations on X and Discord under aliases AdiraFN and AdiraFNInfo despite signing an NDA. The leaker shared confidential franchise information.

news.title: Lenovo Announces Legion 7A with AMD Strix Halo APU
news.summary: Lenovo's 15-inch Legion 7A is the first gaming laptop with integrated AMD Strix Halo APU. Features include Ryzen AI Max Plus 392, 15.3-inch OLED display (2560x1600 resolution, 165Hz variable refresh rate), and 500 nits brightness. Charging via 180W USB-C port.

news.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Virtual Boy Lineup Expands
news.summary: Nintendo is adding Virtual Boy versions of Mario Tennis and a game called Mario Clash to Switch Online + Expansion Pack subscription starting March 10th. Also adding Game Boy Advance version of Mario vs. Donkey Kong.

news.title: PS5 Pro Receiving PSSR Upscaling Upgrade
news.summary: An improved Spectral Super Resolution feature is rolling out to PS5 Pro. Quality is described as similar to AMD's FSR 4 upscaling technology. Will enhance gaming visuals for supported titles.

news.title: Super Pocket's Next Handheld is Rare Find
news.summary: Evercade has announced a new Rare-themed version of its Super Pocket handheld. The $69.99 "Rare Edition" will feature 14 built-in Rare games and be compatible with multi-title cartridges. Launching in June.

news.title: Switch 2 Pro Controller: Resident Evil Edition
news.summary: A $99.99 themed Pro controller for Switch 2 has been announced. Features premium materials and design based on Resident Evil franchise. Premium pricing raises questions over standard controller options.

news.title: AI Wikipedia Translations Adding Hallucinated Sources
news.summary: Open Knowledge Association's AI translations of Wikipedia articles have led to "hallucinations" with incorrect, fabricated, and unrelated sources. Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators.

news.title: Anthropic Usage Surges After Pentagon "Supply Chain Risk" Designation
news.summary: Anthropic's Claude is experiencing skyrocketing demand after being designated a "supply chain risk" by the Department of War, breaking daily signup records across multiple countries.

news.title: Defense Contractors Abandoning Anthropic AI After Pentagon Designation
news.summary: Companies doing business with the US military are pivoting away from Anthropic's AI after Defense Secretary Hegseth announced the "supply chain risk" designation, citing an "abundance of caution."

news.title: Meta Launches AI Shopping Research Tool in US
news.summary: Meta is rolling out an AI shopping research tool to testers in the US, aiming to compete with similar offerings found in ChatGPT and Gemini.

news.title: OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival After Recent Outages
news.summary: Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in early stages of developing its own code repository, potentially putting ChatGPT creator in direct competition with Microsoft.

news.title: Protesters to Gather Outside OpenAI Offices Today
news.summary: Organized by QuitGPT campaign, protesters plan to gather outside OpenAI offices to demand the company abandon its agreement with the Pentagon, citing concerns about AI-powered mass domestic surveillance and lethal autonomous weapons.

news.title: Sam Altman Plans to Add Two Sentences to OpenAI Pentagon Agreement
news.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman laid out updated wording he hoped would address people's concerns about mass domestic surveillance, though new language still includes the phrase "consistent with applicable laws."

news.title: US Launches Major Airstrikes on Iran
news.summary: Israel conducted military strikes against Iranian military command bunker, with Trump demanding "unconditional surrender" while Pentagon warns attacks will intensify. Tehran reports "constant explosions" as worst night so far.

news.title: Legal Epic v. Google case withdrawn from Supreme Court
news.summary: Both Epic Games and Google have agreed to withdraw the case, meaning the antitrust battle won't proceed to the Supreme Court. Rule 46.1 allows dismissal when all parties file in writing.

news.title: Anthropic CEO: Refusal to Pander to Trump Caused Pentagon Conflict
news.summary: CEO Dario Amodei suggested in a scathing 1,600-word memo that Anthropic's relationship with government soured because unlike OpenAI, "we haven't donated to Trump" and "we haven't given dictator-style praise to Trump."

news.title: Hugging Face 发布嵌入式平台机器人 AI 部署流程
news.summary: 完整的端到端指南，涵盖数据集记录、VLA（Vision-Language-Action）模型微调、设备端优化，让机器人 AI 能在嵌入式平台上高效运行。

news.title: Hugging Face 推出模块化 Diffusers
news.summary: 新的模块化框架为扩散管道提供可组合的构建块，让开发者能更灵活地构建和自定义扩散模型流水线。

news.title: Meta to Temporarily Allow Rival AI Chatbots on WhatsApp in EU
news.summary: Meta will temporarily allow competing general-purpose AI chatbots using WhatsApp Business API in Europe for next 12 months, aiming to appease EU antitrust regulators with a paid access model.

news.title: Meta 在欧盟开放 WhatsApp 给竞争对手 AI
news.summary: Meta 宣布将在未来12个月内允许竞争对手的 AI 聊天机器人通过付费接入 WhatsApp Business API，以应对欧盟反垄断监管压力。

news.title: Meta 测试 AI 购物研究工具
news.summary: Meta 正在美国测试新的 AI 购物研究工具，旨在与 ChatGPT 和 Gemini 的类似功能竞争，帮助用户快速找到和比较产品。

news.title: OpenAI Updates ChatGPT with More Context for Search Results
news.summary: The new GPT-5.3-Instant model rolling out is designed to provide more accurate answers with richer web search context, reducing unnecessary caveats, dead ends, and overly declarative phrasing that can interrupt the flow of conversation.

news.title: OpenAI 推出 GPT-5.3-Instant 模型
news.summary: 新模型旨在提供更准确的搜索结果和更好的上下文理解，减少了不必要的死胡同和过度声明，提升了对话流畅度。

news.title: OpenAI 正在开发 GitHub 竞品
news.summary: 在 GitHub 停机事件后，OpenAI 开始开发自己的代码仓库，计划向 OpenAI 客户开放，将直接与微软（OpenAI 投资方）竞争。

news.title: Pro-Human Declaration Signed by Teachers, Congress, Progressive Democrats, and Steve Bannon
news.summary: A major declaration has been signed by American Federation of Teachers, Congress of Christian Leaders, Progressive Democrats of America, and Steve Bannon, highlighting concerns about AI-powered surveillance and autonomous weapons.

news.title: Xbox Ally X 推出 AI 录屏功能
news.summary: 微软 Xbox Ally X 手持设备通过集成 NPU 和 Copilot，让玩家可以一键录制屏幕并自动生成游戏精彩回放，目前支持 Among Us、Elden Ring 等多款游戏。

news.title: 前特朗普顾问称与 Anthropic 的冲突是"试图对企业进行谋杀"
news.summary: 前高级 AI 政策顾问 Dean Ball 在社交媒体上批评五角大楼将 Anthropic 标记为供应链风险的行为，称这是对企业的不公正打压。

news.title: 美国在禁令后仍使用 Anthropic AI 进行伊朗打击
news.summary: 尽管特朗普几小时前宣布禁止联邦政府使用 Anthropic 工具，但美国在对伊朗的空袭中仍使用了 Claude 进行情报评估和目标识别。

news.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Released: Professional-Grade Agents at Scale
news.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 4.6 with enhanced capabilities for coding, agents, and professional work at scale. The model delivers frontier performance across coding, agent orchestration, and complex task execution.

news.title: Hugging Face: Modular Diffusers for Composable Diffusion
news.summary: Hugging Face introduced Modular Diffusers, a framework providing composable building blocks for diffusion models. Enables more flexible and efficient development of generative AI applications with customizable diffusion pipelines.

news.title: IBM and UC Berkeley Research: Enterprise Agent Failures
news.summary: Research from IBM and UC Berkeley revealed significant failure rates for enterprise AI agents when evaluated using IT-Bench and MAST benchmarks. Highlights challenges in reliability and deployment of AI agents in business environments.

news.title: Australia-Iran Relations Tense
news.summary: Sri Lanka takes control of second Iranian vessel a day after US sub attack. Australia claims its soldiers were on US submarine, as tensions rise over US-Iran military campaign.

news.title: Iran School/Base Strike with 168 Casualties
news.summary: Iranian authorities say attack on Saturday killed 168 people at school and nearby military base. Satellite imagery confirms multiple strikes on same location. Iran vows revenge for weekend attacks.

news.title: Noem Homeland Security Chief Removed
news.summary: President Trump fired Kirstjen Nielsen as Homeland Security secretary after mounting backlash over deportation crackdown. Chooses Oklahoma senator Markwayne Mullin as replacement. Leadership change at DHS could affect cybersecurity policy, immigration rules for tech workers.

news.title: Oil Markets Volatile Over Iran Conflict
news.summary: Stock markets and oil prices remain volatile amid fears Iran war may drag on. Experts warn prolonged elevated energy prices could make goods and services more expensive globally, impacting consumer tech spending and data center costs.

news.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Disrupts Oil/Gas Shipping
news.summary: Iran closed Strait of Hormuz threatening oil and gas shipping. Millions of barrels moved through daily before war started. Energy price volatility continues affecting global markets.

news.title: US & Israel Firepower Surge Against Iran
news.summary: Israel announces it's starting the "next phase" of war, with US supporting drone and missile strikes. Iran's high-risk strategy centers on endurance and deterrence as it absorbs strikes longer than adversaries can sustain costs.

news.summary: TechCrunch, Bloomberg, and 36Kr returned access restrictions (403 errors, robot detection, loading issues). Unable to retrieve major funding rounds, product launches, M&A activity, or regulatory changes affecting AI/tech industries.

news.title: Australian Troops on US Submarine, Health Apology Probe
news.summary: Australian claims its soldiers are on US submarine that sank Iranian warship. NSW health minister faces apology committee over medical misconduct probe. Girls lead exodus from state's public schools.

news.title: Iran-Israel War Escalates
news.summary: Israel announced it is starting the "next phase" of war against Iran, while UAE issued emergency alerts urging residents to seek shelter. Iran intercepted six missiles and 131 drones from Israel. Earlier, US submarine sank an Iranian warship in Indian Ocean with a torpedo.

news.title: Nepal Deputy PM Steps Down Ahead of Parliament Move

news.title: NSW Passes Hate Group Laws, AI Copyright Warning

news.title: Agentics 2.0: Lightweight, Python-Native Framework for Agentic Workflows
news.summary: Agentics 2.0 is a lightweight, Python-native framework for building high-quality, structured, explainable, and type-safe agentic data workflows. At its core, logical transduction algebra formalizes LLM inference calls as typed semantic transformations, which compose into larger programs via algebraically grounded operators and execute as stateless asynchronous calls in parallel Map-Reduce programs.

news.title: Build, Judge, Optimize: A Blueprint for Continuous Improvement of Multi-Agent Consumer Assistants
news.summary: Presents a practical blueprint for evaluating and optimizing multi-agent systems, illustrated through a production-scale AI grocery assistant. Introduces a multi-faceted evaluation rubric decomposing end-to-end shopping quality into structured dimensions, and develops two prompt-optimization strategies: Sub-agent GEPA and MAMuT (Multi-Agent Multi-Turn) GEPA.

news.title: Inherited Goal Drift: Contextual Pressure Can Undermine Agentic Goals
news.summary: Investigates goal drift in state-of-the-art models within a stock-trading environment. While models are robust to adversarial pressure, they often inherit drift when conditioned on prefilled trajectories from weaker agents. Only GPT-5.1 maintained consistent resilience among tested models, with drift behavior inconsistent between prompt variations.

news.title: Proactive Real-Time Agentic System Capable of Modeling Human State of Mind
news.summary: Real-time agentic system using foundation EXG model and text embeddings model, running fully offline on edge. Custom harness manages engagement with humans on multiple cognitive and affective levels of their State of Mind (e.g., empathy), providing actionable tool calls and protocol execution. GPLv3 open-source software with ethically aligned AI100 licensing.

news.title: RealPref: Evaluating Long-Horizon Preference Following in Personalized LLM Interactions
news.summary: RealPref is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating how well LLMs can follow user preferences in realistic, long-horizon situations. Features 100 user profiles, 1,300 personalized preferences, and long-horizon interaction histories with three types of test questions and detailed rubrics for LLM-as-a-judge evaluation.

Wikipedia editors are now placing restrictions on OKA translators, including blocking them if too many of their contributions are found with errors.

Hallucination rates decreased by 26.8% when using the web and 19.7% when relying on internal knowledge, compared to prior models.

news.summary: No major consumer or e-commerce industry developments detected in the current monitoring window. Continuing monitoring for emerging stories relevant to retail, e-commerce, consumer technology, and digital shopping platforms.

news.title: Agentics 2.0: Lightweight, Python-Native Framework for High-Quality Agentic Data Workflows
news.summary: Agentics 2.0 is a lightweight, Python-native framework for building high-quality, structured, explainable, and type-safe agentic data workflows. At core is logical transduction algebra formalizing LLM inference calls as typed semantic transformations, composing into larger programs via algebraically grounded operators in asynchronous Map-Reduce-style parallel execution. Provides semantic reliability through strong typing, semantic observability through evidence tracing, and scalability through stateless parallel execution. Evaluates on challenging benchmarks including DiscoveryBench for data-driven discovery and Archer for NL-to-SQL semantic parsing, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance.

news.title: Build, Judge, Optimize: A Blueprint for Continuous Improvement of Multi-Agent Consumer Assistants
news.summary: Conversational shopping assistants represent a compelling application of agentic AI, but moving from prototype to production reveals two underexplored challenges: how to evaluate multi-turn interactions and how to optimize tightly coupled multi-agent systems. This paper presents a practical blueprint for evaluating and optimizing conversational shopping assistants, illustrated through production-scale AI grocery assistant. Introduces multi-faceted evaluation rubric decomposing end-to-end shopping quality into structured dimensions and develops a calibrated LLM-as-judge pipeline aligned with human annotations. Building on evaluation foundation, investigates two complementary prompt-optimization strategies: (1) Sub-agent GEPA optimizing individual agent nodes against localized rubrics, and (2) MAMuT (Multi-Agent Multi-Turn) GEPA jointly optimizing prompts across agents using multi-turn simulation and trajectory-level scoring.

news.title: RealPref: Evaluating Long-Horizon Preference Following in Personalized User-LLM Interactions
news.summary: LLMs are increasingly serving as personal assistants where users share complex, diverse preferences over extended interactions. This work proposes RealPref, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating how well LLMs can follow these preferences in realistic, long-horizon situations. RealPref features 100 user profiles, 1,300 personalized preferences, four types of preference expression (ranging from explicit to implicit), and long-horizon interaction histories. Results indicate that LLM performance significantly degrades as context length grows and preference expression becomes more implicit. This research provides foundation for developing user-aware LLM assistants that can better adapt to individual needs.
