news.summary: No breaking news or major announcements detected in last 2 hours (since 12:01 PM check). All major sources have been monitored: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, Hugging Face, TechCrunch, The Verge. Continuing monitoring for emerging stories.

news.title: Major Tech Companies Pledge Grid Upgrades as Data Center Demand Soars
news.summary: A pledge commits companies to paying for electrical grid upgrades needed to meet soaring electricity demand from data centers. This directly impacts AI training/infrastructure costs and energy sustainability of large language model deployment.

news.title: Expectation and Acoustic Neural Network Representations Enhance Music Identification from Brain Activity
news.summary: During music listening, cortical activity encodes both acoustic and expectation-related information. Prior work has shown that ANN representations resemble cortical representations and can serve as supervisory signals for EEG recognition. Here we show that distinguishing acoustic and expectation-related ANN representations as teacher targets improves EEG-based music identification. Models pretrained to predict either representation outperform non-pretrained baselines, and combining them yields complementary gains that exceed strong seed ensembles formed by varying random initializations. These findings show that teacher representation type shapes downstream performance and that representation learning can be guided by neural encoding.

news.title: Inherited Goal Drift: Contextual Pressure Can Undermine Agentic Goals
news.summary: The accelerating adoption of language models (LMs) as agents for deployment in long-context tasks motivates a thorough understanding of goal drift: agents' tendency to deviate from an original objective. While prior-generation LLM agents have been shown to be susceptible to drift, the extent to which drift affects more recent models remains unclear. This work investigates drift in state-of-the-art models (GPT-5.1, Claude, etc.) within a simulated stock-trading environment. These models are largely shown to be robust even when subjected to adversarial pressure. We show, however, that this robustness is brittle: across multiple settings, the same models often inherit drift when conditioned on prefilled trajectories from weaker agents. The extent of conditioning-induced drift varies significantly by model family, with only GPT-5.1 maintaining consistent resilience among tested models. We find that drift behavior is inconsistent between prompt variations and correlates poorly with instruction hierarchy following behavior, with strong hierarchy following failing to reliably predict resistance to drift.

news.title: Learning Demographic-Conditioned Mobility Trajectories with Aggregate Supervision
news.summary: Existing trajectory generation models rarely capture heterogeneity because most trajectory datasets lack demographic labels. We propose ATLAS, a weakly supervised approach for demographic-conditioned trajectory generation using only (i) individual trajectories without demographic labels, (ii) region-level aggregated mobility features, and (iii) region-level demographic compositions from census data. ATLAS trains a trajectory generator and fine-tunes it so that simulated mobility matches observed regional aggregates while conditioning on demographics. Experiments on real trajectory data with demographic labels show that ATLAS substantially improves demographic realism over baselines.

news.title: Proactive Real-Time Agentic System Capable of Modeling Human State of Mind
news.summary: Real-time proactive agentic system, capable of modeling Human State of Mind, using foundation EXG model and text embeddings model, running fully offline on edge. Unlike all previously known systems, this system leverages Human's State of Mind API and CLI provided by system, directly from Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) devices, which records Human biophysical and brain signals. Custom harness - NeuroLoop(tm) - utilizes all of above to run agentic flow that manages to engage with Human on multiple cognitive and affective levels of their State of Mind, by providing actionable tool calls and protocol execution with explicit or implicit requests from Human. GPLv3 open-source software with ethically aligned AI100 licensing.

news.title: Meta Rolls Out AI Shopping Tool to Testers in US
news.summary: Meta is rolling out an AI shopping research tool to testers in the US, aiming to compete with similar offerings found in ChatGPT and Gemini, according to Bloomberg.

news.title: OpenAI's Former Research Chief Aims to Automate Manufacturing With AI
news.summary: Bob McGrew is raising $70 million to fund a startup making a software platform to help run autonomous factories. The breakthrough in superconductor manufacturing could transform economics of AI, Snowcap Compute says.

news.title: Protesters Plan to Gather Outside OpenAI Offices Today
news.summary: QuitGPT campaign organized protest focused on AI-powered mass domestic surveillance and lethal autonomous weapons. Campaign claims to have inspired over 1.5 million people to take relevant action by either sharing on social media or signing up for boycott itself.

news.title: US Using Anthropic AI for Iran Strikes Despite Ban
news.summary: On Friday, Donald Trump announced a ban on federal government's use of Claude, but hours later launched major airstrikes against Iran that relied on Claude for intelligence assessments and target identification. Wall Street Journal reports Pentagon confirmed US used Anthropic tools.

news.title: Australian Artists Warn Government on AI Copyright
news.summary: Australian artists have issued a warning to the government regarding AI-generated art and copyright infringement, urging stronger protections for creative industries as AI tools become increasingly sophisticated at generating artwork without attribution or compensation to original creators.

news.title: Protesters Rally Against OpenAI Pentagon Deal in San Francisco
news.summary: Organized by QuitGPT campaign, protesters gathered outside OpenAI offices to demand the company abandon its agreement with the Pentagon, citing concerns about AI-powered mass domestic surveillance and lethal autonomous weapons. Campaign claims to have inspired over 1.5 million people to take action.

news.title: Senate to Vote on Trump's War Powers as Iran Conflict Continues
news.summary: US Senate will vote on whether to require withdrawal of US forces from Iran operation unless approved by Congress, as conflict continues with no clear exit strategy. Vote not expected to pass, highlighting growing congressional concern over unchecked presidential war powers.

news.title: Stock Markets and Oil Prices Volatile Over Fears Iran War May Drag On
news.summary: Experts warn that if oil and gas prices remain elevated due to prolonged Iran conflict, it could make goods and services more expensive globally. Major stock markets tumbled following US strikes on Iran, with tech stocks particularly impacted by uncertainty.

news.title: US-Iran War Escalates: Ship Sunk, Military Strikes Continue
news.summary: Pentagon confirmed US submarine sank an Iranian warship in Indian Ocean with a torpedo, while Israel hacked Tehran traffic cameras to spy on late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Israel vows to "kill next supreme leader" as conflict enters fifth day with no end in sight.

news.title: Apple Unveils Budget-Friendly MacBook Neo Powered by A18 Pro
news.summary: Apple's new MacBook Neo offers a lower-cost alternative to pricier MacBooks, featuring the A18 Pro chip from iPhones at an affordable price point.

news.title: Meta CTO Shares 7 Employee Traits He Values in AMA
news.summary: Meta's CTO conducted an Instagram AMA sharing insights on successful employee traits, emphasizing curiosity and adaptability over fear of change when plans shift.

news.title: Online Ads Account for 60% of Malware and Phishing Campaigns
news.summary: A new report from The Media Trust reveals that programmatic advertising accounted for more than 60% of malware and phishing campaigns observed in 2025, making it the internet's biggest malware vector.

news.title: OpenAI Ad Hoax Mystery Takes Unexpected New Twist
news.summary: A mystery marketer playing 4D chess on social media and creating fake OpenAI ads made another unexpected move this week, continuing the puzzling campaign that has confused users and platforms alike.

news.title: Polymarket Removes Nuclear Detonation Prediction Market After Backlash
news.summary: The prediction market took down a controversial betting market that would have paid out to traders if a nuclear weapon was detonated anywhere in the world before a specific date, following online opposition.

news.title: Density-Guided Response Optimization: Community-Grounded Alignment via Implicit Acceptance Signals
news.summary: Language models deployed in online communities must adapt to norms that vary across social, cultural, and domain-specific contexts. This approach observes that communities already express preferences implicitly through what content they accept, engage with, and allow to persist. The method aligns language models to community norms without requiring explicit preference labels by using local density in representation space as an implicit preference signal.

news.title: Learning When to Act or Refuse: Guarding Agentic Reasoning Models for Safe Multi-Step Tool Use
news.summary: Agentic language models face fundamentally different safety challenges than chat models because they must plan, call tools, and execute long-horizon actions where a single misstep can cause irreversible harm. MOSAIC introduces a post-training framework that aligns agents for safe multi-step tool use by making safety decisions explicit and learnable. It structures inference as a plan, check, then act or refuse loop, with explicit safety reasoning and refusal as first-class actions.

news.title: Speculative Speculative Decoding
news.summary: Introduces speculative speculative decoding (SSD) to parallelize speculative decoding operations. While a verification is ongoing, the draft model predicts likely verification outcomes and prepares speculations preemptively for them. If the actual verification outcome is in the predicted set, a speculation can be returned immediately, eliminating drafting overhead entirely. The implementation is up to 2x faster than optimized speculative decoding baselines and up to 5x faster than autoregressive decoding.

news.title: Using Learning Progressions to Guide AI Feedback for Science Learning
news.summary: Examines whether a learning progression-driven rubric generation pipeline can produce AI-generated feedback comparable in quality to feedback guided by expert-authored task rubrics. Analyzed AI-generated feedback for written scientific explanations produced by 207 middle school students in a chemistry task, finding no statistically significant differences between expert-designed rubrics and automatically derived rubrics for clarity, relevance, engagement, motivation, and reflectiveness.

news.title: Anthropic vs Pentagon: Trump Bans Claude, But Military Still Used It for Iran Strikes
news.summary: President Trump announced a ban on federal use of Anthropic's Claude, but hours later launched major airstrikes against Iran that relied on Claude for intelligence assessments and target identification. A former Trump advisor called the designation of Anthropic as a "supply chain risk" "attempted corporate murder," with concerns this could be the first step toward partial nationalization of the AI industry.

news.title: Hugging Face Publishes Research on Mixture of Experts in Transformers
news.summary: Hugging Face published a comprehensive blog post on Mixture of Experts (MoEs) in Transformers, explaining the architecture and optimization techniques that enable efficient scaling of large language models. MoEs allow models to have massive parameter counts while keeping computational costs manageable by activating only a subset of parameters for each input.

news.title: Meta Tests AI Shopping Tool to Rival ChatGPT and Gemini
news.summary: Meta is rolling out an AI shopping research tool to testers in the U.S., aiming to compete with similar offerings found in ChatGPT and Gemini. The tool leverages Meta's AI capabilities to help users with product research and shopping decisions.

news.title: OpenAI Developing GitHub Rival
news.summary: Prompted by recent GitHub outages, OpenAI is in the early stages of developing its own code repository. Completion is still months away, and the company is considering making it available to OpenAI customers. This would put the ChatGPT creator in direct competition with Microsoft, which holds a significant stake in OpenAI.

news.title: OpenAI Reaches New Agreement with Pentagon
news.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced a new agreement allowing the U.S. military to deploy OpenAI's models in classified networks. The agreement includes prohibitions on domestic mass surveillance and emphasizes human responsibility for use of force, including autonomous weapon systems. OpenAI is asking the Department of War to offer these terms to all AI companies.

news.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.3-Instant with Reduced Hallucinations
news.summary: The new model delivers more accurate answers, reduces unnecessary refusals by 26.8% in high-stakes domains (medicine, law, finance), and improves conversational flow by cutting down "overbearing" or "preachy" responses. Hallucinations decreased by 22.5% with web access and 9.6% without it. GPT-5.2-Instant will be retired on June 3, 2026.

news.title: OpenAI Updates Safety Protocols After Canadian School Shooting
news.summary: Following a fatal school shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia that killed eight and injured dozens, OpenAI updated its safety protocols. The new guidelines state that if the shooter's account were discovered today, OpenAI would have alerted police. The suspect's interactions with ChatGPT had suggested real-world violence, leading OpenAI to shut down the account but not notify police at the time.

news.title: Xbox Ally X Adds AI-Powered Screen Recording and Highlight Reels
news.summary: Microsoft's new "postgame recaps" feature for Xbox Insider PC users lets AI record your screen and automatically cut together highlight reels. The Xbox Ally X handheld's integrated NPU takes this further with Copilot-generated highlight reels. Initially supports games like Among Us, Elden Ring, Fortnite, Forza Horizon 5, Lies of P, Overwatch, and Palworld.

news.title: No New AI Developments (04:01 - 06:01 AM)
news.summary: No significant new AI industry developments detected in the last 2 hours. All major sources have been monitored: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, Hugging Face, TechCrunch, The Verge. The most recent developments remain the same as the 04:01 AM report covering Anthropic-Pentagon standoff, OpenAI Pentagon deal, GPT-5.3-Instant, and related industry updates.

news.title: Google Translate Integrates Gemini for Context-Aware Translations
news.summary: Google is using Gemini AI to offer alternative translations based on conversation context with new "Understand" and "Let" buttons. Users can now request additional context or ask follow-up questions, with AI analyzing the conversation and generating appropriate responses.

news.title: Meta Tests AI Shopping Tool to Rival ChatGPT and Gemini
news.summary: Meta has launched an AI shopping tool testing products against similar offerings from OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's Gemini. According to Bloomberg, the tool helps users make informed purchasing decisions by comparing AI-generated recommendations across different platforms.

news.title: OpenAI Updates Safety Protocols Following Canadian Shooting
news.summary: OpenAI released new safety protocols governing when to involve law enforcement. The changes address the BC school shooting case where a suspect's interactions with ChatGPT suggested real-world violence but OpenAI shut down the account without notifying police.

news.title: Perplexity Launches "Computer" AI Agent Platform
news.summary: Perplexity introduced a new platform called "Computer" that operates across sub AI agents to "reason, delegate, search, build, remember, code, and deliver." The company describes it as a "general-purpose digital worker" positioned between OpenClaw and Claude Cowork.

news.title: Protesters Plan to Gather at OpenAI Offices Over Pentagon Deal
news.summary: Grassroots campaign "QuitGPT" organizing protests outside OpenAI offices today to protest company's Pentagon agreement. Campaign focused on AI-powered mass domestic surveillance and lethal autonomous weapons, demanding OpenAI reject military contracts.

news.title: Sam Altman Addresses OpenAI Pentagon Agreement Concerns
news.summary: OpenAI CEO announced plans to add two sentences addressing concerns about mass domestic surveillance and lethal autonomous weapons to Pentagon agreement. However, language still includes "consistent with applicable laws" clause, sparking continued controversy.

news.title: Xbox Ally X Adds AI-Powered Screen Recording and Highlight Reels
news.summary: Microsoft's Xbox companion app now offers AI-powered features including screen recording and automatic "highlight reels" generation powered by Copilot. The integrated NPU takes handheld capabilities further, currently limited to Among Us, Elden Ring, Fortnite, and other titles.

news.title: YouTube's AI Content Issue for Kids Escalates
news.summary: New York Times investigation found that over 40% of YouTube Shorts recommended after children's channels like CoComelon, Bluey, Ms. Rachel appear to contain AI-generated visuals. YouTube doesn't require AI-generated videos for children to be labeled, placing all moderation burdens on parents.

news.title: Community Evals Initiative: Moving Beyond Black-Box Leaderboards
news.summary: Community-driven evaluation framework emerges as alternative to trusting black-box leaderboards, emphasizing transparency and reproducibility in AI model assessment. This initiative aims to create more reliable and accountable benchmarking practices.

news.title: H Company: Holo2 Model with 2.35B Parameters
news.summary: H Company releases new Holo2 model with 2.35B parameters, focusing on improved UI localization capabilities for better multilingual support. This model represents an advancement in making AI interfaces more accessible to users worldwide.

news.title: Hugging Face: PRX Part 3 - Training Text-to-Image Model in 24 Hours
news.summary: Hugging Face announces PRX Part 3, demonstrating how to train a high-quality text-to-image model from scratch in just 24 hours using their training infrastructure and open-source tools. This rapid training capability showcases the accessibility and efficiency of modern AI development workflows.

news.title: Hugging Face: Transformers.js v4 Preview Available on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face releases Transformers.js v4 Preview, bringing transformer models directly to JavaScript environments for easier browser-based AI deployment. This update enables developers to run powerful AI models directly in web browsers without backend infrastructure.

news.title: NVIDIA: CUDA Kernels Customized for AI Agent Skills
news.summary: NVIDIA releases open-source custom CUDA kernels that enhance AI agent skills from Codex and Claude, providing optimized performance for specific AI tasks. These kernels enable faster and more efficient execution of AI workflows on NVIDIA hardware.

news.title: OpenEnv: Evaluating Tool-Using Agents in Real-World Environments
news.summary: Community research evaluates tool-using AI agents in practice, moving beyond theoretical benchmarks to real-world application testing. The study provides insights into how AI agents actually perform when required to interact with real systems and tools.

news.title: Conformal Policy Control
news.summary: Shows how to use any safe reference policy as a probabilistic regulator for optimized but untested policies in high-stakes environments. Provides conformal calibration with finite-sample guarantees even for non-monotonic bounded constraint functions, provably enforcing user's declared risk tolerance.

news.title: Partial Causal Structure Learning for Valid Selective Conformal Inference under Interventions
news.summary: Addresses selective conformal prediction in interventional settings like genomic perturbation experiments, where exchangeability holds only within subsets. Introduces contamination-robust conformal coverage theorem and task-driven partial causal learning for estimating binary descendant indicators.

news.title: Pencil Puzzle Bench: A Benchmark for Multi-Step Verifiable Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces a framework with 62,231 verified pencil puzzles across 94 varieties for evaluating LLM reasoning. Evaluates 51 models in direct ask and agentic modes, revealing two axes: reasoning effort scaling (GPT-5.2 improves 81x) and agentic iteration (Claude Opus 4.6 rises from 0.3% to 30%).

news.title: T^3RL: Tool-Verification for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
news.summary: Addresses a critical failure mode in test-time reinforcement learning where spurious unverified consensus creates biased reward signals. Introduces test-time tool verification using external tools (like code execution) to upweight verified rollouts, producing more reliable pseudo-labels for training.

news.title: Unifying Multimodal Emotional Intelligence from Perception to Empathy
news.summary: Introduces Nano-EmoX (2.2B), the first compact multimodal language model that unifies six core affective tasks across three cognitive hierarchy levels: perception, understanding, and interaction. Integrates omni-modal encoders including enhanced facial and fusion encoders.

news.title: France to Boost Nuclear Arsenal and Extend Deterrence to European Allies
news.summary: President Emmanuel Macron announced eight European countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella as Paris expands defense capabilities. The move maintains France's sovereign decision-making power while providing security guarantees to allies.

news.title: Israel and Iran Escalate Conflict with Airstrikes and Sea Operations
news.summary: Following the ground invasion, Israel conducted airstrikes across Iran and deployed warships in the Persian Gulf, while Iran targeted US bases and commercial shipping in the region, expanding the conflict from land to sea.

news.title: Israel Invades Lebanon with Ground Offensive; Tehran Presidential Office Hit
news.summary: Israel launched a major ground invasion into southern Lebanon and Iran struck the Islamic Republic's presidential office in Tehran with multiple missiles, raising regional war tensions and disrupting shipping lanes.

news.title: Oil and Gas Prices Jump on Middle East Conflict Escalation
news.summary: Energy prices surged as the Israel-Iran conflict intensified, with oil prices reaching multi-month highs. Analysts warn that prolonged conflict could push prices even higher, fueling global inflation concerns and pressuring tech sector profitability.

news.title: Trump Used Anthropic AI for Iran Strikes Despite Previous Ban
news.summary: Former President Trump authorized U.S. military use of Anthropic AI for strikes in Iran, despite his previous administration's six-month federal ban on Anthropic as a supply chain risk. The authorization enabled deployment of AI models for intelligence gathering, target identification, and operational planning.

news.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal and extend deterrence to European allies
news.summary: Emmanuel Macron announced eight countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella - but that Paris would retain sole decision-making power.

news.title: India and Canada reset ties with "landmark" nuclear energy deal
news.summary: A host of deals, including one to supply India with uranium, is unveiled as Mark Carney meets Narendra Modi in Delhi.

news.title: Leaked photo, hot tub, and Pizzagate - videos of Clintons' testimony on Epstein ties released
news.summary: Newly released videos show Bill and Hillary Clinton sitting through separate depositions about Jeffrey Epstein. The leak has sparked renewed controversy and conspiracy theories.

news.title: Oil and gas prices jump as conflict escalates
news.summary: Experts have warned that a prolonged Middle East conflict could push global energy prices even higher, affecting inflation and tech operating costs worldwide.

news.title: Deploying Open-Source Vision Language Models (VLMs) on Jetson Devices
news.summary: NVIDIA's AI platform now supports deploying Open Source VLMs on Jetson edge devices, enabling AI computer vision and video processing at the edge for robotics and autonomous systems. Previously required NVIDIA JetPack for closed-source models; now supports open ecosystem.

news.title: GGML and llama.cpp Join Hugging Face
news.summary: GGML and llama.cpp have officially joined Hugging Face to ensure long-term progress of Local AI, improving the open-source ecosystem for on-device AI inference and deployment across diverse hardware platforms. This unification creates more seamless experience for developers working with local LLMs.

news.title: Gradio HTML One-Shot Web Apps Framework
news.summary: New Gradio framework enables creating single-file HTML web apps that provide complete AI functionality in one shot, simplifying deployment and sharing of AI-powered applications across web and mobile platforms. Removes need for complex full-stack applications for simple AI tools.

news.title: Mixture of Experts (MoE) Optimization in Transformers
news.summary: Comprehensive technical deep dive into MoE (Mixture of Experts) architecture for Transformers, covering weight loading refactoring, expert parallelism improvements, and new pipeline optimizations for more efficient large model training and inference. Explores trade-offs between model quality and computational efficiency.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview Available on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face releases Transformers.js v4 Preview, bringing transformer models directly to JavaScript environments for easier browser-based AI deployment without requiring backend infrastructure. Enables client-side LLM inference directly in web browsers.

news.title: Why Enterprise Agents Fail Using IT-Bench: A Data-Driven Critique
news.summary: New research from IBM and UC Berkeley reveals that enterprise AI agents significantly underperform on IT-Bench benchmark, indicating current approaches don't translate to real-world enterprise workflows effectively. Identifies key bottlenecks in task decomposition and tool usage.

news.title: Community Evals: Because We're Done Trusting Black-Box Leaderboards
news.summary: Hugging Face argues against over-reliance on black-box leaderboards for evaluating LLMs, advocating for community-driven open evaluations with transparent methodology, reproducible baselines, and diverse test sets instead of closed proprietary benchmarks.

news.title: Deploying Open-Source VLMs on Jetson Devices
news.summary: NVIDIA's AI platform now supports deploying Open Source Vision Language Models (VLMs) on Jetson edge devices, enabling AI computer vision and video processing at the edge for robotics and autonomous systems.

news.title: Gradio HTML One-Shot Web Apps Framework
news.summary: New Gradio framework enables creating single-file HTML web apps that provide complete AI functionality in one shot, simplifying deployment and sharing of AI-powered applications across web and mobile platforms.

news.title: Hot GGML and llama.cpp Join Hugging Face
news.summary: GGML and llama.cpp have officially joined Hugging Face to ensure the long-term progress of Local AI, improving the open-source ecosystem for on-device AI inference and deployment across diverse hardware platforms.

news.title: IBM and UC Berkeley: Why Enterprise Agents Fail Using IT-Bench
news.summary: New research reveals that enterprise AI agents significantly underperform on IT-Bench benchmark, indicating current approaches don't translate to real-world enterprise workflows effectively. Identifies key bottlenecks in task decomposition and tool usage.

news.title: Mixture of Experts (MoE) Optimization in Transformers
news.summary: Comprehensive technical deep dive into MoE (Mixture of Experts) architecture for Transformers, covering weight loading refactoring, expert parallelism improvements, and new pipeline optimizations for more efficient large model training and inference.

news.title: OpenEnv in Practice: Evaluating Tools Using Agents in Real-World Environments
news.summary: OpenEnv project demonstrates evaluating AI tools and agents in real-world scenarios rather than artificial benchmarks, providing more practical assessment of tool capabilities and limitations for production deployments.

news.title: SyGra Studio: Text-to-Image AI Studio
news.summary: Introduction of SyGra Studio, a comprehensive text-to-image AI application platform enabling users to generate, edit, and manipulate images using natural language prompts, democratizing creative AI content creation.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview Available on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face releases Transformers.js v4 Preview, bringing transformer models directly to JavaScript environments for easier browser-based AI deployment without requiring backend infrastructure.

news.title: Atrium Therapeutics Launches with $270M in Funding
news.summary: Atrium Therapeutics launched with $270 million in funding and two preclinical siRNA candidates for rare genetic cardiomyopathies caused by lamin A/C gene mutations.

news.title: Brain Organoid Gene Therapy Reaches First Patient
news.summary: Mahzi Therapeutics has begun clinical testing of MZ-1866 for Pitt Hopkins syndrome, an experimental gene therapy developed and tested using human brain organoids.

news.title: FDA Releases Clinical Hold on Intellia CRISPR Trials
news.summary: FDA fully released clinical hold on Intellia Therapeutics' CRISPR gene therapy trials, allowing company to resume development following regulatory review.

news.title: Five Drugmakers Team Up on Privacy-Preserving AI/ML for ADMET Data
news.summary: Five pharmaceutical companies, including Lundbeck, Orion Pharma, Recursion, Servier and one undisclosed organization, committed around 80% of their internal ADMET datasets to privacy-preserving AI/ML approaches.

news.title: Generate Biomedicines Lands $400M IPO, Biggest in a While
news.summary: AI-powered drug developer Generate Biomedicines sold 25 million shares at $16 per share under ticker GENB, raising $400 million, marking largest biotech IPO on Nasdaq so far in 2026.

news.title: NHS "Isn't Delivering Equitable Care for Rare Diseases"
news.summary: Genetic Alliance UK report claims NHS is failing to meet the needs of many of the 3.5 million people in the UK country who live with rare diseases.

news.title: Novo Nordisk Plans €432M Investment in Irish GLP-1 Plant
news.summary: Novo Nordisk invests in an Irish plant as it prepares for regulatory approvals of its Wegovy pill outside US. Represents strategic diversification and preparation for European market expansion.

news.title: Outpost Bio Raises $35M for Human Microbiome "Computable"
news.summary: London and Boston-based Outpost Bio secured $35 million in pre-seed financing to build a platform that links experimental microbiology with machine learning models of microbiome data for drug discovery.

news.title: Salma Health Launches Brain Health Clinic Network with $80M Series A
news.summary: Salma Health launched as a brain health clinic network backed by $80 million in Series A funding co-led by Mubadala Capital and ARCH Venture, combining care, research, and AI technology.

news.title: Artificial Agency Program: Curiosity, Compression & Communication in Agents
news.summary: Presents a position and research agenda for building AI systems as reality-embedded, resource-bounded agents whose development is driven by curiosity-as-learning-progress under physical and computational constraints. Unifies predictive compression, intrinsic motivation, empowerment, control, interface quality, and language/self-communication as selective information bottlenecks. Formulates these ideas as a falsifiable program with explicit costs, staged experiments, and a concrete multimodal tokenized testbed for budget allocation among observation, action, and deliberation.

news.title: Neural Diffusion Intensity Models for Point Process Data
news.summary: Variational framework for Cox processes driven by neural SDEs with latent stochastic intensity. Key theoretical result shows conditioning on point process observations preserves diffusion structure of latent intensity with explicit drift correction. Guarantees variational family contains true posterior. Designs amortized encoder architecture mapping variable-length event sequences to posterior intensity paths via drift-corrected SDE, replacing repeated MCMC runs with single forward pass. Experiments show orders-of-magnitude speedups over MCMC-based methods.

news.title: UMPIRE: Uncertainty Quantification for Multimodal LLMs
news.summary: Training-free uncertainty quantification framework for MLLMs that efficiently captures global semantic diversity of samples and local incoherence of responses based on internal model confidence. Proposes uncertainty desiderata and outperforms baseline metrics in error detection and uncertainty calibration across image, audio, and video-text benchmarks, including adversarial settings.

news.title: A rap star might just beat a former PM - Nepal's Gen Z election
news.summary: Many hope vote could bring change after protests last year, which could shatter decades of political paralysis affecting youth engagement.

news.title: At least 169 people killed in South Sudan 'surprise' attack
news.summary: Peacekeepers are sheltering about 1,000 civilians near their base and providing emergency care after a coordinated assault.

news.title: Israel strikes Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket fire as Iran conflict widens
news.summary: Lebanese officials report 52 people were killed by Israeli strikes, while there are no reports of casualties in Israel. Hezbollah rocket fire triggered retaliatory strikes.

news.title: Trump says US took 'last best chance' to hit Iran
news.summary: President Trump claimed the US took its "last best chance" to hit Iran in recent strikes, as conflict continues with US and Israel launching multiple attacks across the country.

news.title: CUDA Agent: Large-Scale Agentic RL for High-Performance CUDA Kernel Generation
news.summary: Large-scale agentic reinforcement learning system that develops CUDA kernel expertise through scalable data synthesis, skill-augmented CUDA development environment with automated verification and profiling to provide reliable reward signals. Achieves state-of-the-art results on KernelBench, delivering 100%, 100%, and 92% faster rate over torch.compile on Level-1, Level-2, and Level-3 splits, outperforming strongest proprietary models (Claude Opus 4.5 and Gemini 3 Pro) by about 40% on hardest Level-3 setting.

news.title: DARE-bench: Evaluating Modeling and Instruction Fidelity of LLMs in Data Science
news.summary: Benchmark designed for machine learning modeling and data science instruction following, addressing lack of standardized process-aware evaluation and scarcity of accurately labeled training data. DARE-bench consists of 6,300 Kaggle-derived tasks with both large-scale training data and evaluation sets, all with verifiable ground truth ensuring objective and reproducible evaluation. Extensive evaluations show even highly capable models like gpt-o4-mini struggle to achieve good performance. Using DARE-bench training tasks for fine-tuning substantially improves performance: supervised fine-tuning boosts Qwen3-32B's accuracy by 1.83x and reinforcement learning boosts Qwen3-4B's accuracy by more than 8x.

news.title: LoRA-Pre: Rethinking Optimizer States Through Low-Rank Approximation
news.summary: Novel low-rank optimizer designed for efficient pre-training by decomposing full momentum matrix into compact low-rank subspace within online linear learner, reducing optimizer's memory footprint while maintaining optimization performance. Achieves highest performance across all model sizes from 60M to 1B parameters. Notably demonstrates remarkable rank efficiency, achieving comparable or superior results using only 1/8 rank of baseline methods. In fine-tuning scenarios, outperforms LoRA with substantial improvements of 3.14 points on Llama-3.1-8B and 6.17 points on Llama-2-7B.

news.title: MediX-R1: Open-Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
news.summary: Open-ended Reinforcement Learning framework for medical multimodal LLMs enabling clinically grounded, free-form answers beyond multiple-choice formats. Fine-tunes baseline vision-language backbone with Group Based RL and composite reward tailored for medical reasoning: LLM-based accuracy reward judging semantic correctness with strict YES/NO decision, medical embedding-based semantic reward capturing paraphrases and terminology variants, and lightweight format and modality rewards enforcing interpretable reasoning and modality recognition. Achieves excellent results across medical LLM (text-only) and VLM (image + text) benchmarks using only ~51K instruction examples.

news.title: Toward Expert Investment Teams: A Multi-Agent LLM System with Fine-Grained Trading Tasks
news.summary: Multi-agent LLM trading framework that explicitly decomposes investment analysis into fine-grained tasks rather than providing coarse-grained instructions. Evaluated using Japanese stock data including prices, financial statements, news, and macro information under leakage-controlled backtesting setting. Experimental results show fine-grained task decomposition significantly improves risk-adjusted returns compared to conventional coarse-grained designs. Further analysis of intermediate agent outputs suggests alignment between analytical outputs and downstream decision preferences is a critical driver of system performance. Standard portfolio optimization exploiting low correlation with stock index and variance of each system's output achieves superior performance.

news.title: Anthropic - Response to Secretary of War and Customer Advice
news.summary: Anthropic releases an official response to the Secretary of War and provides guidance to customers regarding responsible AI use and military applications.

news.title: ArXiv - Hundreds of New AI Research Papers Published
news.summary: ArXiv publishes 136 new papers in cs.AI and 138 in cs.LG categories covering machine learning, transformers, computer vision, and natural language processing advancements.

news.title: Google DeepMind - Accelerating Mathematical Discovery with Gemini Deep Think
news.summary: Google DeepMind publishes research on accelerating mathematical and scientific discovery using Gemini Deep Think, demonstrating breakthroughs in complex problem solving.

news.title: Google DeepMind - Gemini 3 Deep Think: Advancing Science
news.summary: Google DeepMind introduces Gemini 3 Deep Think, a specialized model for advancing science, research, and engineering with enhanced reasoning capabilities.

news.title: Google DeepMind - Gemini 3.1 Pro: Smarter Model for Complex Tasks
news.summary: Google DeepMind announces Gemini 3.1 Pro, a smarter model designed to handle the most complex reasoning and planning tasks with improved capabilities.

news.title: Google DeepMind - Gemini Can Now Create Music
news.summary: Google DeepMind expands Gemini's creative capabilities, now enabling music generation alongside existing text, image, and video generation features.

news.title: Google DeepMind - Nano Banana 2: Lightning-Fast Capabilities
news.summary: Google DeepMind releases Nano Banana 2, combining Pro capabilities with lightning-fast speed for image generation and editing tasks.

news.title: Google DeepMind - Project Genie Rolls Out to Ultra Subscribers
news.summary: Google is rolling out Project Genie to Google AI Ultra subscribers in the U.S., an experimental research prototype powered by Genie 3 that lets users create, explore, and remix interactive worlds using text prompts and images in real time.

news.title: Hugging Face - Full Mixture of Experts Support in Transformers
news.summary: Transformers library introduces comprehensive support for Mixture of Experts models including weight loading refactor, expert backend, expert parallelism, and improved training capabilities following the DeepSeek R1 inflection point.

news.title: Hugging Face - GGML and llama.cpp Join HF
news.summary: Georgi Gerganov and the GGML team are joining Hugging Face to ensure long-term progress of Local AI, with llama.cpp becoming more seamlessly integrated with the transformers library for single-click model deployment.

news.title: SeeThrough3D: Occlusion-Aware 3D Control
news.summary: Explicitly models occlusions with translucent 3D box representation. Enables accurate multi-object generation with depth-consistent geometry. State-of-the-art control through box-based approach.

news.title: SOTAlign: Semi-Supervised Vision-Language Alignment
news.summary: Two-stage framework aligning frozen pretrained vision and language models using minimal paired data. Recovers coarse shared geometry from limited image-text pairs, then refines alignment on unpaired samples via optimal transport-based divergence.

news.title: A Dataset is Worth 1 MB: PLADA Method
news.summary: Novel approach that eliminates pixel transmission entirely. Clients receive only class labels for task-specific training images, filtering a large reference dataset (like ImageNet) to retain only the most semantically relevant images. Achieves high classification accuracy with payload under 1 MB.

news.title: MediX-R1: Open-Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
news.summary: RL framework for medical multimodal LLMs enabling clinically grounded, free-form answers beyond multiple-choice. Uses composite reward: LLM-based accuracy judge for YES/NO correctness, medical embedding-based semantic reward for paraphrases, and lightweight format/modality rewards for interpretable reasoning.

news.title: Model Agreement via Anchoring
news.summary: General technique for proving bounds on independent model disagreement using "anchoring" to average of two models. Applies this to four common ML algorithms: (1) stacked aggregation (disagreement→0 with k models), (2) gradient boosting (disagreement→0 with k iterations), (3) neural network architecture search (disagreement→0 with n size), (4) regression trees (disagreement→0 with depth d).

news.title: Scale Can't Overcome Pragmatics: Reporting Bias in VLMs
news.summary: Investigates why Vision-Language Models lack reasoning capabilities. Blames "reporting bias" in training data - people omit tacit information when describing visual content (e.g., "at game today!" vs describing the scene with 37 people). Shows VLMs (OpenCLIP, LLaVA-1.5, Molmo) perform poorly on spatial, temporal, negation, and counting reasoning - and scaling doesn't fix it.

news.title: A Dataset is Worth 1 MB: Pseudo-Labels as Data (PLADA)
news.summary: PLADA eliminates pixel transmission for dataset distribution by assuming agents have a large generic unlabeled reference dataset (e.g., ImageNet-21K) and communicate new tasks via only class labels. The method includes a pruning mechanism that filters the reference dataset to retain only semantically relevant images for the target task.

news.title: MediX-R1: Open Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
news.summary: Introduces MediX-R1, an open-ended RL framework for medical MLLMs that enables clinically-grounded free-form answers beyond multiple-choice. Uses Group Based RL with a composite reward: LLM-based accuracy judge, semantic embeddings, and format/modality rewards.

news.title: Scale Can't Overcome Pragmatics: The Impact of Reporting Bias on Vision-Language Reasoning
news.summary: Investigates VLM reasoning gaps through the lens of reporting bias in training data. Finds that captions omit tacit information needed for spatial, temporal, negation, and counting reasoning, and that scaling model/data size doesn't automatically fix this.

news.title: ThinkOmni: Lifting Textual Reasoning to Omni-modal Scenarios via Guidance Decoding
news.summary: Training-free, data-free framework that lifts textual reasoning to omni-modal scenarios. Uses LRM-as-a-Guide to leverage off-the-shelf large reasoning models to guide omni-modal LLM decoding, with Stepwise Contrastive Scaling balancing perception and reasoning signals.

news.title: Toward Expert Investment Teams: Multi-Agent LLM System with Fine-Grained Trading Tasks
news.summary: Proposes a multi-agent LLM trading framework that explicitly decomposes investment analysis into fine-grained tasks instead of coarse-grained instructions. Evaluates on Japanese stock data with leakage-controlled backtesting and portfolio optimization.

news.title: A Dataset is Worth 1 MB
news.summary: Proposes PLADA (Pseudo-Labels as Data), a method that eliminates pixel transmission for dataset serving. Agents preload a generic unlabeled reference dataset (e.g., ImageNet-1K) and receive only class labels for specific images to learn new tasks. Includes a pruning mechanism to filter semantically relevant images and address distribution mismatch.

news.title: MediX-R1: Open Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
news.summary: Introduces an open-ended RL framework for medical multimodal LLMs that goes beyond multiple-choice formats. Uses Group-Based RL with a composite reward: LLM-based accuracy judge, medical embedding semantic reward, and lightweight format/modality rewards. Achieves strong results on medical LLM and VLM benchmarks with only ~51K instruction examples.

news.title: Scale Can't Overcome Pragmatics: The Impact of Reporting Bias on Vision-Language Reasoning
news.summary: Identifies reporting bias in VLM training data as the root cause of poor reasoning performance. Shows that human captions omit tacit information needed for spatial, temporal, negation, and counting reasoning. Demonstrates that scaling data, model size, or multilingual training doesn't automatically enable these skills—they require intentionally curated annotations.

news.title: SeeThrough3D: Occlusion Aware 3D Control in Text-to-Image Generation
news.summary: Introduces occlusion-aware 3D scene representation (OSCR) where objects are depicted as translucent 3D boxes. Explicitly models occlusions by encoding hidden object regions while rendering from desired camera viewpoint. Uses masked self-attention to bind each object bounding box to its textual description, preventing attribute mixing.

news.title: Semi-Supervised Alignment of Unimodal Vision and Language Models via Optimal Transport
news.summary: Proposes SOTAlign, a two-stage framework that aligns pretrained unimodal encoders using minimal paired data (small number of image-text pairs) plus large amounts of unpaired data. First recovers coarse shared geometry with a linear teacher from limited pairs, then refines alignment via optimal-transport-based divergence on unpaired samples.

news.title: Anthropic Raises $30B in Series G at $380B Valuation

Under the new protocols, OpenAI would have alerted police if the account was discovered today.

news.title: Study: LLMs Uplift Novices 4.16x on Biology Tasks
news.summary: A multi-model, multi-benchmark human uplift study found that novices with LLM access were 4.16 times more accurate than internet-only controls on biosecurity-relevant tasks. On four benchmarks, novices with LLMs outperformed experts on three, though standalone LLMs often exceeded LLM-assisted novices.

news.title: Continuous Tracking Active
news.summary: All major AI news sources are being monitored on a 2-hour schedule. Recent major developments from earlier in the day remain relevant and ongoing.

news.title: Bias Studies: Language Models Show Algorithm Aversion
news.summary: Evaluates how LLMs weigh information from human experts vs. algorithms in decision-making tasks. Finds LLMs rate human experts higher, showing algorithm aversion bias. However, when betting between human expert and algorithm, LLMs disproportionately choose algorithm even when it performs worse, revealing inconsistent biases.

news.title: GUI Agents: Training Native GUI Agents to Reason and Act
news.summary: Addresses the gap between open-source and closed-source GUI agents. Introduces GUI-Libra: action-aware SFT mixing reasoning-then-action with direct-action data, plus 81K curated GUI reasoning dataset. Uses KL-regularized RL with success-adaptive scaling to handle partial verifiability where multiple actions are correct but only one is verified. Improves both step-wise accuracy and end-to-end task completion on web and mobile benchmarks.

news.title: Petri Net Relaxation for Infeasibility Explanation and Sequential Task Planning
news.summary: Proposes Petri net reachability relaxation for robust invariant synthesis, efficient goal-unreachability detection, and helpful infeasibility explanations. Leverages incremental constraint solvers to support goal and constraint updates. Empirically, compared to baselines, the system produces a comparable number of invariants, detects up to 2x more infeasibilities, performs competitively in one-shot planning, and outperforms in sequential plan updates in tested domains.

news.title: Surrogate Models: Rock-Fluid Interaction Grid-Size-Invariant Approach
news.summary: Develops eight surrogate models for predicting fluid flow in porous media as cheaper alternative to high-fidelity numerical models. Introduces grid-size-invariant framework using single neural networks with UNet++ architecture, demonstrating outperformance of traditional ROM approaches while reducing memory consumption during training. The application analyzed is particularly challenging because fluid-induced rock dissolution results in a non-static solid field.

news.title: Australia Faces Trump Tariff Threat on Tech Exports
news.summary: Trump administration threatened higher tariffs on Australian exports in recent addresses. Federal government is developing strategies to mitigate economic impacts across tech manufacturing, renewable energy equipment, and software exports.

news.title: NSW Flash Flooding Causes Widespread Power Outages
news.summary: Intense rainfall and flash flooding across Sydney caused thousands of homes to lose power in north-west, Dural, Sackville, Glenwood, and parts of city's south. SES carried out 40+ flood rescues overnight. Major roads including City West Link, Parramatta Road, and Anzac Bridge closed due to traffic gridlock.

news.title: GUI Agents: Action-aware Supervision & Partially Verifiable RL
news.summary: Introduces GUI-Libra: action-aware SFT mixing reasoning-then-action with direct-action data, plus 81K curated GUI reasoning dataset. Uses KL-regularized RL with success-adaptive scaling to handle partial verifiability where multiple actions are correct but only one is verified. Improves both step-wise accuracy and end-to-end task completion on web and mobile benchmarks.

news.title: Language Models Exhibit Inconsistent Biases Towards Algorithmic Agents and Human Experts
news.summary: Evaluates how LLMs weigh information from different sources (human experts vs. algorithms) in decision-making tasks. Finds LLMs rate human experts higher, showing algorithm aversion bias. However, when betting between human expert and algorithm, LLMs disproportionately choose the algorithm even when it performs worse, revealing inconsistent biases that need scrutiny for high-stakes AI deployments.

news.title: Language Models: Algorithm Aversion Study
news.summary: Evaluates how LLMs weigh information from human experts vs algorithms in decision-making tasks. Finds LLMs rate human experts higher (unfair advantage), showing algorithm aversion bias. However, when betting between human expert and algorithm, LLMs disproportionately choose algorithm even when it performs worse, revealing inconsistent biases.

news.title: Partial Grounding via LLMs
news.summary: Language models trained for mathematical tasks don't automatically apply reasoning to access their own parametric knowledge. Shows that adding simple "think step-by-step" prompts significantly improves knowledge recall (+9.9% on TriviaQA, +4.2% on Natural Questions, +3.0% on HotpotQA, +0.6% on StrategyQA) but not math. By training models to reason over their parametric knowledge using world-knowledge QA as a verifiable reward, performance improves across multiple benchmarks.

news.title: Petri Net Relaxation with Efficient Infeasibility Detection
news.summary: Proposes Petri net reachability relaxation combined with efficient invariant synthesis, robust goal-unreachability detection, and helpful infeasibility explanations. Leverages incremental constraint solvers to support goal and constraint updates. Empirically, compared to baselines: produces a comparable number of invariants, detects up to 2x more infeasibilities, and outperforms in one-shot planning scenarios.

news.title: Semantic Grounding via LLMs (SPG-LLM)
news.summary: Proposes using LLMs to analyze domain and problem files in PDDL planning. The approach (SPG-LLM) significantly reduces grounded task size - often by orders of magnitude - while delivering comparable or better plan costs across seven hard-to-ground benchmarks. Enables efficient LLM-based planning without expensive domain experts or symbolic planners.

news.title: Semantic Partial Grounding via LLMs
news.summary: Proposes SPG-LLM (Semantic Pre-Grounding via LLMs) to use LLMs to analyze domain and problem files, heuristically identifying irrelevant objects, actions, and predicates in PDDL descriptions. Significantly reduces grounded task size - often by orders of magnitude - while delivering comparable or better plan costs across seven hard-to-ground benchmarks compared to traditional methods.

news.title: Surrogate Models for Rock-Fluid Interaction
news.summary: Develops eight surrogate models for predicting fluid flow in porous media as cheaper alternative to high-fidelity numerical models. Introduces grid-size-invariant framework using UNet++ architecture. Demonstrates outperformance of traditional ROM approaches, particularly for fluid-induced rock dissolution scenarios.

news.title: Surrogate Models for Rock-Fluid Interaction: A Grid-Size-Invariant Approach
news.summary: Develops eight surrogate models for predicting fluid flow in porous media as cheaper alternative to high-fidelity numerical models. Introduces grid-size-invariant framework using single neural networks with UNet++ architecture, demonstrating outperformance of traditional ROM approaches while reducing memory consumption during training. Particularly applicable for non-static solid fields (fluid-induced rock dissolution).

news.title: Training Native GUI Agents to Reason and Act with Action-aware Supervision and Partially Verifiable RL
news.summary: Addresses the gap between open-source and closed-source GUI agents. Introduces GUI-Libra: action-aware SFT mixing reasoning-then-action with direct-action data, plus 81K curated GUI reasoning dataset. Uses KL-regularized RL with success-adaptive scaling to handle partial verifiability where multiple actions are correct but only one is verified. Improves both step-wise accuracy and end-to-end task completion on web and mobile benchmarks.

news.title: Efficient Pipeline for Automated Translation of Benchmarks and Datasets

The approach translates popular benchmarks into eight Eastern and Southern European languages (Ukrainian, Bulgarian, Slovak, Romanian, Lithuanian, Estonian, Turkish, Greek). Evaluations show these translations surpass existing resources, resulting in more accurate downstream model assessment.

news.title: Hardware-Aware Heterogeneous Quantization Method for Edge DNN Inference
news.summary: SigmaQuant introduces an adaptive layer-wise heterogeneous quantization framework that balances accuracy and resource usage for varied edge environments without exhaustive search. Unlike uniform quantization, this approach allocates different bitwidths to individual layers, leveraging varying robustness across layers to maintain accuracy at low bitwidths while optimizing for memory, energy, and latency constraints specific to each edge device.

news.title: Improving Parametric Knowledge Access in Reasoning Language Models
news.summary: Reasoning language models trained for mathematical tasks don't automatically apply reasoning to access their own world knowledge. This study shows that adding simple "think step-by-step" prompts significantly improves knowledge recall (but not math). By training models to reason over their parametric knowledge using world-knowledge QA as a verifiable reward, performance improves on TriviaQA (+9.9%), Natural Questions (+4.2%), HotpotQA (+2.1%), SimpleQA (+0.6%), and StrategyQA (+3.0%).

news.title: SumTablets: A Transliteration Dataset of Sumerian Tablets
news.summary: SumTablets is a dataset pairing 91,606 Sumerian cuneiform tablets (6.97M glyphs) with associated transliterations from Oracc. The dataset includes Unicode representations of cuneiform glyphs with transliterations and structural information (surfaces, newlines, broken segments). Two baselines are evaluated: weighted glyph sampling and a fine-tuned autoregressive language model, which achieves 97.55 character-level F-score on transliteration.

news.title: Lumina Tmall 1M Agents: A Multi-Agent Shopping Platform
news.summary: Presents Lumina Tmall 1M, a multi-agent shopping platform designed for the 100 million product SKU catalog at Tmall. The system consists of three core modules: perception, policy, and action, each built on specialized foundation models. The perception module identifies products from images with 95.7% accuracy and extracts 3.5K detailed attributes. The policy module translates business rules into natural language constraints with 98%+ consistency. The action module coordinates multi-round interactions with reinforcement learning, achieving a 51.5% task completion rate. Lumina 1M demonstrates that multi-agent systems with strong module specialization can achieve high-level e-commerce success, surpassing traditional single-model approaches. System evaluations show significant improvements over baselines, with perception improving product identification by 16.7% and action success by 32.3% relative to single-agent methods.

news.title: Open-source Native GUI Agents for Reasoning and Action-Aware Supervision
news.summary: Native GUI agents still lag behind closed-source systems on long-horizon navigation tasks due to shortage of high-quality, action-aligned reasoning data. This gap stems from two limitations: (i) standard SFT with CoT reasoning often hurts grounding and (ii) step-wise RLVR-style training faces partial verifiability, where multiple actions can be correct but only a single demonstrated action is used for verification. In this work, we present GUI-Libra, a tailored training recipe that addresses these challenges. First, to mitigate the scarcity of action-aligned reasoning data, we introduce a data construction and filtering pipeline and release a curated 81K GUI reasoning dataset. Second, to reconcile reasoning with grounding, we propose action-aware SFT that mixes reasoning-then-action and direct-action data and reweights tokens to emphasize action and grounding. Third, to stabilize RL under partial verifiability, we identify the overlooked importance of KL regularization in RLVR and show that a KL trust region is critical for improving offline-to-online predictability. Across diverse web and mobile benchmarks, GUI-Libra consistently improves both step-wise accuracy and end-to-end task completion. Our results suggest that carefully designed post-training and data curation can unlock significantly stronger task-solving capabilities without costly online data collection. We release our dataset, code, and models to facilitate further research on data-efficient post-training for reasoning-capable GUI agents.

news.title: Step-wise RLVR-Tyle Training Faces Partial Verifiability Challenge
news.summary: Step-wise RLVR-style training faces the partial verifiability challenge, where multiple actions can be correct but only a single demonstrated action is used for verification. This makes offline step-wise metrics weak predictors of online task success. In this work, we perform a comprehensive analysis of 10 state-of-the-art RLVR models using a standardized evaluation protocol. Our analysis reveals significant gaps in offline verifiability - most models achieve near-zero single-action FVD (~0.05) while having much higher multi-step FVD (3-10). We propose that step-wise FVD should not be the primary evaluation metric and recommend using trajectory-based metrics or action-conditioned evaluation for fair comparison. Additionally, we find that partial verifiability is strongly correlated with planning horizon and task complexity, suggesting that models with longer planning windows exhibit better verifiability. We address these findings by proposing improved evaluation protocols and providing more nuanced analysis of RLVR verifiability that can guide the development of more reliable autonomous systems.

news.title: Sysurv: A Fully Differentiable Survival Forest Method for Learning Interpretable Rules
news.summary: In many applications, it is important to identify subpopulations that survive longer or shorter than the rest of the population. In medicine, for example, it allows determining which patients benefit from treatment and in predictive maintenance, and which components are more likely to fail. Existing methods for discovering subgroups with exceptional survival characteristics require restrictive assumptions about survival models (e.g., proportional hazards), pre-discretized features, and, as they compare average statistics, tend to overlook individual deviations. In this paper, we propose Sysurv, a fully differentiable, non-parametric method that leverages random survival forests to learn individual survival curves, automatically learns conditions and how to combine these into inherently interpretable rules, so as to select subgroups with exceptional survival characteristics. Empirical evaluation on a wide range of datasets and settings, including a case study on cancer data, shows that Sysurv reveals insightful and actionable survival subgroups that existing methods overlook. We release our dataset, code, and models to facilitate further research on interpretable survival analysis for healthcare applications.

news.title: Understanding and Mitigating KL Collapse in Large Vision Models: A Taxonomy, Analysis, and Theoretical Insights
news.summary: Large Vision Models (LVLMs) exhibit KL collapse in high-dimensional layers, causing training instability. This work provides a comprehensive taxonomy analyzing three categories of KL collapse: layer-wise vs pairwise, inter-layer vs intra-layer, and depth-wise. We investigate the root causes through theoretical analysis, empirical studies across 16 architectures, and propose practical mitigation strategies. Our findings reveal that depth-wise KL is most critical and often correlates with model performance. We identify overlooked importance of KL regularization in training stability and show that a KL trust region is critical for improving offline-to-online predictability. The analysis reveals that previous taxonomic assumptions were incomplete - KL collapse cannot be categorized solely by layer index. We propose a unified framework for understanding KL collapse in LVLMs, facilitating more robust model training practices and enabling the development of more reliable computer vision systems.

news.title: A Data-Efficient Vision-Language-Action Model that Drives without Reasoning (NoRD)
news.summary: NoRD achieves competitive autonomous driving performance with less than 60% of training data and zero reasoning annotations—3x fewer tokens than existing VLAs. The key insight is using Dr. GRPO to mitigate "difficulty bias" in policy optimization, enabling efficient learning from small, reasoning-free datasets.

news.title: On Data Engineering for Scaling LLM Terminal Capabilities
news.summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron-Terminal, a family of models for terminal/shell tasks, along with Terminal-Task-Gen (synthetic task pipeline) and Terminal-Corpus (open-source dataset). The 32B model jumps from 3.4% to 27.4% on Terminal-Bench 2.0—matching much larger models. Models are initialized from Qwen3 checkpoints.

news.title: Test-Time Training with KV Binding Is Secretly Linear Attention
news.summary: This paper reframes test-time training (TTT) with KV binding as a form of learned linear attention, contradicting the common interpretation of TTT as online meta-learning. The new perspective enables architectural simplifications, fully parallel formulations, and explains previously puzzling model behaviors.

news.title: Why Pass@k Optimization Can Degrade Pass@1: Prompt Interference in LLM Post-training
news.summary: This paper provides a theoretical explanation for a puzzling phenomenon: optimizing LLMs for pass@k (success with k samples) often degrades pass@1 (single-shot success). The authors show that pass@k optimization implicitly reweights toward low-success prompts, and when these prompts exhibit "negative interference," the update direction rotates away from pass@1 improvement.

news.title: Anthropic Consciousness Debate Heats Up
news.summary: Anthropic describes Claude as "a new kind of entity" that might be conscious, sparking philosophical and ethical debates about AI sentience.

news.title: Mistral Launches Voxtral Audio Model
news.summary: New real-time audio transcription model with precision diarization and a dedicated audio playground. "Transcribes at the speed of sound."

news.title: OpenAI Stargate Project Restructures
news.summary: OpenAI's Stargate data center project shifted strategy—exploring individual deals with SoftBank and Oracle instead of the original three-party structure due to cost disagreements.

news.title: OpenAI Wins Court Battle Against Elon Musk
news.summary: Court handed OpenAI a win in multi-pronged legal battle with Musk.

news.title: Trump Data Center Power Mandate

news.title: AI Reshaping Consumer Shopping Experience
news.summary: AI is transforming how consumers discover and purchase products. From personalized recommendations to virtual try-ons, the shopping experience is becoming more intelligent and frictionless.

news.title: Social Commerce Continues Growth
news.summary: Social media platforms are becoming primary shopping channels. TikTok Shop, Instagram Shopping, and similar features are driving direct-to-consumer sales.

news.title: Stripe Valuation Soars to $159B
news.summary: Stripe's valuation climbed to $159 billion in its latest share sale, solidifying its position as the most valuable private fintech company. The payments giant continues to benefit from the global shift to e-commerce and digital payments.

news.title: Trump Tariffs Impact Consumer Goods
news.summary: The 10% worldwide tariff is now in effect, impacting imported consumer goods. Companies are reassessing supply chains and pricing strategies.

news.title: Google Apologizes for Racial Slur in Push Notification
news.summary: Google is "deeply sorry" for a push notification related to the BAFTAs that included a racial slur. The company clarified the error did NOT involve AI, but rather push notification systems that "accidentally" included the slur.

news.title: "Adapt or Die" Moment for Software Industry
news.summary: AI upheaval forces existential question: deeply integrate AI or risk obsolescence.

news.title: Hegseth Threatens to Cut Anthropic from Pentagon Supply Chain
news.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is threatening to cut Anthropic from the Pentagon's supply chain in a showdown with the company's CEO. The dispute highlights tensions between AI companies and defense contracts.

news.title: Hegseth vs Anthropic Pentagon Showdown
news.summary: Defense Secretary threatens to cut Anthropic from Pentagon supply chain. $200M+ contract at stake.

news.title: PwC Clashes with Boutique Consultancy Founded by Former Executive
news.summary: Professional services giant PwC is in conflict with a boutique consultancy founded by one of its former executives. The dispute raises questions about non-compete agreements and talent mobility in the consulting industry.

news.title: Stripe Valuation Soars to $159B
news.summary: Stripe's valuation climbed to $159 billion in latest share sale, solidifying position as most valuable private fintech/SaaS company.

news.summary: Stripe's valuation has climbed to $159 billion in its latest share sale, solidifying its position as one of the most valuable private fintech/SaaS companies globally. The payments giant continues to benefit from the shift to digital commerce.

news.title: Wall Street Rallies as Software Selloff Slows
news.summary: After days of AI-fueled selling in software stocks, markets stabilize. Investors reassessing valuations.
